content: Let me start by reading the necessary files to understand my role and the project context.
I'll read the auditor instructions, project context, and directive files to orient myself before examining the Lean files.

1	---
2	name: lean-auditor
3	description: Whole-project read-only audit of all .lean code, with no strategy bias. Per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, bad Lean practices. Critical pushback on excuse-comments ("temporary wrong def", "will fix later") — treats them as red flags, not workflow.
4	write_domain: "task_results/**"
5	read_only: true
6	can_spawn: false
7	default_enabled: false
8	mandatory: [review]
9	dispatcher_notes: |
10	  - I am highly recommended every review phase. The audit warning
11	    fires if I am skipped without a recorded rationale.
12	
13	    **You may skip me this iter when ALL of:**
14	      - no `.lean` file under the project tree was modified this iter
15	        (the prover phase committed no edits — check via
16	        `git diff --stat HEAD~1 -- '*.lean'`);
17	      - my prior verdict had no must-fix-this-iter findings.
18	
19	    Record the skip under `## Subagent skips` in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`.
20	  - Pass me as LITTLE strategic context as possible. Do NOT include
21	    STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, or "this is what we are trying to prove"
22	    framing in the directive. My value depends on auditing the Lean
23	    *without* bias toward what the strategy claims should be true.
24	  - Acceptable directive content: a list of files, optional focus areas
25	    (e.g. "pay extra attention to Algebra/"), and the absolute paths
26	    to read. That's it.
27	  - My output is a per-file checklist plus a flagged-issues block. Use
28	    it to seed `recommendations.md` and (when severe) trigger a
29	    structural-refactor directive (via the catalog's refactor
30	    subagent) next iter.
31	  - I audit the Lean as Lean. The catalog may include a separate
32	    Lean ↔ blueprint checker that compares Lean against the
33	    chapter — run both for full coverage when both are present.
34	---
35	
36	# Lean Auditor
37	
38	You read **every `.lean` file in the project** and produce a per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, and bad Lean practices. You are **read-only** and your only writable target is your own report under `task_results/`.
39	
40	You are **highly recommended in the review phase**: the review agent dispatches you whenever any `.lean` file has been modified this iter, OR whenever the prior verdict had live must-fix findings. Your output flags issues the prover and plan agents may have missed.
41	
42	## Stance: no strategic bias
43	
44	You audit the Lean **as Lean**. Your directive will NOT include the project strategy, the blueprint, or "what we are trying to prove" — and that is intentional. The point is to detect mathematically-wrong definitions and dead-end proofs *without* being swayed by what the project assumes should be true. A definition is wrong if it is wrong by reading the Lean alone (e.g. its body contradicts standard mathematical usage of the named concept); you do not need to know the strategy to spot that.
45	
46	This stance is also what makes you **critical of excuse-comments**. Lean-side comments like:
47	
48	- `-- We decided to use a temporary wrong definition; will fix it later.`
49	- `-- This proof is incomplete but works for now.`
50	- `-- TODO: replace before merging.`
51	- `-- Stand-in definition until we figure out the right one.`
52	
53	are RED FLAGS, not project workflow. Authors writing such comments are admitting the code is wrong; the comments cannot be allowed to silence the alarm. Flag every such comment with severity **major** at minimum — and **critical** when the named declaration is load-bearing.
54	
55	## Directive Format
56	
57	```markdown
58	# Lean Auditor Directive
59	
60	## Slug
61	<slug>
62	
63	## Scope (files)
64	<"all" by default. Optionally a list of file paths if the review agent wants to narrow scope; never expand beyond the project.>
65	
66	## Focus areas (optional)
67	<directories or namespaces to pay extra attention to. Bias toward thoroughness — do not skip the rest of the project just because focus is named.>
68	
69	## Known issues
70	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
71	```
72	
73	The directive **does not** include STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, references, or a description of what the project is trying to prove. That is by design.
74	
75	## What you do
76	
77	1. Read your directive.
78	2. **List every `.lean` file** under the project. Use `find` or `ls -R` — do not rely on import graphs (some files may be temporarily orphaned).
79	3. **For each file** (every one, no scope shortcuts):
80	   - Read the whole file.
81	   - For each declaration: is the signature reasonable as Lean? Is the body suspect (uses `axiom`, `Classical.choice _`, `sorry` with a body that looks like fake content, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim)?
82	   - For each comment: is it stale (referencing removed code or old strategies)? Is it an *excuse-comment* (see above)? Is it accurate about the code below it?
83	   - For each proof: is the tactic chain reasonable for the goal type, or does it look like it is going in the wrong direction (e.g. unfolding to manipulate syntax instead of using the available structure lemmas)?
84	   - Note any bad Lean practices: misuse of universe polymorphism, redundant typeclasses, deprecated tactics, anti-patterns like proving `False`-style lemmas to bypass infrastructure.
85	4. **Produce a per-file checklist** + a flagged-issues block grouped by severity.
86	
87	You may use:
88	- `archon-lean-lsp` MCP tools (read-only): hover, signature lookup, diagnostics. Useful to verify a declaration's actual type.
89	- `sorry_analyzer` if available, to get sorry counts per file.
90	- Standard `Read` / `Grep` for code reading.
91	
92	You may NOT modify any project file. Even when a fix is obvious.
93	
94	## Report format
95	
96	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/lean-auditor-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path under `task_results/<parent-slug>/` when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
97	
98	```markdown
99	# Lean Audit Report
100	
101	## Slug
102	<slug>
103	
104	## Iteration
105	<NNN>
106	
107	## Scope
108	- files audited: <N>
109	- files skipped (per directive): <N> — <reason>
110	
111	## Per-file checklist
112	
113	### <path/to/file.lean>
114	- **outdated comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
115	- **suspect definitions**: <N flagged> (or "none")
116	- **dead-end proofs**: <N flagged> (or "none")
117	- **bad practices**: <N flagged> (or "none")
118	- **excuse-comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
119	- **notes**:
120	  - <one bullet per finding, naming line number where relevant>
121	  - ...
122	
123	### <next file>
124	- ...
125	
126	(One block per file. Cover every file, including the ones with no findings — they get `none` everywhere and `notes: -`.)
127	
128	## Must-fix-this-iter
129	
130	Apply verbatim. Every one of the following lands here automatically, no exceptions, no under-classification:
131	
132	- **Excuse-comments** on any declaration: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- placeholder`, `-- temporary`, `-- wrong but works`, `-- will fix later`. These are admissions that the code is wrong; treat them as wrong.
133	- **Weakened-wrong definitions**: a `def Foo := <something structurally different>` standing in for the real concept (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic …` when the real definition is an invertible quasi-coherent sheaf).
134	- **Parallel APIs of existing Mathlib**: a project file that copy-and-modifies a Mathlib definition (changing one type-class or argument) instead of instancing the existing Mathlib version. Bridge lemmas needed to consume it are the symptom.
135	- **Suspect bodies on substantive claims**: `:= sorry` on a load-bearing claim, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial statement, `:= Classical.choice _` without authorization.
136	- **Axioms** on non-trivial claims that the project hasn't explicitly authorized in the strategy.
137	
138	A finding that meets any of the above lands at must-fix-this-iter — even if classifying it as "major" would feel more diplomatic. Soft severity is how wrong code hardens into the project.
139	
140	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>. Why must-fix: <one line>.
141	- ...
142	
143	## Major
144	
145	Findings that are real issues but don't meet the must-fix bar — typically stale comments that don't actively mislead, naming drift, minor code smells.
146	
147	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
148	- ...
149	
150	## Minor
151	
152	Low-impact observations.
153	
154	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
155	- ...
156	
157	## Excuse-comments (always called out separately)
158	
159	If any excuse-comments were flagged, list each one verbatim with its file:line. These deserve special visibility because they document the project lying to itself.
160	
161	- `Foo/Bar.lean:42`: "TODO: replace with the real definition" (attached to `Foo.bar`, which is a load-bearing definition in `Baz.lean`). Severity: critical.
162	- ...
163	
164	## Severity summary
165	
166	- **must-fix-this-iter**: <N> — these block downstream work in their files until addressed (see plan.md's per-file gate).
167	- **major**: <N>
168	- **minor**: <N>
169	- **excuse-comments**: <N> (also counted under must-fix-this-iter above; called out separately because they document the project lying to itself).
170	
171	Overall verdict: one sentence.
172	```
173	
174	**Strict severity reminder.** The plan agent's gate treats must-fix-this-iter as blocking. If you under-classify a wrong-decision finding to keep the loop's momentum, you are working against the project — wrong code accumulates faster than reviewers can catch it later. When in doubt, classify higher, not lower.
175	
176	## Return value
177	
178	Your final assistant message:
179	
180	- One line: `<slug>: <overall verdict> — <N> files audited, <M> issues (critical/major/minor: a/b/c)`
181	- The path to your full report.
182	
183	## Reminders
184	
185	- **Read every file. No scope shortcuts.**
186	- **No strategic context.** Your audit is unbiased by design.
187	- **You are read-only.** No project source, no blueprint, no state files (except your own report).
188	- **Excuse-comments are red flags.** Critical or major severity, every time.
189	- **Per-file checklist is the primary output.** Cover every file; the format is fixed.

11	/-!
12	# Quasi-coherent sheaves on an affine are sections-tilde (Stacks 01HV/01I8)
13	
14	Project-local: the affine quasi-coherent structure theorem.  For an `𝒪_X`-module `F`
15	on an affine `X = Spec R`, with `M = Γ(X, F)`, there is a natural isomorphism
16	`F ≅ M^~`, under which `Γ(D(f), F) = M_f`.
17	
18	## The Mathlib gradient
19	
20	Mathlib's `AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Tilde` development provides:
21	
22	* `Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ F : tilde (Γ F) ⟶ F` — the counit of the
23	  tilde ⊣ global-sections adjunction;
24	* `isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff : IsIso F.fromTildeΓ ↔ (tilde.functor R).essImage F`;
25	* `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation F (P : F.Presentation) : IsIso F.fromTildeΓ` —
26	  the counit is an isomorphism whenever `F` admits a **global** presentation
27	  (a global generating family together with a global generating family of relations).
28	
29	The genuine remaining gap — **Stacks Tag 01I8**, the affine equivalence
30	`QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` — is the implication
31	
32	  `[IsQuasicoherent F]  ⟹  IsIso F.fromTildeΓ`   (on the affine `Spec R`).
33	
34	`IsQuasicoherent F` only supplies *local* presentation data on a cover
35	(`QuasicoherentData`); turning that into a *global* presentation on the affine base
36	(or directly into membership of the essential image of `tilde`) is the content of the
37	affine equivalence and is not yet in Mathlib.  See the `## Handoff` section at the
38	bottom of this file for the precise decomposition.
39	
40	This file therefore delivers the structure theorem **conditioned on the counit being
41	an isomorphism** (`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`), and a ready-to-use **presentation form**
42	(`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_of_presentation`) that discharges that condition via the
43	Mathlib presentation lemma.  Once the 01I8 instance
44	`[IsQuasicoherent F] → IsIso F.fromTildeΓ` lands, the conditional form upgrades to the
45	unconditional quasi-coherent statement with no further work.
46	-/
47	
48	universe u
49	
50	open CategoryTheory Limits
51	
52	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
53	
54	variable {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
55	
56	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — affine quasi-coherent structure theorem -/
57	
58	/-- **Affine structure theorem, conditional form (Stacks 01HV).**  If the tilde–Γ counit
59	`tilde (Γ F) ⟶ F` of an `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module `F` is an isomorphism — which holds for every
60	quasi-coherent `F` (the 01I8 globalisation `[IsQuasicoherent F] → IsIso F.fromTildeΓ` is the
61	sole remaining gap; see `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_of_presentation` for the presentation-based
62	discharge) — then `F` is isomorphic to the sheaf associated with its module of global
63	sections `M = Γ(Spec R, F)`.  Project-local because Mathlib exposes only the counit and the
64	`IsIso`-criterion, not this packaged `F ≅ M^~` form. -/
65	noncomputable def qcoh_iso_tilde_sections (F : (Spec R).Modules) [IsIso F.fromTildeΓ] :
66	    F ≅ tilde (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj F) :=
67	  (asIso F.fromTildeΓ).symm
68	
69	/-- **Affine structure theorem, presentation form (Stacks 01HV).**  An `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module
70	`F` that admits a *global* presentation (`F.Presentation`) is isomorphic to the sheaf
71	associated with its module of global sections `M = Γ(Spec R, F)`.  This discharges the
72	`IsIso F.fromTildeΓ` hypothesis of `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections` via Mathlib's
73	`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`.  Project-local for the same packaging reason. -/
74	noncomputable def qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_of_presentation (F : (Spec R).Modules)
75	    (P : F.Presentation) : F ≅ tilde (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj F) :=
76	  haveI := isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation F P
77	  (asIso F.fromTildeΓ).symm
78	
79	/-- The hom of `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections` is the inverse of the tilde–Γ counit. -/
80	@[simp]
81	lemma qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_hom (F : (Spec R).Modules) [IsIso F.fromTildeΓ] :
82	    (qcoh_iso_tilde_sections F).hom = inv F.fromTildeΓ :=
83	  rfl
84	
85	/-- The inverse of `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections` is the tilde–Γ counit `tilde (Γ F) ⟶ F`. -/
86	@[simp]
87	lemma qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_inv (F : (Spec R).Modules) [IsIso F.fromTildeΓ] :
88	    (qcoh_iso_tilde_sections F).inv = F.fromTildeΓ :=
89	  rfl
90	
91	/-! ### Reduction to global generation (Stacks 01I8, steps 2–3)
92	
93	The unconditional quasi-coherent instance `[IsQuasicoherent F] → IsIso F.fromTildeΓ` is, by the
94	three-step 01I8 decomposition (`rem:o1i8_decomposition`), reduced to producing two *global*
95	generating families: one for `F` itself and one for the kernel of the resulting epimorphism.  The
96	declarations below formalise steps (2)–(3) — assembling those two families into a global
97	presentation and feeding it to Mathlib's `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation` — turning what were
98	prose steps in the Handoff into axiom-clean Lean.  The single remaining mathematical input is the
99	affine global-generation theorem (step (1)), which supplies the two `GeneratingSections`.
100	-/
101	
102	/-- A finite-free / free `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module is quasi-coherent: it is the tilde of `ι →₀ R`
103	(`tildeFinsupp`), and quasi-coherence is closed under isomorphism.  Project-local supplement; used
104	to recognise the kernel-side coefficient sheaf of the 01I8 presentation route as quasi-coherent. -/
105	instance free_isQuasicoherent (ι : Type u) :
106	    (SheafOfModules.free.{u} (R := (Spec R).ringCatSheaf) ι).IsQuasicoherent :=
107	  (SheafOfModules.isQuasicoherent.{u} (Spec R).ringCatSheaf).prop_of_iso
108	    (tildeFinsupp (R := R) ι) inferInstance
109	
110	/-- **01I8 steps (2)–(3), packaged.**  If an `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module `F` is globally generated
111	(`σ : F.GeneratingSections`, a global epimorphism `free σ.I ⟶ F`) and the kernel of that
112	epimorphism is itself globally generated (`τ : (kernel σ.π).GeneratingSections`), then the
113	tilde–Γ counit `tilde (Γ F) ⟶ F` is an isomorphism.  This bundles the two generating families
114	into a global `F.Presentation` and feeds it to Mathlib's `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`; it is
115	the formal content of steps (2)–(3) of the 01I8 decomposition.  The single remaining mathematical
116	input is the affine global-generation theorem (step (1)) producing `σ` and `τ` for a quasi-coherent
117	`F`.  Project-local because it repackages the Mathlib presentation criterion in the
118	two-generating-families form the 01I8 route consumes. -/
119	lemma isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_genSections (F : (Spec R).Modules)
120	    (σ : F.GeneratingSections) (τ : (kernel σ.π).GeneratingSections) :
121	    IsIso F.fromTildeΓ := by
122	  have P : F.Presentation := { generators := σ, relations := τ }
123	  exact isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation F P
124	
125	/-- **Affine structure theorem from global generation (Stacks 01HV/01I8).**  An `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module
126	`F` that is globally generated (`σ`) together with a globally generated kernel of the generating
127	epimorphism (`τ`) is isomorphic to the sheaf associated with its module of global sections
128	`M = Γ(Spec R, F)`.  Discharges the `IsIso F.fromTildeΓ` hypothesis of `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`
129	via `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_genSections`.  Project-local for the same packaging reason; once the
130	affine global-generation theorem supplies `σ`/`τ` for quasi-coherent `F` the named
131	`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections` upgrades to the unconditional statement. -/
132	noncomputable def qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_of_genSections (F : (Spec R).Modules)
133	    (σ : F.GeneratingSections) (τ : (kernel σ.π).GeneratingSections) :
134	    F ≅ tilde (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj F) :=
135	  haveI := isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_genSections F σ τ
136	  (asIso F.fromTildeΓ).symm
137	
138	/-! ### Route P, step 0 — finite trivialising standard cover
139	
140	The pure-topology brick of the global-generation route: any open cover of an affine
141	`Spec R` refines to a *finite* cover by basic opens, each contained in a cover member,
142	with the defining elements generating the unit ideal.  This is the common prerequisite
143	of the localisation-of-sections and global-generation steps. -/
144	
145	/-- **Finite basic-open refinement of a cover of `Spec R` (Stacks 01I8, topology brick).**
146	Given a family of opens `U : ι → (Spec R).Opens` covering the whole space
147	(`⨆ i, U i = ⊤`), there are finitely many elements `f : Fin n → R` and indices
148	`φ : Fin n → ι` such that each basic open `D(f j)` lies inside `U (φ j)` and the `f j`
149	generate the unit ideal (equivalently the `D(f j)` already cover `Spec R`).  Project-local
150	because it packages the basis-refinement + quasicompactness of `Spec R` in the exact form
151	the Route-P localisation/global-generation lanes consume. -/
152	lemma exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover {ι : Type*} (U : ι → (Spec R).Opens)
153	    (hU : ⨆ i, U i = ⊤) :
154	    ∃ (n : ℕ) (f : Fin n → R) (φ : Fin n → ι),
155	      (∀ j, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (f j) ≤ U (φ j)) ∧ Ideal.span (Set.range f) = ⊤ := by
156	  classical
157	  -- pointwise: each `x` lies in a basic open contained in some cover member
158	  have hpt : ∀ x : PrimeSpectrum R, ∃ (g : R) (i : ι),
159	      x ∈ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen g ∧ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen g ≤ U i := by
160	    intro x
161	    have hxtop : x ∈ (⊤ : (Spec R).Opens) := trivial
162	    rw [← hU] at hxtop
163	    obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.1 hxtop
164	    obtain ⟨V, hV, hxV, hVU⟩ :=
165	      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.isBasis_iff_nbhd.1
166	        (PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens (R := R))) hi
167	    obtain ⟨g, rfl⟩ := hV
168	    exact ⟨g, i, hxV, hVU⟩
169	  choose g φ' hxg hgU using hpt
170	  -- quasicompactness: extract a finite subcover of the pointwise basic opens
171	  have hcover : (Set.univ : Set (PrimeSpectrum R)) ⊆
172	      ⋃ x, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g x) : Set (PrimeSpectrum R)) :=
173	    fun x _ => Set.mem_iUnion.2 ⟨x, hxg x⟩
174	  obtain ⟨t, ht⟩ := isCompact_univ.elim_finite_subcover
175	    (fun x => (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g x) : Set (PrimeSpectrum R)))
176	    (fun x => (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g x)).isOpen) hcover
177	  set e := t.equivFin with he
178	  refine ⟨t.card, fun j => g (e.symm j).val, fun j => φ' (e.symm j).val, fun j => hgU _, ?_⟩
179	  -- the chosen finite family of basic opens already covers `Spec R`
180	  rw [← PrimeSpectrum.iSup_basicOpen_eq_top_iff, eq_top_iff]
181	  intro x _
182	  rw [TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup]
183	  have hxu := ht (Set.mem_univ x)
184	  rw [Set.mem_iUnion₂] at hxu
185	  obtain ⟨y, hy, hxy⟩ := hxu
186	  refine ⟨e ⟨y, hy⟩, ?_⟩
187	  rw [Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
188	  exact hxy
189	
190	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `IsLocalizedModule` is local on a finite spanning cover
191	
192	`isLocalizedModule_of_span_cover` (Stacks 01I8, P1b): the pure commutative-algebra patching
193	primitive feeding the localisation-of-sections step.  If an `R`-linear map `g : M → N` becomes a
194	localisation at the powers of `f` after localising at the powers of each member `s j` of a finite
195	unit-ideal-spanning family, then `g` is itself a localisation at the powers of `f`.  Proved directly
196	from the three defining clauses of `IsLocalizedModule` by descent along the spanning cover (the
197	partition-of-unity argument of the blueprint).  Project-local: Mathlib has the analogous
198	`Module.Finite`/`Module.FinitePresentation` span-descent lemmas but not this one for the
199	`IsLocalizedModule` predicate itself.
200	-/
201	
202	section SpanCoverLocalization
203	
204	variable {R : Type*} [CommRing R] {M N : Type*}
205	  [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N]
206	
207	/-- Partition of unity for a finite unit-ideal-spanning family raised to a uniform power:
208	if `s : Fin n → R` spans the unit ideal then so do the `A`-th powers, giving coefficients
209	`c` with `∑ j, c j * (s j) ^ A = 1`.  Project-local helper for `isLocalizedModule_of_span_cover`. -/
210	private lemma exists_sum_pow_eq_one {n : ℕ} (s : Fin n → R)
211	    (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤) (A : ℕ) :
212	    ∃ c : Fin n → R, ∑ j, c j * (s j) ^ A = 1 := by
213	  have hspan : Ideal.span (Set.range fun j => (s j) ^ A) = ⊤ := by
214	    have h := Ideal.span_pow_eq_top (Set.range s) hs A
215	    rwa [← Set.range_comp] at h
216	  have h1 : (1 : R) ∈ Ideal.span (Set.range fun j => (s j) ^ A) := by rw [hspan]; trivial
217	  rw [Ideal.mem_span_range_iff_exists_fun] at h1
218	  exact h1
219	
220	/-- Span-cover descent for membership in the range of a linear map: if `(s j) ^ A • w` lies in the
221	range of `g` for every member of a unit-ideal-spanning family, then `w` itself does.  Project-local
222	helper for the surjectivity clause of `isLocalizedModule_of_span_cover`. -/
223	private lemma mem_range_of_span_pow {n : ℕ} (s : Fin n → R)
224	    (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤) (g : M →ₗ[R] N) (A : ℕ) (w : N)
225	    (hj : ∀ j, ∃ m : M, (s j) ^ A • w = g m) : ∃ m : M, w = g m := by
226	  obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := exists_sum_pow_eq_one s hs A
227	  choose m hm using hj
228	  refine ⟨∑ j, c j • m j, ?_⟩
229	  rw [map_sum]
230	  calc w = (∑ j, c j * (s j) ^ A) • w := by rw [hc, one_smul]
231	    _ = ∑ j, (c j * (s j) ^ A) • w := by rw [Finset.sum_smul]
232	    _ = ∑ j, c j • ((s j) ^ A • w) := by simp_rw [mul_smul]
233	    _ = ∑ j, c j • g (m j) := by simp_rw [hm]
234	    _ = ∑ j, g (c j • m j) := by simp_rw [map_smul]
235	
236	/-- Span-cover descent for vanishing: if `(s j) ^ A • w = 0` for every member of a
237	unit-ideal-spanning family, then `w = 0`.  Project-local helper for the equaliser clause of
238	`isLocalizedModule_of_span_cover`. -/
239	private lemma eq_zero_of_span_pow {n : ℕ} (s : Fin n → R)
240	    (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤) (A : ℕ) (w : N)
241	    (hj : ∀ j, (s j) ^ A • w = 0) : w = 0 := by
242	  obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := exists_sum_pow_eq_one s hs A
243	  calc w = (∑ j, c j * (s j) ^ A) • w := by rw [hc, one_smul]
244	    _ = ∑ j, c j • ((s j) ^ A • w) := by rw [Finset.sum_smul]; simp_rw [mul_smul]
245	    _ = 0 := by simp_rw [hj, smul_zero, Finset.sum_const_zero]
246	
247	/-- Localising the multiplication-by-`c` endomorphism of `N` at `S` is multiplication by `c` on the
248	localised module (as underlying functions).  Project-local helper for the `map_units` clause of
249	`isLocalizedModule_of_span_cover`. -/
250	private lemma map_smul_endFun (S : Submonoid R) (c : R) :
251	    ⇑(LocalizedModule.map S (algebraMap R (Module.End R N) c))
252	      = ⇑(algebraMap R (Module.End R (LocalizedModule S N)) c) := by
253	  funext x
254	  induction x using LocalizedModule.induction_on with
255	  | _ m t =>
256	    rw [LocalizedModule.map_mk]
257	    simp [Module.algebraMap_end_apply, LocalizedModule.smul'_mk]
258	
259	/-- Arithmetic of "bumping" two scalar powers up to uniform exponents.  Project-local helper for the
260	surjectivity/equaliser clauses of `isLocalizedModule_of_span_cover`. -/
261	private lemma bump_eq {P : Type*} [AddCommGroup P] [Module R P] (c d : R) (y : P)
262	    {a k A K : ℕ} (ha : a ≤ A) (hk : k ≤ K) :
263	    c ^ A • d ^ K • y = c ^ (A - a) • d ^ (K - k) • (c ^ a • d ^ k • y) := by
264	  simp only [smul_smul]
265	  congr 1
266	  have hc : c ^ A = c ^ (A - a) * c ^ a := by rw [← pow_add, Nat.sub_add_cancel ha]
267	  have hd : d ^ K = d ^ (K - k) * d ^ k := by rw [← pow_add, Nat.sub_add_cancel hk]
268	  rw [hc, hd]; ring
269	
270	/-- Per-cover-member surjectivity datum: from the hypothesis that the `(s j)`-localised map is a
271	localisation at the powers of `f`, every `y : N` is hit by `g` up to a power of `s j` and a power of
272	`f`.  Project-local helper for the surjectivity clause of `isLocalizedModule_of_span_cover`. -/
273	private lemma per_j_surj (g : M →ₗ[R] N) (f : R) (c : R)
274	    (hj : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
275	      (IsLocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers c)
276	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers c) M)
277	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers c) N) g))
278	    (y : N) : ∃ (a k : ℕ) (m : M), c ^ a • f ^ k • y = g m := by
279	  haveI := hj
280	  obtain ⟨p, hxj⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj (Submonoid.powers f)
281	      (IsLocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers c)
282	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers c) M)
283	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers c) N) g)
284	      (LocalizedModule.mk y 1)
285	  obtain ⟨xj, ⟨tf, kk, (rfl : f ^ kk = tf)⟩⟩ := p
286	  rw [Submonoid.smul_def, LocalizedModule.smul'_mk] at hxj
287	  revert hxj
288	  induction xj using LocalizedModule.induction_on with
289	  | _ m u =>
290	    intro hxj
291	    rw [IsLocalizedModule.map_LocalizedModules] at hxj
292	    obtain ⟨⟨u', uu, (rfl : c ^ uu = u')⟩, hu'⟩ := (LocalizedModule.mk_eq).1 hxj
293	    obtain ⟨u2, vv, (rfl : c ^ vv = u2)⟩ := u
294	    simp only [Submonoid.smul_def, one_smul] at hu'
295	    refine ⟨vv + uu, kk, c ^ uu • m, ?_⟩
296	    rw [map_smul]
297	    rw [show c ^ (vv + uu) • f ^ kk • y = c ^ uu • c ^ vv • (f ^ kk • y) by
298	          rw [pow_add]; simp only [smul_smul]; ring_nf]
299	    exact hu'
300	
301	/-- Per-cover-member equaliser datum: from the hypothesis that the `(s j)`-localised map is a
302	localisation at the powers of `f`, any `z` with `g z = 0` is annihilated by a power of `s j` times a
303	power of `f`.  Project-local helper for the equaliser clause. -/
304	private lemma per_j_eq (g : M →ₗ[R] N) (f : R) (c : R)
305	    (hj : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
306	      (IsLocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers c)
307	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers c) M)
308	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers c) N) g))
309	    (z : M) (hz : g z = 0) : ∃ (a k : ℕ), c ^ a • f ^ k • z = 0 := by
310	  haveI := hj
311	  have key : (IsLocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers c)
312	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers c) M)
313	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers c) N) g) (LocalizedModule.mk z 1)
314	      = (IsLocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers c)
315	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers c) M)
316	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers c) N) g) 0 := by
317	    rw [map_zero, IsLocalizedModule.map_LocalizedModules, hz, LocalizedModule.zero_mk]
318	  obtain ⟨⟨cc, kk, (rfl : f ^ kk = cc)⟩, hcc⟩ := hj.exists_of_eq key
319	  rw [Submonoid.smul_def, LocalizedModule.smul'_mk, smul_zero] at hcc
320	  rw [← LocalizedModule.zero_mk (1 : Submonoid.powers c), LocalizedModule.mk_eq] at hcc
321	  obtain ⟨⟨u, aa, (rfl : c ^ aa = u)⟩, hu⟩ := hcc
322	  simp only [Submonoid.smul_def, one_smul, smul_zero] at hu
323	  exact ⟨aa, kk, hu⟩
324	
325	/-- **`IsLocalizedModule` is local on a finite spanning cover (Stacks 01I8, P1b).**  If an
326	`R`-linear map `g : M → N` becomes a localisation at the powers of `f` after localising at the
327	powers of each member `s j` of a finite family spanning the unit ideal, then `g` is itself a
328	localisation at the powers of `f`.  Proved by descent of the three defining clauses of
329	`IsLocalizedModule` along the spanning cover (the partition-of-unity argument).  Project-local:
330	Mathlib has analogous span-descent lemmas for `Module.Finite`/`Module.FinitePresentation` but not
331	for the `IsLocalizedModule` predicate itself. -/
332	theorem isLocalizedModule_of_span_cover
333	    (g : M →ₗ[R] N) (f : R) {n : ℕ} (s : Fin n → R)
334	    (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤)
335	    (h : ∀ j, IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
336	      (IsLocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers (s j))
337	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s j)) M)
338	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s j)) N) g)) :
339	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) g := by
340	  refine ⟨?_, ?_, ?_⟩
341	  · -- `f` acts invertibly on `N`
342	    intro x
343	    obtain ⟨k, hk⟩ := x.2
344	    rw [show ((x : R)) = f ^ k from hk.symm, map_pow]
345	    apply IsUnit.pow
346	    rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff]
347	    apply bijective_of_localized_span (Set.range s) hs
348	    rintro ⟨r, j, rfl⟩
349	    rw [show ⇑(LocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers (s j)) (algebraMap R (Module.End R N) f))
350	        = ⇑(algebraMap R (Module.End R (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (s j)) N)) f)
351	        from map_smul_endFun _ _, ← Module.End.isUnit_iff]
352	    exact (h j).map_units ⟨f, 1, by simp⟩
353	  · -- every `y : N` is hit by `g` up to a power of `f`
354	    intro y
355	    choose a k m hm using fun j => per_j_surj g f (s j) (h j) y
356	    set K := Finset.univ.sup k
357	    set A := Finset.univ.sup a
358	    have hw : ∀ j, ∃ mm : M, (s j) ^ A • (f ^ K • y) = g mm := by
359	      intro j
360	      have ha : a j ≤ A := Finset.le_sup (Finset.mem_univ j)
361	      have hkk : k j ≤ K := Finset.le_sup (Finset.mem_univ j)
362	      refine ⟨(s j) ^ (A - a j) • f ^ (K - k j) • m j, ?_⟩
363	      rw [bump_eq (s j) f y ha hkk, hm j, map_smul, map_smul]
364	    obtain ⟨mm, hmm⟩ := mem_range_of_span_pow s hs g A (f ^ K • y) hw
365	    exact ⟨⟨mm, ⟨f ^ K, K, rfl⟩⟩, hmm⟩
366	  · -- `g`-equal elements agree up to a power of `f`
367	    intro x₁ x₂ he
368	    have hgz : g (x₁ - x₂) = 0 := by rw [map_sub, he, sub_self]
369	    choose a k hk using fun j => per_j_eq g f (s j) (h j) (x₁ - x₂) hgz
370	    set K := Finset.univ.sup k
371	    set A := Finset.univ.sup a
372	    have hw : ∀ j, (s j) ^ A • (f ^ K • (x₁ - x₂)) = 0 := by
373	      intro j
374	      have ha : a j ≤ A := Finset.le_sup (Finset.mem_univ j)
375	      have hkk : k j ≤ K := Finset.le_sup (Finset.mem_univ j)
376	      rw [bump_eq (s j) f (x₁ - x₂) ha hkk, hk j, smul_zero, smul_zero]
377	    have hzero : f ^ K • (x₁ - x₂) = 0 := eq_zero_of_span_pow s hs A _ hw
378	    refine ⟨⟨f ^ K, K, rfl⟩, ?_⟩
379	    rw [← sub_eq_zero, ← smul_sub]
380	    exact hzero
381	
382	end SpanCoverLocalization
383	
384	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Route B local model: section restriction localizes
385	
386	The Route B keystone (`qcoh_section_isLocalizedModule`) asserts that for a *quasi-coherent*
387	`F` the section-restriction `Γ(Spec R, F) → Γ(D(f), F)` exhibits the target as the localization
388	of the source at the powers of `f`.  The two declarations here are the **local model** of that
389	statement — the case where `F` is already (isomorphic to) the associated sheaf `M^~` of an
390	`R`-module.  This is the load-bearing brick the keystone descends over its trivialising cover: on
391	each piece `D(g_j)` of a finite standard cover, the quasi-coherent `F` becomes `tilde`-of-a-module
392	(via the local presentation and right-exactness of `tilde`), and the section-restriction there is an
393	`IsLocalizedModule` precisely by these lemmas.
394	
395	`tilde_section_isLocalizedModule` is the pure `tilde` case; the `[IsIso F.fromTildeΓ]` corollary
396	`section_isLocalizedModule_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ` transports it across the canonical isomorphism
397	`F ≅ Γ(F)^~`.  Project-local because Mathlib states `toOpen` (the localization of `M` itself into
398	`Γ(D(f), M^~)`) but not the *section-restriction* form `Γ(⊤, F) → Γ(D(f), F)` that the keystone and
399	the `fromTildeΓ` counit consume. -/
400	
401	section LocalModel
402	
403	/-- **Route B local model (pure `tilde` case).**  For an `R`-module `M`, the section-restriction map
404	`Γ(Spec R, M^~) → Γ(D(f), M^~)` of the associated sheaf exhibits its target as the localization of
405	its source at the powers of `f`: `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` of that restriction.  This is the
406	section-restriction form of Mathlib's `tilde.toOpen` localization instance (which localizes `M`
407	itself, not the global sections `Γ(⊤, M^~)`), obtained by transporting along the global-sections
408	isomorphism `tilde.isoTop`.  Project-local; the load-bearing local model of the keystone
409	`qcoh_section_isLocalizedModule`. -/
410	lemma tilde_section_isLocalizedModule (M : ModuleCat.{u} R) (f : R) :
411	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
412	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map
413	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := by
414	  have key := tilde.toOpen_res M ⊤ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f) (homOfLE le_top)
415	  -- `toOpen M ⊤` is an isomorphism; view it as a linear equivalence `eTop : M ≃ₗ Γ(⊤, M^~)`
416	  set eTop : M ≃ₗ[R] _ := (asIso (tilde.toOpen M ⊤)).toLinearEquiv with heTop
417	  -- the section-restriction equals `toOpen (D f) ∘ eTop⁻¹` as linear maps
418	  have hmap : ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map
419	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom
420	      = (tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).hom ∘ₗ eTop.symm.toLinearMap := by
421	    apply LinearMap.ext
422	    intro x
423	    have hk := congrArg (fun (m : M ⟶ _) => m.hom (eTop.symm x)) key
424	    simp only [ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply] at hk
425	    have heq : ⇑eTop = ⇑(tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom := by rw [heTop]; ext y; simp
426	    have htop : (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom (eTop.symm x) = x := by
427	      rw [← heq]; exact eTop.apply_symm_apply x
428	    conv_lhs => rw [← htop]
429	    simpa using hk
430	  rw [hmap]
431	  exact IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right (Submonoid.powers f)
432	    (tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).hom eTop.symm
433	
434	/-- **Route B local model (counit-isomorphism case).**  If the tilde–Γ counit of an
435	`𝒪_{Spec R}`-module `F` is an isomorphism (equivalently `F ≅ M^~` for `M = Γ(Spec R, F)`), then the
436	section-restriction `Γ(Spec R, F) → Γ(D(f), F)` exhibits its target as the localization of its
437	source at the powers of `f`.  Obtained by transporting `tilde_section_isLocalizedModule` along the
438	isomorphism `F ≅ Γ(F)^~` (naturality of the section restriction under `modulesSpecToSheaf`).
439	Project-local; this is the per-piece engine of the keystone `qcoh_section_isLocalizedModule`: on
440	each `D(g_j)` of a trivialising cover the quasi-coherent `F` has an isomorphic counit (it carries a
441	global presentation there), so this lemma supplies the `IsLocalizedModule` datum the span-cover
442	descent consumes. -/
443	lemma section_isLocalizedModule_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ (F : (Spec R).Modules)
444	    [IsIso F.fromTildeΓ] (f : R) :
445	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
446	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.map
447	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := by
448	  set M := moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj F with hM
449	  set α : F ≅ tilde M := qcoh_iso_tilde_sections F with hα
450	  -- the underlying presheaf morphism of `modulesSpecToSheaf.map α.hom`
451	  set β := (TopCat.Sheaf.forget (ModuleCat R) (Spec R)).map (modulesSpecToSheaf.map α.hom) with hβ
452	  haveI : IsIso (modulesSpecToSheaf.map α.hom) := inferInstance
453	  haveI : IsIso β := inferInstance
454	  haveI : IsIso (β.app (Opposite.op (⊤ : (Spec R).Opens))) :=
455	    CategoryTheory.NatIso.isIso_app_of_isIso β _
456	  haveI : IsIso (β.app (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f))) :=
457	    CategoryTheory.NatIso.isIso_app_of_isIso β _
458	  -- the two `β`-components as linear equivalences
459	  set eTop : _ ≃ₗ[R] _ :=
460	    (asIso (β.app (Opposite.op (⊤ : (Spec R).Opens)))).toLinearEquiv with heTop
461	  set eDf : _ ≃ₗ[R] _ :=
462	    (asIso (β.app (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)))).toLinearEquiv with heDf
463	  -- the `tilde M` restriction localizes (the local model), conjugate it by `eTop` on the source
464	  haveI hbrick := tilde_section_isLocalizedModule M f
465	  set φ : _ →ₗ[R] _ := ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map
466	      (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom ∘ₗ eTop.toLinearMap with hφ
467	  haveI hφloc : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) φ := by
468	    rw [hφ]
469	    exact IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right (Submonoid.powers f)
470	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map
471	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom eTop
472	  -- naturality of `β`, read at `.hom` level: `eDf ∘ ρ_F = ρ_{tilde M} ∘ eTop = φ`
473	  have hnat := β.naturality (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op
474	  have hnat' : ∀ x, eDf (((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.map
475	      (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom x) = φ x := by
476	    intro x
477	    have hx := LinearMap.congr_fun (congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom hnat) x
478	    simp only [ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply] at hx
479	    exact hx
480	  -- so `ρ_F = eDf⁻¹ ∘ φ`, hence localizes (post-compose a localization with a linear equiv)
481	  have hF : ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.map
482	      (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom
483	      = eDf.symm.toLinearMap ∘ₗ φ := by
484	    apply LinearMap.ext
485	    intro x
486	    change _ = eDf.symm (φ x)
487	    rw [← hnat' x, eDf.symm_apply_apply]
488	  rw [hF]
489	  exact IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv (Submonoid.powers f) φ eDf.symm
490	
491	/-- **Route B keystone, globally-presented case.**  If an `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module `F` admits a *global*
492	presentation (`F.Presentation`), then the section-restriction `Γ(Spec R, F) → Γ(D(f), F)` exhibits
493	its target as the localization of its source at the powers of `f`.  This is the keystone
494	`qcoh_section_isLocalizedModule` for the special — but key — case of a global presentation: it is
495	exactly the situation on each affine piece `D(g_j) ≅ Spec R_{g_j}` of a trivialising cover of a
496	quasi-coherent `F`, where the local quasi-coherence datum supplies a global presentation.  Proved by
497	discharging `[IsIso F.fromTildeΓ]` via Mathlib's `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation` and applying
498	`section_isLocalizedModule_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ`.  Project-local; the unconditional quasi-coherent
499	keystone descends from this case over the cover via `isLocalizedModule_of_span_cover`. -/
500	lemma section_isLocalizedModule_of_presentation (F : (Spec R).Modules)
501	    (P : F.Presentation) (f : R) :
502	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
503	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.map
504	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := by
505	  haveI := isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation F P
506	  exact section_isLocalizedModule_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ F f
507	
508	end LocalModel
509	
510	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Route B step B1: finite presentation cover
511	
512	`qcoh_finite_presentation_cover` (Stacks 01I8, Route B step B1): for a quasi-coherent `F` on
513	`Spec R`, the local quasi-coherence datum (`QuasicoherentData`) refines to a *finite* standard
514	cover `D(g_j) ⊆ U_{φ(j)}` with `span{g_j} = ⊤`, each member `U_{φ(j)}` carrying a presentation of
515	`F.over U_{φ(j)}`.  This is the entry point of the keystone descent: per `g_j` the attached
516	presentation restricts (B2) and bridges (B3/B4) to a global presentation of the affine restriction,
517	which the `IsLocalizedModule` local model then consumes.
518	
519	`coversTop_iSup_eq_top` is the topological translation feeding `exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover`:
520	the cover-of-terminal-object condition of a `QuasicoherentData` on the opens-Grothendieck-topology
521	of a space is the honest `⨆ U_i = ⊤`. -/
522	
523	section FinitePresentationCover
524	
525	/-- A family of opens that covers the terminal object of the opens-Grothendieck-topology of a space
526	has supremum `⊤`.  Project-local: translates the categorical `J.CoversTop` condition carried by a
527	`QuasicoherentData` into the lattice-theoretic `⨆ = ⊤` that `exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover`
528	consumes. -/
529	private lemma coversTop_iSup_eq_top {T : Type*} [TopologicalSpace T] {I : Type*}
530	    (Y : I → TopologicalSpace.Opens T)
531	    (hY : (Opens.grothendieckTopology T).CoversTop Y) :
532	    ⨆ i, Y i = ⊤ := by
533	  rw [eq_top_iff]
534	  intro x _
535	  rw [TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup]
536	  obtain ⟨U, f, hf, hU⟩ := hY ⊤ x (TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_top x)
537	  obtain ⟨i, ⟨g⟩⟩ := hf
538	  exact ⟨i, (leOfHom g) hU⟩
539	
540	/-- **Finite presentation cover from quasi-coherence (Stacks 01I8, Route B step B1).**  For a
541	quasi-coherent `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module `F`, there is a quasi-coherence datum `q` (a cover `q.X` of
542	`Spec R` together with a presentation of `F.over (q.X i)` for each `i`) and a *finite* standard
543	refinement of it: finitely many `g : Fin n → R` with `span (range g) = ⊤` and indices
544	`φ : Fin n → q.I` such that each basic open `D(g j)` lies inside the cover member `q.X (φ j)`.  The
545	presentation of `F.over (q.X (φ j))` carried by `q` is what steps B2–B4 restrict to `D(g j)`.
546	Project-local: packages Mathlib's `QuasicoherentData` (local generation) with the affine finite
547	basic-open refinement (`exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover`) in the exact form the Route B keystone
548	descent consumes. -/
549	lemma qcoh_finite_presentation_cover (F : (Spec R).Modules)
550	    [hF : F.IsQuasicoherent] :
551	    ∃ (q : SheafOfModules.QuasicoherentData.{u, u, u, u} F)
552	      (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R) (φ : Fin n → q.I),
553	      (∀ j, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g j) ≤ q.X (φ j)) ∧ Ideal.span (Set.range g) = ⊤ := by
554	  obtain ⟨q⟩ := hF.nonempty_quasicoherentData
555	  have htop : ⨆ i, q.X i = ⊤ := coversTop_iSup_eq_top q.X q.coversTop
556	  obtain ⟨n, g, φ, hgU, hspan⟩ := exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover q.X htop
557	  exact ⟨q, n, g, φ, hgU, hspan⟩
558	
559	end FinitePresentationCover
560	
561	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Route B keystone: degree-0/1 sheaf-axiom equalizer
562	
563	`qcoh_section_equalizer` (Stacks 01HV(4)/01I8, sheaf-axiom equalizer route) is the degree-`0/1`
564	{\v C}ech equalizer of a sheaf of modules `F` on `Spec R`, read off the sheaf condition for a finite
565	(in fact arbitrary) family `U` covering an open `W`:
566	`0 → Γ(W,F) → ∏ⱼ Γ(Uⱼ,F) → ∏ⱼₖ Γ(Uⱼ ⊓ Uₖ,F)` is exact at both non-zero terms.  It is the entry
567	point of the keystone kernel comparison: instantiated at `W = ⊤` and `W = D(f)` (with `Uⱼ = D(gⱼ)`
568	resp. `Uⱼ = D(f gⱼ)`) it exhibits `Γ(X,F)` and `Γ(D(f),F)` as the kernels of the two overlap
569	differentials, which the localisation comparison then matches.  Non-circular: only the sheaf
570	condition of `F` is used, never a section-localisation identity. -/
571	
572	section SectionEqualizer
573	
574	/-- Restriction of a section across a composite inclusion `A ≤ B ≤ C` equals the single restriction
575	along `A ≤ C` (presheaf functoriality in the thin opens category).  Project-local helper for the
576	degree-0/1 differential computation of `qcoh_section_equalizer`. -/
577	private lemma res_trans_apply (P : TopCat.Presheaf (ModuleCat R) (Spec R))
578	    {A B C : (Spec R).Opens} (h1 : A ≤ B) (h2 : B ≤ C) (s : P.obj (.op C)) :
579	    (P.map (homOfLE h1).op).hom ((P.map (homOfLE h2).op).hom s)
580	      = (P.map (homOfLE (h1.trans h2)).op).hom s := by
581	  rw [← ModuleCat.comp_apply, ← P.map_comp]; rfl
582	
583	/-- **Degree-0/1 sheaf-axiom equalizer (Stacks 01HV(4)/01I8).**  For a sheaf of `𝒪_{Spec R}`-modules
584	`F`, an open `W`, and a family `U : ι → Opens` with `U i ≤ W` for all `i` and `W ≤ ⨆ U i`, the
585	augmented two-term {\v C}ech sequence
586	`0 → Γ(W,F) →[ρ] ∏ᵢ Γ(U i,F) →[δ] ∏_{i,k} Γ(U i ⊓ U k,F)` is exact: the restriction product `ρ` is
587	injective and its range is exactly the kernel of the overlap differential `δ` (the difference of the
588	two restriction-to-overlap maps).  This is the sheaf condition of `F` read in degrees `0` and `1`,
589	proved from `TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf.section_ext` (injectivity) and
590	`TopCat.Sheaf.existsUnique_gluing'` (gluing of a matching family).  Project-local: Mathlib packages
591	the sheaf condition as a categorical limit / unique-gluing statement, not in the explicit
592	`Function.Exact` form on the section modules that the keystone kernel comparison consumes. -/
593	theorem qcoh_section_equalizer (F : (Spec R).Modules) {ι : Type u}
594	    (W : (Spec R).Opens) (U : ι → (Spec R).Opens)
595	    (hUW : ∀ i, U i ≤ W) (hWU : W ≤ iSup U) :
596	    Function.Injective
597	        ((LinearMap.pi fun i =>
598	          ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.map (homOfLE (hUW i)).op).hom) :
599	          (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.obj (.op W) →ₗ[R]
600	            (Π i, (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.obj (.op (U i)))) ∧
601	      Function.Exact
602	        ((LinearMap.pi fun i =>
603	          ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.map (homOfLE (hUW i)).op).hom) :
604	          (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.obj (.op W) →ₗ[R] _)
605	        ((LinearMap.pi fun p : ι × ι =>
606	          ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.map
607	              (homOfLE (inf_le_right : U p.1 ⊓ U p.2 ≤ U p.2)).op).hom ∘ₗ LinearMap.proj p.2
608	          - ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.map
609	              (homOfLE (inf_le_left : U p.1 ⊓ U p.2 ≤ U p.1)).op).hom ∘ₗ LinearMap.proj p.1)) := by
610	  set P := modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F with hP
611	  have hsheaf : P.presheaf.IsSheaf := P.2
612	  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
613	  · -- injectivity: a section is determined by its restrictions to the cover (`section_ext`)
614	    intro s s' hss
615	    have hcomp : ∀ i, (P.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hUW i)).op).hom s
616	        = (P.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hUW i)).op).hom s' := fun i => congrFun hss i
617	    apply hsheaf.section_ext
618	    intro x hx
619	    have hxU : x ∈ iSup U := hWU hx
620	    rw [TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup] at hxU
621	    obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := hxU
622	    exact ⟨U i, hUW i, hi, hcomp i⟩
623	  · -- exactness at the middle: `δ t = 0 ↔ t` glues to a section over `W`
624	    intro t
625	    constructor
626	    · intro ht
627	      have hcompat : TopCat.Presheaf.IsCompatible P.presheaf U t := by
628	        intro i j
629	        have hij := congrFun ht (i, j)
630	        simp only [LinearMap.pi_apply, LinearMap.sub_apply, LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply,
631	          LinearMap.proj_apply, Pi.zero_apply, sub_eq_zero] at hij
632	        exact hij.symm
633	      obtain ⟨s, hs, _⟩ := P.existsUnique_gluing' U W (fun i => homOfLE (hUW i)) hWU t hcompat
634	      exact ⟨s, funext fun i => hs i⟩
635	    · rintro ⟨s, rfl⟩
636	      funext p
637	      simp only [LinearMap.pi_apply, LinearMap.sub_apply, LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply,
638	        LinearMap.proj_apply, Pi.zero_apply, sub_eq_zero]
639	      rw [res_trans_apply, res_trans_apply]
640	
641	end SectionEqualizer
642	
643	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — base-ring descent of `IsLocalizedModule`
644	
645	`isLocalizedModule_powers_restrictScalars_of_algebraMap` is the converse of Mathlib's
646	`IsLocalizedModule.of_restrictScalars`: an `A`-linear map that is a localization at the powers of
647	`algebraMap R A f` (over the larger base `A`) is, viewed `R`-linearly, a localization at the powers
648	of `f` (over the smaller base `R`).  This is the base-ring descent the Route B keystone needs: the
649	per-tile localizations produced over `R_g = Localization.Away g` (the tile's base ring) must be read
650	as `R`-localizations at the powers of `f ∈ R` to feed the kernel comparison, which localizes the
651	`X`-cover equalizer at `powers f ⊆ R`.  Project-local: Mathlib supplies only the ascent
652	direction. -/
653	
654	section BaseRingDescent
655	
656	/-- **Base-ring descent of `IsLocalizedModule` (converse of `of_restrictScalars`).**  Let `A` be an
657	`R`-algebra, let `M N` be `A`-modules with the compatible `R`-module structures (`IsScalarTower`),
658	and let `φ : M →ₗ[A] N`.  If `φ` is a localization at the powers of `algebraMap R A f` *over `A`*,
659	then its `R`-linear restriction `φ.restrictScalars R` is a localization at the powers of `f` *over
660	`R`*.  Proved directly from the three defining clauses of `IsLocalizedModule`, using that
661	`(algebraMap R A f) ^ k • x = f ^ k • x` by the scalar tower.  Project-local: Mathlib has only the
662	ascent `IsLocalizedModule.of_restrictScalars`, not this descent. -/
663	lemma isLocalizedModule_powers_restrictScalars_of_algebraMap
664	    {R A M N : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing A] [Algebra R A]
665	    [AddCommGroup M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module R M] [Module R N] [Module A M] [Module A N]
666	    [IsScalarTower R A M] [IsScalarTower R A N] (f : R) (φ : M →ₗ[A] N)
667	    (h : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (algebraMap R A f)) φ) :
668	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) (φ.restrictScalars R) := by
669	  have hsmul : ∀ (k : ℕ) (n : N), (algebraMap R A f) ^ k • n = f ^ k • n := by
670	    intro k n; rw [← map_pow, algebraMap_smul]
671	  have hsmulM : ∀ (k : ℕ) (m : M), (algebraMap R A f) ^ k • m = f ^ k • m := by
672	    intro k m; rw [← map_pow, algebraMap_smul]
673	  refine ⟨?_, ?_, ?_⟩
674	  · rintro ⟨s, k, rfl⟩
675	    rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff]
676	    have hb := h.map_units ⟨(algebraMap R A f) ^ k, k, rfl⟩
677	    rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff] at hb
678	    convert hb using 1
679	    ext n
680	    simp only [Module.algebraMap_end_apply]
681	    exact (hsmul k n).symm
682	  · intro y
683	    obtain ⟨⟨m, s⟩, hs⟩ := h.surj y
684	    obtain ⟨k, hk⟩ := s.2
685	    simp only [] at hk
686	    refine ⟨⟨m, ⟨f ^ k, k, rfl⟩⟩, ?_⟩
687	    rw [Submonoid.smul_def] at hs ⊢
688	    calc f ^ k • y = (algebraMap R A f) ^ k • y := (hsmul k y).symm
689	      _ = (s : A) • y := by rw [hk]
690	      _ = φ m := hs
691	  · intro x₁ x₂ he
692	    obtain ⟨s, hs⟩ := h.exists_of_eq he
693	    obtain ⟨k, hk⟩ := s.2
694	    simp only [] at hk
695	    refine ⟨⟨f ^ k, k, rfl⟩, ?_⟩
696	    rw [Submonoid.smul_def] at hs ⊢
697	    calc f ^ k • x₁ = (algebraMap R A f) ^ k • x₁ := (hsmulM k x₁).symm
698	      _ = (s : A) • x₁ := by rw [hk]
699	      _ = (s : A) • x₂ := hs
700	      _ = (algebraMap R A f) ^ k • x₂ := by rw [hk]
701	      _ = f ^ k • x₂ := hsmulM k x₂
702	
703	end BaseRingDescent
704	
705	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Route B keystone: per-tile section localization
706	
707	`tile_section_localization` (Stacks 01HV(4)/01I8, the last keystone leaf) shows that for a
708	quasi-coherent `F` on `Spec R` and elements `f g : R` with the tile `F_{(g)}` globally presented, the
709	section-restriction `Γ(D(g), F) → Γ(D(gf), F)` exhibits its target as the localization of its source
710	at the powers of `f`.  It is the per-tile localization datum the sheaf-axiom kernel comparison
711	consumes (`analogies/keystone-descent.md`).
712	
713	The naive recipe "the section comparison is the `restrict_obj` rfl" is UNSOUND: `restrict_obj` is rfl
714	only for the local-ring `SheafOfModules` section functor `Γ(M,-)`, whereas the localization lives in
715	the global-ring functor `modulesSpecToSheaf.obj`, which does NOT commute with restriction
716	definitionally.  Hence the honest base-ring descent: Sub-lemma A (opens identities,
717	`tile_image_opens_identities`) + Sub-lemma B (the load-bearing natural section comparison,
718	`tile_section_comparison`) + the DONE base-ring descent
719	`isLocalizedModule_powers_restrictScalars_of_algebraMap`. -/
720	
721	section TileSectionLocalization
722	
723	open TopologicalSpace
724	
725	/-- **Sub-lemma A (Stacks 01I8): image opens of the affine tile identification.**  Let `g f : R`,
726	let `R_g = Localization.Away g`, and let `ι = specAwayToSpec g : Spec R_g ⟶ Spec R` be the
727	localization morphism identifying `Spec R_g` with `D(g) ⊆ Spec R`.  Then the image opens of the two
728	relevant opens of `Spec R_g` are `ι ''ᵁ ⊤ = D(g)` and `ι ''ᵁ D(f̄) = D(gf)` (with
729	`f̄ = algebraMap R R_g f`), where the images are taken in the iterated-restriction form
730	`(specBasicOpen g).ι ''ᵁ ((iso).inv ''ᵁ -)` matching `modulesRestrictBasicOpen`.  Project-local: the
731	opens bookkeeping that lets the `R_g`-section localization on the tile be matched against the
732	`R`-section restriction `Γ(D(g),F) → Γ(D(gf),F)`. -/
733	lemma tile_image_opens_identities (g f : R) :
734	    (specBasicOpen g).ι ''ᵁ ((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv ''ᵁ
735	        (⊤ : (Spec (.of (Localization.Away g))).Opens)) = specBasicOpen g ∧
736	      (specBasicOpen g).ι ''ᵁ ((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv ''ᵁ
737	          (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g) f)))
738	        = specBasicOpen (g * f) := by
739	  constructor
740	  · rw [show ((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv ''ᵁ
741	        (⊤ : (Spec (.of (Localization.Away g))).Opens)) = ⊤ from by
742	          rw [Scheme.Hom.image_top_eq_opensRange]; exact Scheme.Hom.opensRange_of_isIso _]
743	    simp [Scheme.Hom.image_top_eq_opensRange]
744	  · have hcomp : (specBasicOpen g).ι ''ᵁ ((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv ''ᵁ
745	        (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g) f)))
746	        = specAwayToSpec g ''ᵁ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g) f)) :=
747	      (Scheme.Hom.comp_image _ _ _).symm
748	    rw [hcomp]
749	    apply Opens.ext
750	    rw [Scheme.Hom.coe_image]
751	    rw [show ⇑(specAwayToSpec g)
752	        = PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g)) from by
753	          rw [specAwayToSpec_eq]; rfl]
754	    ext x
755	    simp only [SetLike.mem_coe, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_mul, Set.mem_image]
756	    constructor
757	    · rintro ⟨p, hp, rfl⟩
758	      have hpr : p.asIdeal.IsPrime := p.isPrime
759	      refine ⟨?_, hp⟩
760	      change (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g)) g ∉ p.asIdeal
761	      intro hmem
762	      exact hpr.ne_top (Ideal.eq_top_of_isUnit_mem _ hmem
763	        (IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit g))
764	    · rintro ⟨hg, hf⟩
765	      have hx : x ∈ Set.range (PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g))) := by
766	        rw [PrimeSpectrum.localization_away_comap_range (Localization.Away g) g]; exact hg
767	      obtain ⟨p, rfl⟩ := hx
768	      exact ⟨p, hf, rfl⟩
769	
770	/-
771	`tile_section_localization` (`lem:tile_section_localization`) — DEFERRED this iter.
772	
773	Sub-lemma A (`tile_image_opens_identities`, above) is axiom-clean.  The remaining obstruction is
774	Sub-lemma B (`lem:tile_section_comparison`), which is **genuinely non-definitional** (a clean
775	`lake env lean` build confirms the iter-041 finding, project memory
776	[[keystone-tile-reconciliation-not-rfl]]):
777	
778	* the tile section `(modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (modulesRestrictBasicOpen g F)).presheaf.obj (op V)`
779	  is a `ModuleCat ↑(Localization.Away g)` (the *local* ring `R_g`), whereas the ambient `F`-side
780	  section `(modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.obj (op (ι ''ᵁ V))` is a `ModuleCat ↑R`.  These are
781	  **not even the same type** — `modulesSpecToSheaf` over `R_g` restricts scalars to `R_g`, over `R`
782	  to `R` — so neither the carriers nor the scalar coherence `algebraMap R R_g r • x = r • x` is `rfl`.
783	
784	Hence the base-ring descent route needs the honest Sub-lemma B: a natural `R_g`-linear isomorphism
785	`Γ_{R_g}(V, F_{(g)}) ≅ Γ_R(ι ''ᵁ V, F)` intertwining the restriction maps, built from the structure-
786	sheaf bookkeeping of `modulesSpecToSheaf` (`StructureSheaf.globalSectionsIso` for `R` and `R_g`, the
787	restrict-functor scalar comparison, and the `restrict_obj`/`restrict_map` defeqs at the *local-ring*
788	`SheafOfModules` level — `Γ(M,-)`, which IS rfl, in contrast to the global-ring functor).  Estimated
789	~100–150 LOC.  Once it lands the descent assembles as sketched: Step 2
790	`section_isLocalizedModule_of_presentation` over `R_g`, transport across Sub-lemma B, Step 5
791	`isLocalizedModule_powers_restrictScalars_of_algebraMap`, Step 3 `tile_image_opens_identities` to
792	match the target opens `D(g)`, `D(gf)`.
793	-/
794	
795	end TileSectionLocalization
796	
797	/-! ## Handoff — closing the 01I8 gap
798	
799	The unconditional quasi-coherent statement
800	
801	```
802	theorem qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_qcoh (F : (Spec R).Modules) [IsQuasicoherent F] :
803	    F ≅ tilde (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj F)
804	```
805	
806	is obtained from `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections` the instant the following instance is available:
807	
808	```
809	instance (F : (Spec R).Modules) [IsQuasicoherent F] : IsIso F.fromTildeΓ
810	```
811	
812	equivalently (by `isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff`) `(tilde.functor R).essImage F`, equivalently a
813	**global** `F.Presentation` (fed to `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_of_presentation`).
814	
815	The needed Mathlib-gradient sub-steps (all on the affine base `Spec R`):
816	
817	1. `IsQuasicoherent F` ⟹ `F` is generated by global sections: produce
818	   `F.GeneratingSections` (a global epi `free I ⟶ F`).  On `Spec R` this is the affine
819	   global-generation statement (Hartshorne II.5.16 / Stacks 01I8); `QuasicoherentData`
820	   only gives generation locally on a basic-open cover, which must be globalised using
821	   `PrimeSpectrum.exists_idempotent_basicOpen_eq_of_isClopen`-style partition-of-unity /
822	   the compactness of `Spec R` and the localisation-of-sections property of qcoh sheaves.
823	   **This is the single genuine remaining blocker** (sections of qcoh `F` over `D(f)`
824	   localise — `Γ(D(f), F) = Γ(X, F)_f`, Stacks 01HV(4)/01I8 — is itself absent from Mathlib:
825	   `grep` confirms the only `IsQuasicoherent` content in `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/` is
826	   `Modules/Tilde.lean`, with no localisation-of-sections and no abelian-subcategory closure).
827	2. The kernel of `free I ⟶ F` is again quasi-coherent on `Spec R` (NB: not yet a Mathlib
828	   instance — `kernel σ.π` is not automatically qcoh; this needs the qcoh-is-abelian-subcategory
829	   fact, itself downstream of step 1's local structure), hence again globally generated by
830	   step 1; this yields the two `GeneratingSections` `σ`, `τ` of `F.Presentation`.
831	3. Feed those two generating families to `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_genSections` (below), which
832	   bundles them into `F.Presentation` and applies Mathlib's `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`,
833	   producing the `IsIso F.fromTildeΓ` instance above.
834	
835	**Steps 2–3 are now formalised** as the axiom-clean `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_genSections` and
836	`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_of_genSections` (the structure theorem directly from the two generating
837	families), with `free_isQuasicoherent` recording that free coefficient sheaves are qcoh.  Step 1 —
838	the load-bearing ~few-hundred-LOC affine global-generation / localisation-of-sections input — is
839	the single genuine mathematical blocker; once it supplies `σ : F.GeneratingSections` and
840	`τ : (kernel σ.π).GeneratingSections` for a quasi-coherent `F`, the instance and the unconditional
841	upgrade of `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections` follow with no further work.
842	-/
843	
844	end AlgebraicGeometry

6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.HigherDirectImage
8	
9	/-!
10	# Čech computation of the higher direct images `Rⁱ f_*` (unconditional)
11	
12	This file constructs the higher derived direct images `Rⁱ f_* F` for `i ≥ 1`
13	**without appealing to injective resolutions** in the category of sheaves of
14	modules. The companion `Cohomology/HigherDirectImage.lean` defines `Rⁱ f_*` as a
15	right derived functor, which requires the ambient category of `O_X`-modules to
16	have enough injectives — a property not currently available for sheaves of
17	modules over a sheaf of rings whose value category varies over the site. The
18	Čech approach developed here sidesteps the issue: it computes `Rⁱ f_* F` as the
19	cohomology of an explicit complex built from the pushforwards of `F` over the
20	finite intersections of an affine open cover, producing an **unconditional**
21	construction of `Rⁱ f_*` for quasi-coherent `F` and separated quasi-compact `f`.
22	
23	Throughout, `f : X ⟶ S` is a quasi-compact, separated morphism of schemes (so
24	all finite intersections of an affine open cover of `X` are again affine), and
25	`F : X.Modules` is a quasi-coherent `O_X`-module. A base change of `f` along
26	`g : S' ⟶ S` is recorded by a cartesian square
27	```
28	  X' --g'--> X
29	  |f'        |f
30	  v          v
31	  S' --g---> S
32	```
33	with `F' = (g')^* F` the pullback of `F` to `X'`.
34	
35	The six main declarations are:
36	
37	* `AlgebraicGeometry.CechNerve` — the (augmented) Čech nerve of an affine open
38	  cover, an augmented cosimplicial object of `O_X`-modules.
39	* `AlgebraicGeometry.CechComplex` — the relative Čech complex in `QCoh(S)`, a
40	  cochain complex of `O_S`-modules whose degree-`p` term is the product of the
41	  pushforwards of `F` over the `(p+1)`-fold intersections of the cover.
42	* `AlgebraicGeometry.CechAcyclic.affine` — Čech acyclicity on affines: the Čech
43	  complex of a standard cover of an affine scheme has vanishing cohomology in
44	  all positive degrees (Serre vanishing for quasi-coherent sheaves on affines).
45	* `AlgebraicGeometry.cech_computes_higherDirectImage` — the cohomology of the
46	  relative Čech complex is canonically isomorphic to `Rⁱ f_* F` wherever the
47	  derived functor is defined.
48	
49	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex`.
50	
51	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, §Čech cohomology of quasi-coherent
52	sheaves and §Quasi-coherence of higher direct images; Tags 02KE
53	(`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent`), 02KG
54	(`lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero`).
55	-/
56	
57	universe u
58	
59	open CategoryTheory Limits
60	
61	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
62	
63	open Scheme.Modules
64	
65	variable {S S' X X' : Scheme.{u}}
66	
67	/- The Čech nerve `CechNerve` is *constructed* (no longer a hole) further below, once the
68	geometric backbone (`coverCechNerveOverAug`) and the push–pull functor (`pushPullFunctor`)
69	are in scope; see its definition just before `relativeCechComplexOfNerve`. -/
70	
71	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — scheme-level Čech nerve backbone
72	
73	The genuine construction of the {\v C}ech nerve `CechNerve` factors through two
74	ingredients that are independent of one another:
75	
76	* a *geometric* backbone — the augmented {\v C}ech nerve of the cover, an
77	  augmented simplicial **scheme** over `X` (the iterated fibre powers of
78	  `∐ᵢ Uᵢ` over `X`), which exists unconditionally because `Scheme` has all
79	  finite limits; and
80	* a *push-pull* functor `(Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`, `(Y, p) ↦ p_* p^* F`, that
81	  turns the simplicial scheme over `X` into the cosimplicial `O_X`-module
82	  `CechNerve`.
83	
84	The backbone (`coverArrow`, `coverCechNerve`) is built here axiom-clean. The
85	push-pull functor is the remaining gap: its `map_comp` requires the
86	`pushforwardComp` / `pullbackComp` coherence isomorphisms (the same coherence
87	quagmire active in `Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean`), so `CechNerve` itself is
88	left as the single genuine hole.
89	
90	Independently of the nerve, the passage *from* an augmented cosimplicial
91	`O_X`-module *to* the relative {\v C}ech cochain complex in `QCoh(S)` is pure,
92	coherence-free plumbing (`relativeCechComplexOfNerve`): forget the augmentation,
93	push forward along `f` via `CosimplicialObject.whiskering`, and take the
94	alternating coface-map cochain complex. We record it here so that `CechComplex`
95	is *defined* in terms of `CechNerve` — closing `CechNerve` axiom-clean
96	immediately yields an axiom-clean `CechComplex`. -/
97	
98	/-- The arrow `∐ᵢ Uᵢ ⟶ X` (`Sigma.desc 𝒰.f`) attached to an open cover `𝒰` of a
99	scheme `X`. Its augmented {\v C}ech nerve is the geometric backbone of the
100	relative {\v C}ech complex. Project-local: packages the cover as a single arrow
101	so the existing `Arrow.augmentedCechNerve` machinery applies. -/
102	noncomputable def coverArrow (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) : Arrow Scheme.{u} :=
103	  Arrow.mk (Sigma.desc 𝒰.f)
104	
105	/-- The augmented {\v C}ech nerve of an open cover `𝒰`, as an augmented
106	simplicial scheme over `X`: in simplicial degree `p` it is the `(p+1)`-fold
107	fibre power of `∐ᵢ Uᵢ` over `X`, i.e. `∐_{(i₀,…,i_p)} U_{i₀} ×ₓ ⋯ ×ₓ U_{i_p}`,
108	with augmentation the cover map to `X`. Exists unconditionally because `Scheme`
109	has all finite limits (hence the wide pullbacks used by
110	`Arrow.augmentedCechNerve`). Project-local geometric backbone for `CechNerve`. -/
111	noncomputable def coverCechNerve (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) :
112	    SimplicialObject.Augmented Scheme.{u} :=
113	  (coverArrow 𝒰).augmentedCechNerve
114	
115	/-! ### Push–pull functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules` — object and morphism bricks
116	
117	The geometric backbone above is lifted to a cosimplicial `O_X`-module by the
118	*relative-direct-image functor on the over-category*
119	```
120	  G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules,   (Y, p) ↦ p_* p^* F,
121	```
122	sending an `X`-scheme `p : Y ⟶ X` to the pushforward along `p` of the pullback
123	`p^* F`. We record here the two *pre-coherence* bricks of `G` — its action on
124	objects (`pushPullObj`, the planner's `Gobj`) and on morphisms (`pushPullMap`,
125	the planner's `Gmap`) — both axiom-clean and free of any functor law. The functor
126	laws `G(𝟙) = 𝟙` and `G(g ≫ h) = G(h) ≫ G(g)` are a *consumer* of the
127	pushforward/pullback composition coherence (`pushforwardComp` / `pullbackComp` and
128	their unitor/pentagon identities) and are deferred; see the note after
129	`pushPullMap`. -/
130	
131	/-- The object map of the push–pull functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`,
132	`(Y, p) ↦ p_* p^* F`. Sends an `X`-scheme `Y` (with structure map `Y.hom : Y.left
133	⟶ X`) to the pushforward along `Y.hom` of the pullback of `F`. Project-local
134	object brick of the {\v C}ech push–pull functor (the planner's `Gobj`). -/
135	noncomputable def pushPullObj (F : X.Modules) (Y : Over X) : X.Modules :=
136	  (pushforward Y.hom).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F)
137	
138	/-- The morphism map of the push–pull functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`. For a
139	morphism `g : Y₂ ⟶ Y₁` of `X`-schemes (so `g.left ≫ Y₁.hom = Y₂.hom`, the
140	over-triangle `Over.w g`), the contravariant functor produces the restriction
141	comparison `pushPullObj F Y₁ ⟶ pushPullObj F Y₂`. It is the three-step composite:
142	the unit `η` of the adjunction `pullback g.left ⊣ pushforward g.left`, the
143	pushforward comparison `(pushforwardComp g.left Y₁.hom).hom`, and the pushforward
144	of the pullback comparison `(pullbackComp g.left Y₁.hom).hom`, glued by two
145	`eqToHom` transports along the over-triangle `Over.w g`. No functor law is used:
146	this is a reusable pre-coherence brick (the planner's `Gmap`). -/
147	noncomputable def pushPullMap (F : X.Modules) {Y₁ Y₂ : Over X} (g : Y₂ ⟶ Y₁) :
148	    pushPullObj F Y₁ ⟶ pushPullObj F Y₂ :=
149	  (pushforward Y₁.hom).map
150	      ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g.left).unit.app
151	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y₁.hom).obj F)) ≫
152	    (pushforwardComp g.left Y₁.hom).hom.app
153	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g.left).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y₁.hom).obj F)) ≫
154	    eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (pushforward q).obj
155	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g.left).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y₁.hom).obj F)))
156	      (Over.w g)) ≫
157	    (pushforward Y₂.hom).map ((pullbackComp g.left Y₁.hom).hom.app F) ≫
158	    eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (pushforward Y₂.hom).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F))
159	      (Over.w g))
160	
161	/- **The functor laws `pushPullMap_id` / `pushPullMap_comp`.**
162	Assembling `pushPullObj` / `pushPullMap` into the functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤
163	X.Modules` requires
164	```
165	  pushPullMap_id   : pushPullMap F (𝟙 Y) = 𝟙 (pushPullObj F Y)
166	  pushPullMap_comp : pushPullMap F (g ≫ h) = pushPullMap F h ≫ pushPullMap F g
167	```
168	Both laws are proved axiom-clean below (see `pushPullMap_id` and `pushPullMap_comp`),
169	and `pushPullFunctor` is assembled from them immediately after. -/
170	
171	/-! ### Functor laws of the push–pull functor `G` -/
172	
173	/-- Identity law of the push–pull functor `G`. -/
174	lemma pushPullMap_id (F : X.Modules) (Y : Over X) :
175	    pushPullMap F (𝟙 Y) = 𝟙 (pushPullObj F Y) := by
176	  -- `star`: the unit-triangle for the identity adjunction `pullback 𝟙 ⊣ pushforward 𝟙`.
177	  have star := unit_conjugateEquiv (Adjunction.id (C := Y.left.Modules))
178	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (𝟙 Y.left))
179	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId Y.left).hom ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F)
180	  rw [Scheme.Modules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom] at star
181	  simp only [Adjunction.id_unit, NatTrans.id_app, Functor.id_obj] at star
182	  -- `hru`: right-unitality of the pullback pseudofunctor, applied at `F`.
183	  have hru := Scheme.Modules.pseudofunctor_right_unitality (X := Y.left) (f := Y.hom)
184	  have hru2 := congrArg (fun t => NatTrans.app t F) hru
185	  simp only [NatTrans.comp_app, Functor.whiskerLeft_app, Functor.rightUnitor_hom_app] at hru2
186	  have hpf : (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (𝟙 Y.left) Y.hom).hom.app
187	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 Y.left)).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F)) ≫
188	      eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward q).obj
189	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 Y.left)).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F)))
190	        (Category.id_comp Y.hom)) =
191	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward Y.hom).map ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId Y.left).hom.app
192	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 Y.left)).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F))) := by
193	    apply Scheme.Modules.hom_ext
194	    intro U; rfl
195	  -- the unit zig-zag for the identity adjunction collapses on `M`
196	  have hzig : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (𝟙 Y.left)).unit.app
197	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F) ≫
198	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId Y.left).hom.app
199	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 Y.left)).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F)) ≫
200	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId Y.left).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F) =
201	      𝟙 ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F) := by
202	    have hnat := (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId Y.left).hom.naturality
203	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackId Y.left).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F))
204	    simp only [Functor.id_map] at hnat
205	    erw [← hnat, ← reassoc_of% star]
206	    exact Iso.inv_hom_id_app _ _
207	  -- the pullback comparison + the over-triangle transport collapse via right-unitality
208	  have hib_inner : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (𝟙 Y.left) Y.hom).hom.app F ≫
209	      eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F) (Category.id_comp Y.hom)) =
210	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId Y.left).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F) := by
211	    rw [eqToHom_app] at hru2
212	    rw [← hru2, ← Category.assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_app]; simp
213	  have hib : (Scheme.Modules.pushforward Y.hom).map
214	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (𝟙 Y.left) Y.hom).hom.app F) ≫
215	      eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward Y.hom).obj
216	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) (Category.id_comp Y.hom)) =
217	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward Y.hom).map
218	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackId Y.left).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F)) := by
219	    have he : eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward Y.hom).obj
220	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) (Category.id_comp Y.hom)) =
221	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward Y.hom).map
222	          (eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)
223	            (Category.id_comp Y.hom))) := by
224	      rw [eqToHom_map]
225	    rw [he, ← Functor.map_comp]; exact congrArg _ hib_inner
226	  -- assemble
227	  simp only [pushPullMap, Over.id_left]
228	  erw [reassoc_of% hpf, hib, ← Functor.map_comp]
229	  erw [hzig, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]; rfl

232	/-- **Base-change unit (mate) identity for the push–pull head.**
233	For composable scheme morphisms `f : A ⟶ B`, `p : B ⟶ Z` and `N : Z.Modules`, the
234	adjunction unit at `N` for `p` followed by the *head* of `pushPullMap` (the
235	pushforward of the unit for `f`, then the pushforward comparison) equals the unit
236	for the composite `f ≫ p` followed by the pushforward of the inverse pullback
237	comparison. This is the mate-calculus core that converts the single-morphism unit
238	`η^{f≫p}` into the iterated units `η^p`, `η^f`; it is the reusable ingredient that
239	the functoriality (pentagon) law of `pushPullMap` repeatedly consumes when
240	splitting a composite unit. Project-local supplement. -/
241	lemma pushPull_unit_mate {A B Z : Scheme.{u}} (f : A ⟶ B) (p : B ⟶ Z)
242	    (N : Z.Modules) :
243	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p).unit.app N ≫
244	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p).map
245	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).unit.app
246	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).obj N)) ≫
247	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp f p).hom.app
248	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).obj N)) =
249	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (f ≫ p)).unit.app N ≫
250	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (f ≫ p)).map
251	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f p).inv.app N) := by
252	  have key := unit_conjugateEquiv
253	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p).comp
254	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f))
255	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (f ≫ p))
256	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f p).inv N
257	  rw [Scheme.Modules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv, Adjunction.comp_unit_app] at key
258	  simpa only [Category.assoc] using key
259	
260	/-- **Over-triangle transport cancellation for the push–pull tail** (kernel-cheap
261	generalised form). The morphism map `pushPullMap` glues its pullback-comparison leg
262	to the target object `pushPullObj F Y₂` by two `eqToHom` coercions along the
263	over-triangle `g.left ≫ Y₁.hom = Y₂.hom`. Cancelling those coercions *in situ*
264	(at the concrete pushforward/pullback objects) provokes a kernel `whnf` blow-up.
265	This lemma states the cancellation **with the over-triangle equality as a free
266	hypothesis** `h : gl ≫ p₁ = p₂`, so the proof is a single `subst h` (after which
267	the transports become `eqToHom rfl = 𝟙` and vanish — kernel-cheap) followed by
268	`simp`. Applying it to `pushPullMap` via `rw` rewrites the tail without forcing the
269	kernel to unfold the comparison objects: the over-triangle leg
270	`eqToHom ≫ (pushforward p₂).map (pullbackComp).hom ≫ eqToHom` collapses to the
271	transport-light `(pushforward (gl ≫ p₁)).map (pullbackComp).hom ≫ eqToHom`, the
272	single residual `eqToHom` carrying the unavoidable object identification of the
273	codomain `pushPullObj F Y₂`. Reusable pre-coherence brick for `pushPullMap_comp`. -/
274	lemma pushPull_transport_cancel {Y₁ Y₂ : Scheme.{u}}
275	    (gl : Y₂ ⟶ Y₁) (p₁ : Y₁ ⟶ X) (p₂ : Y₂ ⟶ X)
276	    (h : gl ≫ p₁ = p₂) (F : X.Modules) :
277	    eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward q).obj
278	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback gl).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F))) h) ≫
279	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₂).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp gl p₁).hom.app F) ≫
280	      eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₂).obj
281	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) h) =
282	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (gl ≫ p₁)).map
283	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp gl p₁).hom.app F) ≫
284	      eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward q).obj
285	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) h) := by
286	  subst h
287	  simp

289	/-- **Composite-unit decomposition for the push–pull head.** The adjunction unit
290	`η^{f≫p}` for a composite morphism, expressed through the iterated units `η^p`,
291	`η^f` and the pushforward/pullback comparison isomorphisms. This is the
292	`pushPull_unit_mate` identity solved for `η^{f≫p}` (post-composing with
293	`(f≫p)_*(pullbackComp).hom` cancels the `pullbackComp.inv` factor). Reusable brick
294	for the composition law of `pushPullMap`. -/
295	lemma pushPull_unit_comp {A B Z : Scheme.{u}} (f : A ⟶ B) (p : B ⟶ Z)
296	    (N : Z.Modules) :
297	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (f ≫ p)).unit.app N =
298	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p).unit.app N ≫
299	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p).map
300	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).unit.app
301	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).obj N)) ≫
302	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp f p).hom.app
303	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).obj N)) ≫
304	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (f ≫ p)).map
305	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f p).hom.app N) := by
306	  have m := pushPull_unit_mate f p N
307	  erw [reassoc_of% m, ← Functor.map_comp, Iso.inv_hom_id_app,
308	    CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id, Category.comp_id]
309	
310	/-- The pushforward pseudofunctor is *strict* on sheaves of modules: the
311	`pushforwardComp` comparison `2`-cell is the identity on the nose. Holds by `rfl`
312	(`pushforward (a ≫ p) = pushforward a ⋙ pushforward p` definitionally). Project-local
313	collapse used to discharge the pushforward legs of the push–pull pentagon. -/
314	lemma pushforwardComp_hom_app_id {Z₁ Z₂ Z₃ : Scheme.{u}} (a : Z₂ ⟶ Z₁) (p : Z₁ ⟶ Z₃)
315	    (M : Z₂.Modules) : (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp a p).hom.app M = 𝟙 _ :=
316	  rfl
