63	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — middle-term quasi-isomorphism transfer
64	
65	Given a morphism `φ : S₁ ⟶ S₂` between two short exact sequences of homological complexes
66	in an abelian category, Mathlib proves that if `φ.τ₁` and `φ.τ₂` are quasi-isomorphisms then so
67	is `φ.τ₃` (`HomologicalComplex.HomologySequence.quasiIso_τ₃`). The companion statements for `φ.τ₁`
68	and `φ.τ₂` are an explicit Mathlib TODO (see `HomologySequenceLemmas.lean`). We supply the `φ.τ₂`
69	version here: it is the engine that proves the horseshoe middle complex `I_B` is an injective
70	*resolution* of `B` (the outer verticals `I_A.ι`, `I_C.ι` are quasi-isos, hence so is the middle
71	augmentation). The proof mirrors Mathlib's `τ₃` argument: a homology four-lemma on the windows of
72	`composableArrows₅`, with the boundary degrees (no predecessor / no successor) handled by
73	`mono_homologyMap_of_mono_of_not_rel` / `epi_homologyMap_of_epi_of_not_rel`. -/
74	
75	namespace HomologicalComplex.HomologySequence
76	
77	open CategoryTheory ComposableArrows Abelian Limits
78	
79	variable {C ι : Type*} [Category C] [Abelian C] {c : ComplexShape ι}
80	  {S₁ S₂ : ShortComplex (HomologicalComplex C c)} (φ : S₁ ⟶ S₂)
81	  (hS₁ : S₁.ShortExact) (hS₂ : S₂.ShortExact)
82	
83	include hS₁ hS₂ in
84	/-- **Middle-term quasi-isomorphism transfer** (the `τ₂` companion of Mathlib's `quasiIso_τ₃`).
85	If `φ.τ₁` and `φ.τ₃` are quasi-isomorphisms then so is `φ.τ₂`, provided that at each boundary
86	degree (one with no incoming / no outgoing differential) the middle component `φ.τ₂.f i` is a
87	mono / epi respectively. This is the `lean_aux` infrastructure behind
88	`InjectiveResolution.ofShortExact_resolvesMiddle`. -/
89	lemma quasiIso_τ₂ (h₁ : QuasiIso φ.τ₁) (h₃ : QuasiIso φ.τ₃)
90	    (hbMono : ∀ i, (∀ k, ¬ c.Rel k i) → Mono (φ.τ₂.f i))
91	    (hbEpi : ∀ i, (∀ j, ¬ c.Rel i j) → Epi (φ.τ₂.f i)) :
92	    QuasiIso φ.τ₂ := by
93	  have hI1 : ∀ d, IsIso (homologyMap φ.τ₁ d) := fun d => by
94	    rw [← quasiIsoAt_iff_isIso_homologyMap]; exact (quasiIso_iff φ.τ₁).1 h₁ d
95	  have hI3 : ∀ d, IsIso (homologyMap φ.τ₃ d) := fun d => by
96	    rw [← quasiIsoAt_iff_isIso_homologyMap]; exact (quasiIso_iff φ.τ₃).1 h₃ d
97	  have hE1 : ∀ d, Epi (homologyMap φ.τ₁ d) := fun d => have := hI1 d; inferInstance
98	  have hM1 : ∀ d, Mono (homologyMap φ.τ₁ d) := fun d => have := hI1 d; inferInstance
99	  have hE3 : ∀ d, Epi (homologyMap φ.τ₃ d) := fun d => have := hI3 d; inferInstance
100	  have hM3 : ∀ d, Mono (homologyMap φ.τ₃ d) := fun d => have := hI3 d; inferInstance
101	  rw [quasiIso_iff]
102	  intro i
103	  rw [quasiIsoAt_iff_isIso_homologyMap]
104	  have hEpi : Epi (homologyMap φ.τ₂ i) := by
105	    by_cases hi : ∃ j, c.Rel i j
106	    · obtain ⟨j, hij⟩ := hi
107	      apply epi_of_epi_of_epi_of_mono
108	        ((δlastFunctor ⋙ δlastFunctor).map (mapComposableArrows₅ φ hS₁ hS₂ i j hij))
109	      · exact (composableArrows₅_exact hS₁ i j hij).δlast.δlast
110	      · exact (composableArrows₅_exact hS₂ i j hij).δlast.δlast
111	      · exact hE1 i
112	      · exact hE3 i
113	      · exact hM1 j
114	    · have hi' : ∀ j, ¬ c.Rel i j := fun j hj => hi ⟨j, hj⟩
115	      have := hbEpi i hi'
116	      exact epi_homologyMap_of_epi_of_not_rel φ.τ₂ i hi'
117	  have hMono : Mono (homologyMap φ.τ₂ i) := by
118	    by_cases hi : ∃ k, c.Rel k i
119	    · obtain ⟨k, hki⟩ := hi
120	      apply mono_of_epi_of_mono_of_mono
121	        ((δ₀Functor ⋙ δ₀Functor).map (mapComposableArrows₅ φ hS₁ hS₂ k i hki))
122	      · exact (composableArrows₅_exact hS₁ k i hki).δ₀.δ₀
123	      · exact (composableArrows₅_exact hS₂ k i hki).δ₀.δ₀
124	      · exact hE3 k
125	      · exact hM1 i
126	      · exact hM3 i
127	    · have hi' : ∀ k, ¬ c.Rel k i := fun k hk => hi ⟨k, hk⟩
128	      have := hbMono i hi'
129	      exact mono_homologyMap_of_mono_of_not_rel φ.τ₂ i hi'
130	  exact isIso_of_mono_of_epi _
131	
132	end HomologicalComplex.HomologySequence
133	
134	namespace CategoryTheory
135	
136	variable {𝒜 : Type*} [Category 𝒜] [Abelian 𝒜] [HasInjectiveResolutions 𝒜]
137	variable {ℬ : Type*} [Category ℬ] [Abelian ℬ]
138	
139	/-!
140	### Right-acyclic objects
141	Blueprint: `def:right_acyclic` (§ "Right-acyclic objects").
142	-/
143	
144	/-- An object `J : 𝒜` is *right-`G`-acyclic* when every higher right-derived
145	functor of `G` vanishes at `J`:
146	```
147	(Rᵏ⁺¹ G)(J) = 0   for all k : ℕ.
148	```
149	The index-shifted quantifier `k + 1` matches the statement of
150	`Functor.isZero_rightDerived_obj_injective_succ` and avoids an inequality
151	side-condition; it is equivalent to `(Rⁿ G)(J) = 0` for all `n ≥ 1`.
152	
153	Blueprint: `CategoryTheory.Functor.IsRightAcyclic` (`def:right_acyclic`).
154	-/
155	class Functor.IsRightAcyclic (G : 𝒜 ⥤ ℬ) [G.Additive] (J : 𝒜) : Prop where
156	  vanish : ∀ k : ℕ, Limits.IsZero ((G.rightDerived (k + 1)).obj J)
157	
158	/-- Every injective object is right-`G`-acyclic.
159	Follows immediately from `Functor.isZero_rightDerived_obj_injective_succ`. -/
160	instance (priority := 100) Functor.IsRightAcyclic.ofInjective
161	    (G : 𝒜 ⥤ ℬ) [G.Additive] (J : 𝒜) [Injective J] : Functor.IsRightAcyclic G J where
162	  vanish k := Functor.isZero_rightDerived_obj_injective_succ G k J
163	-- Note: `Functor.isZero_rightDerived_obj_injective_succ` returns
164	-- `Limits.IsZero ((G.rightDerived (k+1)).obj J)`, matching the class field.
165	
166	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — acyclic resolutions
167	
168	The declarations in this section are project-local infrastructure feeding the
169	dimension-shift and acyclic-resolution comparison theorems (Stacks Tag 015D/015E).
170	They are not yet in Mathlib. -/
171	
172	open Limits
173	
174	/-- The cohomology of the `G`-image of an injective resolution of a right-`G`-acyclic
175	object `J` vanishes in every positive degree. This is the homology-level form of
176	right-acyclicity, obtained by transporting the vanishing of `(R^{k+1} G)(J)` across
177	`InjectiveResolution.isoRightDerivedObj`. It is the input that kills the middle terms
178	of the homology long exact sequence in the dimension-shift step. -/
179	lemma Functor.isZero_homology_mapHomologicalComplex_of_isRightAcyclic
180	    (G : 𝒜 ⥤ ℬ) [G.Additive] {J : 𝒜} (I : InjectiveResolution J)
181	    [G.IsRightAcyclic J] (k : ℕ) :
182	    IsZero ((HomologicalComplex.homologyFunctor ℬ (ComplexShape.up ℕ) (k + 1)).obj
183	      ((G.mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj I.cocomplex)) :=
184	  (Functor.IsRightAcyclic.vanish (G := G) (J := J) k).of_iso
185	    (I.isoRightDerivedObj G (k + 1)).symm
186	
187	omit [HasInjectiveResolutions 𝒜] in
188	/-- A short complex of cochain complexes that is *degreewise split* (each degree carries a
189	`ShortComplex.Splitting`) is short exact. Project-local because Mathlib only provides the
190	degreewise-short-exact criterion `shortExact_of_degreewise_shortExact`; this packages the
191	common special case where the degreewise data is a splitting. -/
192	lemma shortExact_of_degreewise_splitting
193	    {S : ShortComplex (CochainComplex 𝒜 ℕ)}
194	    (splits : ∀ n, (S.map (HomologicalComplex.eval 𝒜 (ComplexShape.up ℕ) n)).Splitting) :
195	    S.ShortExact :=
196	  HomologicalComplex.shortExact_of_degreewise_shortExact S (fun n => (splits n).shortExact)
197	
198	omit [HasInjectiveResolutions 𝒜] in
199	/-- An additive functor applied degreewise to a degreewise-split short complex of cochain
200	complexes yields a short exact short complex. This is the step where degreewise splitness is
201	essential: `G` is not assumed exact, but it preserves the *split* short exact sequences in each
202	degree (`ShortComplex.Splitting.map`), and degreewise short exactness then assembles to a short
203	exact sequence of complexes. -/
204	lemma shortExact_map_mapHomologicalComplex_of_degreewise_splitting
205	    {S : ShortComplex (CochainComplex 𝒜 ℕ)}
206	    (splits : ∀ n, (S.map (HomologicalComplex.eval 𝒜 (ComplexShape.up ℕ) n)).Splitting)
207	    (G : 𝒜 ⥤ ℬ) [G.Additive] :
208	    (S.map (G.mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ))).ShortExact :=
209	  HomologicalComplex.shortExact_of_degreewise_shortExact _
210	    (fun n => ((splits n).map G).shortExact)
211	
212	/-- **Dimension shift, part (1), from a degreewise-split SES of injective resolutions.**
213	Given a short exact sequence `0 → A → J → Z → 0` lifted (via the horseshoe) to a
214	degreewise-split short exact sequence of injective resolutions
215	`0 → I_A → I_J → I_Z → 0`, presented here as chain maps `φ, ψ` with degreewise splittings,
216	and with the middle object `J` right-`G`-acyclic, the connecting map of the homology long
217	exact sequence of `G(I_•)` is an isomorphism in every positive degree:
218	`(R^{k+1} G)(Z) ≅ (R^{k+2} G)(A)`.
219	
220	This is the engine of the staircase induction (`rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution`).
221	It is stated over the *resolution-level* SES because the object-level dimension-shift theorem
222	`rightDerivedShiftIsoOfAcyclic` requires the horseshoe lift to produce that SES; once the
223	horseshoe is available, the object-level statement follows by feeding its output here. -/
224	noncomputable def Functor.rightDerivedShiftIsoOfSplitResolutionSES
225	    (G : 𝒜 ⥤ ℬ) [G.Additive] {A J Z : 𝒜}
226	    (I_A : InjectiveResolution A) (I_J : InjectiveResolution J) (I_Z : InjectiveResolution Z)
227	    [G.IsRightAcyclic J]
228	    (φ : I_A.cocomplex ⟶ I_J.cocomplex) (ψ : I_J.cocomplex ⟶ I_Z.cocomplex)
229	    (w : φ ≫ ψ = 0)
230	    (splits : ∀ n, ((ShortComplex.mk φ ψ w).map
231	      (HomologicalComplex.eval 𝒜 (ComplexShape.up ℕ) n)).Splitting)
232	    (k : ℕ) :
233	    (G.rightDerived (k + 1)).obj Z ≅ (G.rightDerived (k + 2)).obj A :=
234	  have hSG : ((ShortComplex.mk φ ψ w).map
235	      (G.mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ))).ShortExact :=
236	    shortExact_map_mapHomologicalComplex_of_degreewise_splitting splits G
237	  (I_Z.isoRightDerivedObj G (k + 1)) ≪≫
238	    hSG.δIso (k + 1) (k + 2) (by simp)
239	      (G.isZero_homology_mapHomologicalComplex_of_isRightAcyclic I_J k)
240	      (G.isZero_homology_mapHomologicalComplex_of_isRightAcyclic I_J (k + 1)) ≪≫
241	    (I_A.isoRightDerivedObj G (k + 2)).symm
242	
243	omit [HasInjectiveResolutions 𝒜] in
244	/-- **Horseshoe per-stage monomorphism.** Given an exact short complex
245	`A → B → C` with `A → B` a monomorphism, and monomorphisms `α : A ↪ P`, `γ : C ↪ Q` with `P`
246	injective, the map `B → P ⊞ Q` whose first component is the injective extension of `α` along
247	`A ↪ B` (`Injective.factorThru α S.f`) and whose second component is `B ↠ C ↪ Q` is itself a
248	monomorphism. This is the cokernel/kernel step driving each stage of the dual Horseshoe Lemma
249	`InjectiveResolution.ofShortExact`: applied to the `n`-th cosyzygy short exact sequence with
250	`P = I_A.X (n+1)`, `Q = I_C.X (n+1)`, it produces the monomorphism into the next biproduct term
251	whose cokernel feeds the following stage. -/
252	lemma mono_biprod_lift_factorThru_of_exact {S : ShortComplex 𝒜} (hS : S.Exact) [Mono S.f]
253	    {P Q : 𝒜} [Injective P] (α : S.X₁ ⟶ P) [Mono α] (γ : S.X₃ ⟶ Q) [Mono γ] :
254	    Mono (biprod.lift (Injective.factorThru α S.f) (S.g ≫ γ)) := by
255	  rw [Preadditive.mono_iff_cancel_zero]
256	  intro T x hx
257	  have h1 : x ≫ Injective.factorThru α S.f = 0 := by simpa using hx =≫ biprod.fst
258	  have h2 : x ≫ S.g ≫ γ = 0 := by simpa using hx =≫ biprod.snd
259	  have hxg : x ≫ S.g = 0 := by rw [← cancel_mono γ, zero_comp, Category.assoc]; exact h2
260	  have hyf : hS.lift x hxg ≫ S.f = x := hS.lift_f x hxg
261	  have hya : hS.lift x hxg ≫ α = 0 := by
262	    have h3 : hS.lift x hxg ≫ S.f ≫ Injective.factorThru α S.f = 0 := by
263	      rw [← Category.assoc, hyf]; exact h1
264	    rwa [Injective.comp_factorThru] at h3
265	  have hy0 : hS.lift x hxg = 0 := by rw [← cancel_mono α, zero_comp]; exact hya
266	  rw [← hyf, hy0, zero_comp]
267	
268	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — twisted biproduct of cochain complexes
269	
270	This block builds the *structural* core of the dual Horseshoe Lemma as a general, injective-free
271	construction. Given two cochain complexes `K`, `L` and a degreewise family
272	`τ n : L.X n ⟶ K.X (n+1)` satisfying the cocycle identity
273	`L.d n (n+1) ≫ τ (n+1) = -(τ n ≫ K.d (n+1) (n+2))`, the biproduct objects `K.X n ⊞ L.X n` with the
274	twisted matrix differential `[[d_K, τ], [0, d_L]]` form a cochain complex `twistedBiprod τ hτ`,
275	the coprojection `K ⟶ twistedBiprod` and projection `twistedBiprod ⟶ L` are chain maps, and every
276	degree is the canonical split short exact sequence of the biproduct.
277	
278	This is exactly the content of the blueprint sub-lemmas `lem:horseshoe_dComp` (the differential
279	squares to zero) and `lem:horseshoe_chainMap` (the coprojection/projection are chain maps and the
280	degrees split); it is isolated here free of injectivity because the only inputs are the cocycle
281	identity and the biproduct structure. The horseshoe specialises it to `K = I_A.cocomplex`,
282	`L = I_C.cocomplex` once the twist `τ` has been produced by injectivity. -/
283	
284	section TwistedBiprod
285	
286	omit [HasInjectiveResolutions 𝒜]
287	
288	variable {K L : CochainComplex 𝒜 ℕ} (τ : ∀ n, L.X n ⟶ K.X (n + 1))
289	
290	/-- The twisted matrix differential `[[d_K, τ], [0, d_L]]` on the degreewise biproduct
291	`K.X n ⊞ L.X n ⟶ K.X (n+1) ⊞ L.X (n+1)`. -/
292	noncomputable def twistedBiprodD (n : ℕ) :
293	    (K.X n ⊞ L.X n) ⟶ (K.X (n + 1) ⊞ L.X (n + 1)) :=
294	  biprod.lift (biprod.fst ≫ K.d n (n + 1) + biprod.snd ≫ τ n) (biprod.snd ≫ L.d n (n + 1))
295	
296	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
297	lemma twistedBiprodD_fst (n : ℕ) :
298	    twistedBiprodD τ n ≫ biprod.fst = biprod.fst ≫ K.d n (n + 1) + biprod.snd ≫ τ n := by
299	  simp [twistedBiprodD]
300	
301	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
302	lemma twistedBiprodD_snd (n : ℕ) :
303	    twistedBiprodD τ n ≫ biprod.snd = biprod.snd ≫ L.d n (n + 1) := by
304	  simp [twistedBiprodD]
305	
306	variable (hτ : ∀ n, L.d n (n + 1) ≫ τ (n + 1) = -(τ n ≫ K.d (n + 1) (n + 2)))
307	
308	include hτ in
309	lemma twistedBiprodD_comp (n : ℕ) :
310	    twistedBiprodD τ n ≫ twistedBiprodD τ (n + 1) = 0 := by
311	  apply biprod.hom_ext
312	  · simp only [Category.assoc, twistedBiprodD_fst, Preadditive.comp_add,
313	      twistedBiprodD_fst_assoc, twistedBiprodD_snd_assoc, zero_comp]
314	    rw [Preadditive.add_comp, Category.assoc, Category.assoc, HomologicalComplex.d_comp_d,
315	      comp_zero, zero_add, ← Preadditive.comp_add, hτ n, add_neg_cancel, comp_zero]
316	  · simp only [Category.assoc, twistedBiprodD_snd, twistedBiprodD_snd_assoc, zero_comp]
317	    rw [HomologicalComplex.d_comp_d, comp_zero]
318	
319	/-- The twisted biproduct cochain complex with differential `[[d_K, τ], [0, d_L]]`. -/
320	noncomputable def twistedBiprod : CochainComplex 𝒜 ℕ :=
321	  CochainComplex.of (fun n => K.X n ⊞ L.X n) (twistedBiprodD τ) (twistedBiprodD_comp τ hτ)
322	
323	@[simp]
324	lemma twistedBiprod_X (n : ℕ) : (twistedBiprod τ hτ).X n = (K.X n ⊞ L.X n) := rfl
325	
326	@[simp]
327	lemma twistedBiprod_d (n : ℕ) : (twistedBiprod τ hτ).d n (n + 1) = twistedBiprodD τ n :=
328	  CochainComplex.of_d _ _ _ _
329	
330	/-- The coprojection `K ⟶ twistedBiprod τ hτ`, degreewise `biprod.inl`. -/
331	noncomputable def twistedBiprodInl : K ⟶ twistedBiprod τ hτ where
332	  f n := biprod.inl
333	  comm' i j hij := by
334	    obtain rfl : i + 1 = j := hij
335	    simp only [twistedBiprod_d]
336	    apply biprod.hom_ext <;> simp
337	
338	/-- The projection `twistedBiprod τ hτ ⟶ L`, degreewise `biprod.snd`. -/
339	noncomputable def twistedBiprodSnd : twistedBiprod τ hτ ⟶ L where
340	  f n := biprod.snd
341	  comm' i j hij := by
342	    obtain rfl : i + 1 = j := hij
343	    simp [twistedBiprod_d]
344	
345	@[simp]
346	lemma twistedBiprodInl_f (n : ℕ) : (twistedBiprodInl τ hτ).f n = biprod.inl := rfl
347	
348	@[simp]
349	lemma twistedBiprodSnd_f (n : ℕ) : (twistedBiprodSnd τ hτ).f n = biprod.snd := rfl
350	
351	lemma twistedBiprodInl_comp_Snd : twistedBiprodInl τ hτ ≫ twistedBiprodSnd τ hτ = 0 := by
352	  ext n
353	  simp
354	
355	/-- Each degree of `0 → K → twistedBiprod → L → 0` is the canonical split short exact sequence of
356	the biproduct `K.X n ⊞ L.X n`. -/
357	noncomputable def twistedBiprodSplitting (n : ℕ) :
358	    ((ShortComplex.mk _ _ (twistedBiprodInl_comp_Snd τ hτ)).map
359	      (HomologicalComplex.eval 𝒜 (ComplexShape.up ℕ) n)).Splitting where
360	  r := biprod.fst
361	  s := biprod.inr
362	  id := by simpa using biprod.total
363	
364	end TwistedBiprod
365	
366	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the horseshoe twist family
367	
368	Given a short exact sequence `ses : 0 → A → B → C → 0` and chosen injective resolutions
369	`I_A`, `I_C`, this block constructs the off-diagonal twist family
370	`τ n : I_C.X n ⟶ I_A.X (n+1)` together with the augmentation first component, satisfying the
371	cocycle identity `d_C n ≫ τ (n+1) = -(τ n ≫ d_A (n+1))`. Each `τ` is produced by the universal
372	lifting property of injectives (`Injective.factorThru` / `ShortComplex.Exact.descToInjective`)
373	against the cosyzygy monomorphisms of `I_C`. This is the blueprint sub-lemma `lem:horseshoe_twist`
374	(the recursion kernel). Combined with the `twistedBiprod` construction above it yields the middle
375	complex of the dual Horseshoe Lemma. -/
376	
377	namespace InjectiveResolution
378	
379	section OfShortExact
380	
381	variable {ses : ShortComplex 𝒜} (hses : ses.ShortExact)
382	  (I_A : InjectiveResolution ses.X₁) (I_C : InjectiveResolution ses.X₃)
383	
384	/-- First component `B ⟶ I_A^0` of the horseshoe augmentation: the injective extension of the
385	augmentation `A ⟶ I_A^0` along the monomorphism `A ↪ B`. -/
386	noncomputable def horseshoeβ₁ : ses.X₂ ⟶ I_A.cocomplex.X 0 :=
387	  @Injective.factorThru _ _ _ _ _ (I_A.injective 0) (I_A.ι.f 0) ses.f hses.mono_f
388	
389	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
390	lemma f_comp_horseshoeβ₁ : ses.f ≫ horseshoeβ₁ hses I_A = I_A.ι.f 0 :=
391	  @Injective.comp_factorThru _ _ _ _ _ (I_A.injective 0) (I_A.ι.f 0) ses.f hses.mono_f
392	
393	/-- Auxiliary map `C ⟶ I_A^1` through which `β₁ ≫ d_A^0` factors (since it kills `A`). -/
394	noncomputable def horseshoeH : ses.X₃ ⟶ I_A.cocomplex.X 1 :=
395	  hses.exact.descToInjective (horseshoeβ₁ hses I_A ≫ I_A.cocomplex.d 0 1) (by
396	    rw [← Category.assoc, f_comp_horseshoeβ₁]; exact I_A.ι_f_zero_comp_complex_d)
397	
398	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
399	lemma g_comp_horseshoeH :
400	    ses.g ≫ horseshoeH hses I_A = horseshoeβ₁ hses I_A ≫ I_A.cocomplex.d 0 1 :=
401	  hses.exact.comp_descToInjective _ _
402	
403	lemma horseshoeH_comp_d : horseshoeH hses I_A ≫ I_A.cocomplex.d 1 2 = 0 := by
404	  haveI := hses.epi_g
405	  rw [← cancel_epi ses.g, comp_zero, ← Category.assoc, g_comp_horseshoeH, Category.assoc,
406	    HomologicalComplex.d_comp_d, comp_zero]
407	
408	/-- The base twist `τ⁰ : I_C^0 ⟶ I_A^1`, extending `-(C ⟶ I_A^1)` along `C ↪ I_C^0`. -/
409	noncomputable def horseshoeτZero : I_C.cocomplex.X 0 ⟶ I_A.cocomplex.X 1 :=
410	  @Injective.factorThru _ _ _ _ _ (I_A.injective 1) (-horseshoeH hses I_A) (I_C.ι.f 0)
411	    (mono_of_isLimit_fork I_C.isLimitKernelFork)
412	
413	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
414	lemma ιC_comp_horseshoeτZero :
415	    I_C.ι.f 0 ≫ horseshoeτZero hses I_A I_C = -horseshoeH hses I_A :=
416	  @Injective.comp_factorThru _ _ _ _ _ (I_A.injective 1) (-horseshoeH hses I_A) (I_C.ι.f 0)
417	    (mono_of_isLimit_fork I_C.isLimitKernelFork)
418	
419	lemma horseshoeτZero_hf :
420	    I_C.ι.f 0 ≫ (-(horseshoeτZero hses I_A I_C ≫ I_A.cocomplex.d 1 2)) = 0 := by
421	  have e : (-horseshoeH hses I_A) ≫ I_A.cocomplex.d 1 2 = 0 := by
422	    rw [Preadditive.neg_comp, horseshoeH_comp_d, neg_zero]
423	  rw [Preadditive.comp_neg, neg_eq_zero, ← Category.assoc, ιC_comp_horseshoeτZero]
424	  exact e
425	
426	/-- The recursion carrying, at each degree `n`, consecutive twists `τⁿ`, `τⁿ⁺¹` together with the
427	cocycle identity `d_C^n ≫ τⁿ⁺¹ = -(τⁿ ≫ d_A^{n+1})`. The step uses the lifting property of the
428	injective `I_A^{n+3}` against the cosyzygy mono of `I_C` (`exact_succ`); the base uses the
429	augmentation exactness `exact₀`. -/
430	noncomputable def twistPair : (n : ℕ) →
431	    Σ' (t0 : I_C.cocomplex.X n ⟶ I_A.cocomplex.X (n + 1))
432	       (_t1 : I_C.cocomplex.X (n + 1) ⟶ I_A.cocomplex.X (n + 2)),
433	       I_C.cocomplex.d n (n + 1) ≫ _t1 = -(t0 ≫ I_A.cocomplex.d (n + 1) (n + 2))
434	  | 0 => ⟨horseshoeτZero hses I_A I_C,
435	      I_C.exact₀.descToInjective (-(horseshoeτZero hses I_A I_C ≫ I_A.cocomplex.d 1 2))
436	        (horseshoeτZero_hf hses I_A I_C),
437	      I_C.exact₀.comp_descToInjective _ _⟩
438	  | (n + 1) =>
439	      let p := twistPair n
440	      ⟨p.2.1,
441	        (I_C.exact_succ n).descToInjective (-(p.2.1 ≫ I_A.cocomplex.d (n + 2) (n + 3))) (by
442	          have e : (-(p.1 ≫ I_A.cocomplex.d (n + 1) (n + 2))) ≫ I_A.cocomplex.d (n + 2) (n + 3)
443	              = 0 := by
444	            rw [Preadditive.neg_comp, Category.assoc, HomologicalComplex.d_comp_d, comp_zero,
445	              neg_zero]
446	          rw [Preadditive.comp_neg, neg_eq_zero, ← Category.assoc, p.2.2]
447	          exact e),
448	        (I_C.exact_succ n).comp_descToInjective _ _⟩
449	
450	/-- The horseshoe off-diagonal twist family `τⁿ : I_C^n ⟶ I_A^{n+1}`. -/
451	noncomputable def horseshoeτ (n : ℕ) : I_C.cocomplex.X n ⟶ I_A.cocomplex.X (n + 1) :=
452	  (twistPair hses I_A I_C n).1
453	
454	/-- The cocycle identity for the horseshoe twist: `d_C^n ≫ τⁿ⁺¹ = -(τⁿ ≫ d_A^{n+1})`. -/
455	lemma horseshoeτ_cocycle (n : ℕ) :
456	    I_C.cocomplex.d n (n + 1) ≫ horseshoeτ hses I_A I_C (n + 1) =
457	      -(horseshoeτ hses I_A I_C n ≫ I_A.cocomplex.d (n + 1) (n + 2)) :=
458	  (twistPair hses I_A I_C n).2.2
459	
460	/-- The middle cochain complex `I_B` of the horseshoe: the twisted biproduct of `I_A` and `I_C`
461	along the horseshoe twist family. -/
462	noncomputable def horseshoeMid : CochainComplex 𝒜 ℕ :=
463	  twistedBiprod (horseshoeτ hses I_A I_C) (horseshoeτ_cocycle hses I_A I_C)
464	
465	/-- The short complex `0 → I_A → I_B → I_C → 0` of the horseshoe, with the coprojection and
466	projection chain maps. -/
467	noncomputable def horseshoeSES : ShortComplex (CochainComplex 𝒜 ℕ) :=
468	  ShortComplex.mk _ _
469	    (twistedBiprodInl_comp_Snd (horseshoeτ hses I_A I_C) (horseshoeτ_cocycle hses I_A I_C))
470	
471	/-- Each degree of the horseshoe short complex is the canonical biproduct splitting. -/
472	noncomputable def horseshoeSES_splitting (n : ℕ) :
473	    ((horseshoeSES hses I_A I_C).map
474	      (HomologicalComplex.eval 𝒜 (ComplexShape.up ℕ) n)).Splitting :=
475	  twistedBiprodSplitting (horseshoeτ hses I_A I_C) (horseshoeτ_cocycle hses I_A I_C) n
476	
477	/-- The horseshoe short complex `0 → I_A → I_B → I_C → 0` is short exact (degreewise split). -/
478	lemma horseshoeSES_shortExact : (horseshoeSES hses I_A I_C).ShortExact :=
479	  shortExact_of_degreewise_splitting (horseshoeSES_splitting hses I_A I_C)
480	
481	@[simp]
482	lemma horseshoeτ_zero : horseshoeτ hses I_A I_C 0 = horseshoeτZero hses I_A I_C := rfl
483	
484	/-- Clean-domain degree-0 augmentation map of `I_C` (definitionally `I_C.ι.f 0`, but with syntactic
485	domain `ses.X₃` so it composes cleanly on the left with `ses.g`; the bundled `I_C.ι.f 0` carries the
486	single-complex domain `((single₀).obj ses.X₃).X 0`, which blocks rewriting under `ses.g ≫ -`). -/
487	noncomputable def ιC0 : ses.X₃ ⟶ I_C.cocomplex.X 0 := I_C.ι.f 0
488	
489	lemma ιC0_comp_d : ιC0 I_C ≫ I_C.cocomplex.d 0 1 = 0 := I_C.ι_f_zero_comp_complex_d
490	
491	lemma ιC0_comp_τZero :
492	    ιC0 I_C ≫ horseshoeτZero hses I_A I_C = -horseshoeH hses I_A :=
493	  ιC_comp_horseshoeτZero hses I_A I_C
494	
495	/-- The horseshoe augmentation `β : B ⟶ I_A^0 ⊞ I_C^0`. -/
496	noncomputable def horseshoeβ : ses.X₂ ⟶ I_A.cocomplex.X 0 ⊞ I_C.cocomplex.X 0 :=
497	  biprod.lift (horseshoeβ₁ hses I_A) (ses.g ≫ ιC0 I_C)
498	
499	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
500	lemma horseshoeβ_fst : horseshoeβ hses I_A I_C ≫ biprod.fst = horseshoeβ₁ hses I_A := by
501	  rw [horseshoeβ, biprod.lift_fst]
502	
503	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
504	lemma horseshoeβ_snd : horseshoeβ hses I_A I_C ≫ biprod.snd = ses.g ≫ ιC0 I_C := by
505	  rw [horseshoeβ, biprod.lift_snd]
506	
507	/-- The augmentation composes to zero with the first horseshoe differential, so it descends to a
508	chain map from `B` (in degree 0) into the middle complex. -/
509	lemma horseshoeβ_comp_d :
510	    horseshoeβ hses I_A I_C ≫ twistedBiprodD (horseshoeτ hses I_A I_C) 0 = 0 := by
511	  have e : ses.g ≫ ιC0 I_C ≫ horseshoeτZero hses I_A I_C
512	      = -(horseshoeβ₁ hses I_A ≫ I_A.cocomplex.d 0 (0 + 1)) := by
513	    rw [ιC0_comp_τZero, Preadditive.comp_neg, g_comp_horseshoeH]
514	  have e2 : ses.g ≫ ιC0 I_C ≫ I_C.cocomplex.d 0 (0 + 1) = 0 := by
515	    have h0 : ιC0 I_C ≫ I_C.cocomplex.d 0 (0 + 1) = 0 := I_C.ι_f_zero_comp_complex_d
516	    rw [h0, comp_zero]
517	  apply biprod.hom_ext
518	  · simp only [Category.assoc, twistedBiprodD_fst, Preadditive.comp_add,
519	      horseshoeβ_fst_assoc, horseshoeβ_snd_assoc, horseshoeτ_zero, zero_comp]
520	    rw [e, add_neg_cancel]
521	  · simp only [Category.assoc, twistedBiprodD_snd, horseshoeβ_snd_assoc, zero_comp]
522	    exact e2
523	
524	/-- Maps out of a `single₀` cochain complex are determined by their degree-`0` component. -/
525	private lemma single₀_hom_ext {X : 𝒜} {D : CochainComplex 𝒜 ℕ}
526	    {g h : (CochainComplex.single₀ 𝒜).obj X ⟶ D} (h0 : g.f 0 = h.f 0) : g = h := by
527	  apply (CochainComplex.fromSingle₀Equiv D X).injective
528	  ext
529	  exact h0
530	
531	/-- The horseshoe augmentation packaged as a chain map `(single₀ B) ⟶ I_B`, with degree-`0`
532	component the augmentation `β : B ⟶ I_A^0 ⊞ I_C^0`. -/
533	noncomputable def horseshoeι :
534	    (CochainComplex.single₀ 𝒜).obj ses.X₂ ⟶ horseshoeMid hses I_A I_C :=
535	  (CochainComplex.fromSingle₀Equiv (horseshoeMid hses I_A I_C) ses.X₂).symm
536	    ⟨horseshoeβ hses I_A I_C, by
537	      show horseshoeβ hses I_A I_C ≫
538	          (twistedBiprod (horseshoeτ hses I_A I_C) (horseshoeτ_cocycle hses I_A I_C)).d 0 1 = 0
539	      rw [twistedBiprod_d]
540	      exact horseshoeβ_comp_d hses I_A I_C⟩
541	
542	@[simp]
543	lemma horseshoeι_f_zero : (horseshoeι hses I_A I_C).f 0 = horseshoeβ hses I_A I_C := by
544	  simp [horseshoeι, CochainComplex.fromSingle₀Equiv]
545	
546	/-- The augmentation `β : B ⟶ I_A^0 ⊞ I_C^0` is a monomorphism (the base stage of the horseshoe
547	recursion: `mono_biprod_lift_factorThru_of_exact` applied to the original short exact sequence). -/
548	lemma mono_horseshoeβ : Mono (horseshoeβ hses I_A I_C) := by
549	  haveI := hses.mono_f
550	  haveI : Injective (I_A.cocomplex.X 0) := I_A.injective 0
551	  -- The domain of `I_A.ι.f 0` is `((single₀).obj ses.X₁).X 0`, not syntactically `ses.X₁`; we
552	  -- ascribe the clean domain so the `Mono` instance matches the lemma's `α : S.X₁ ⟶ P`.
553	  haveI : Mono (show ses.X₁ ⟶ I_A.cocomplex.X 0 from I_A.ι.f 0) :=
554	    mono_of_isLimit_fork I_A.isLimitKernelFork
555	  haveI : Mono (show ses.X₃ ⟶ I_C.cocomplex.X 0 from I_C.ι.f 0) :=
556	    mono_of_isLimit_fork I_C.isLimitKernelFork
557	  exact mono_biprod_lift_factorThru_of_exact hses.exact
558	    (show ses.X₁ ⟶ I_A.cocomplex.X 0 from I_A.ι.f 0)
559	    (show ses.X₃ ⟶ I_C.cocomplex.X 0 from I_C.ι.f 0)
560	
561	/-- The horseshoe left square: `I_A.ι ≫ (I_A → I_B) = (single₀ A → single₀ B) ≫ horseshoeι`. -/
562	lemma horseshoeφ_comm₁₂ :
563	    I_A.ι ≫ (horseshoeSES hses I_A I_C).f =
564	      (ses.map (CochainComplex.single₀ 𝒜)).f ≫ horseshoeι hses I_A I_C := by
565	  apply single₀_hom_ext
566	  change (I_A.ι ≫ twistedBiprodInl (horseshoeτ hses I_A I_C)
567	      (horseshoeτ_cocycle hses I_A I_C)).f 0 =
568	    ((CochainComplex.single₀ 𝒜).map ses.f ≫ horseshoeι hses I_A I_C).f 0
569	  rw [HomologicalComplex.comp_f, HomologicalComplex.comp_f, twistedBiprodInl_f,
570	    CochainComplex.single₀_map_f_zero, horseshoeι_f_zero, horseshoeβ]
571	  -- The biproduct projections from `twistedBiprod` carry a `(twistedBiprod).X 0`-flavoured
572	  -- domain; ascribe the clean biproduct so the `biprod.*` simp lemmas fire.
573	  change I_A.ι.f 0 ≫ (biprod.inl : I_A.cocomplex.X 0 ⟶ I_A.cocomplex.X 0 ⊞ I_C.cocomplex.X 0) =
574	    ses.f ≫ biprod.lift (horseshoeβ₁ hses I_A) (ses.g ≫ ιC0 I_C)
575	  apply biprod.hom_ext <;> simp [f_comp_horseshoeβ₁, reassoc_of% ses.zero]
576	
577	/-- The horseshoe right square: `horseshoeι ≫ (I_B → I_C) = (single₀ B → single₀ C) ≫ I_C.ι`. -/
578	lemma horseshoeφ_comm₂₃ :
579	    horseshoeι hses I_A I_C ≫ (horseshoeSES hses I_A I_C).g =
580	      (ses.map (CochainComplex.single₀ 𝒜)).g ≫ I_C.ι := by
581	  apply single₀_hom_ext
582	  change (horseshoeι hses I_A I_C ≫
583	      twistedBiprodSnd (horseshoeτ hses I_A I_C) (horseshoeτ_cocycle hses I_A I_C)).f 0 =
584	    ((CochainComplex.single₀ 𝒜).map ses.g ≫ I_C.ι).f 0
585	  rw [HomologicalComplex.comp_f, HomologicalComplex.comp_f, twistedBiprodSnd_f,
586	    CochainComplex.single₀_map_f_zero, horseshoeι_f_zero, horseshoeβ]
587	  change biprod.lift (horseshoeβ₁ hses I_A) (ses.g ≫ ιC0 I_C) ≫
588	    (biprod.snd : I_A.cocomplex.X 0 ⊞ I_C.cocomplex.X 0 ⟶ I_C.cocomplex.X 0) = ses.g ≫ I_C.ι.f 0
589	  rw [biprod.lift_snd]
590	  rfl
591	
592	/-- The morphism of short complexes of cochain complexes
593	`(single₀ A → single₀ B → single₀ C) ⟶ (I_A → I_B → I_C)` whose outer verticals are the
594	resolution augmentations `I_A.ι`, `I_C.ι` and whose middle vertical is the horseshoe augmentation
595	`horseshoeι`. -/
596	noncomputable def horseshoeφ :
597	    ses.map (CochainComplex.single₀ 𝒜) ⟶ horseshoeSES hses I_A I_C :=
598	  ShortComplex.homMk I_A.ι (horseshoeι hses I_A I_C) I_C.ι
599	    (horseshoeφ_comm₁₂ hses I_A I_C) (horseshoeφ_comm₂₃ hses I_A I_C)
600	
601	@[simp] lemma horseshoeφ_τ₁ : (horseshoeφ hses I_A I_C).τ₁ = I_A.ι := rfl
602	@[simp] lemma horseshoeφ_τ₂ : (horseshoeφ hses I_A I_C).τ₂ = horseshoeι hses I_A I_C := rfl
603	@[simp] lemma horseshoeφ_τ₃ : (horseshoeφ hses I_A I_C).τ₃ = I_C.ι := rfl
604	
605	/-- The horseshoe augmentation `(single₀ B) ⟶ I_B` is a quasi-isomorphism: by the middle-term
606	quasi-isomorphism transfer `quasiIso_τ₂`, since the outer augmentations `I_A.ι`, `I_C.ι` are
607	quasi-isomorphisms (they are injective resolutions) and `β` is mono. -/
608	lemma quasiIso_horseshoeι : QuasiIso (horseshoeι hses I_A I_C) := by
609	  have key := HomologicalComplex.HomologySequence.quasiIso_τ₂ (horseshoeφ hses I_A I_C)
610	    (hses.map_of_exact (CochainComplex.single₀ 𝒜))
611	    (horseshoeSES_shortExact hses I_A I_C)
612	    (h₁ := I_A.quasiIso) (h₃ := I_C.quasiIso)
613	    (hbMono := by
614	      intro i hi
615	      obtain rfl : i = 0 := by
616	        rcases i with _ | m
617	        · rfl
618	        · exact absurd rfl (hi m)
619	      simpa only [horseshoeφ_τ₂, horseshoeι_f_zero] using mono_horseshoeβ hses I_A I_C)
620	    (hbEpi := by
621	      intro i hi
622	      exact absurd rfl (hi (i + 1)))
623	  simpa only [horseshoeφ_τ₂] using key
624	
625	/-- **The middle complex resolves `B`** (blueprint `lem:horseshoe_resolvesMiddle`). The horseshoe
626	middle complex `I_B = twistedBiprod I_A I_C`, with augmentation `β`, is an injective resolution of
627	`B = ses.X₂`: its terms `I_A^n ⊞ I_C^n` are injective, and the augmentation is a quasi-isomorphism
628	by `quasiIso_horseshoeι`. -/
629	noncomputable def ofShortExact_resolvesMiddle : InjectiveResolution ses.X₂ where
630	  cocomplex := horseshoeMid hses I_A I_C
631	  injective n := by
632	    haveI : Injective (I_A.cocomplex.X n) := I_A.injective n
633	    haveI : Injective (I_C.cocomplex.X n) := I_C.injective n
634	    exact (inferInstance : Injective (I_A.cocomplex.X n ⊞ I_C.cocomplex.X n))
635	  ι := horseshoeι hses I_A I_C
636	  quasiIso := quasiIso_horseshoeι hses I_A I_C
637	
638	/-- **Dual Horseshoe Lemma** (blueprint `lem:injective_resolution_of_ses`). From a short exact
639	sequence `0 → A → B → C → 0` and chosen injective resolutions `I_A`, `I_C`, the horseshoe produces
640	an injective resolution of the middle term `B` fitting into the degreewise-split short exact
641	sequence of cochain complexes `0 → I_A → I_B → I_C → 0` (`horseshoeSES`, short exact by
642	`horseshoeSES_shortExact` and degreewise split by `horseshoeSES_splitting`). -/
643	noncomputable def ofShortExact : InjectiveResolution ses.X₂ :=
644	  ofShortExact_resolvesMiddle hses I_A I_C
645	
646	end OfShortExact
647	
648	end InjectiveResolution
649	
650	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the dimension-shift isomorphism -/
651	
652	/-- **Dimension shift across an acyclic short exact sequence** (blueprint
653	`lem:acyclic_dimension_shift`, TARGET 2). Given an additive functor `G` and a short exact sequence
654	`0 → A → J → Z → 0` with middle term `J` right-`G`-acyclic, the connecting maps of the long exact
655	sequence of right-derived functors furnish isomorphisms
656	`(R^{k+1} G)(Z) ≅ (R^{k+2} G)(A)` for all `k`.
657	
658	The proof feeds the dual Horseshoe Lemma (`InjectiveResolution.ofShortExact_resolvesMiddle`) — a
659	degreewise-split short exact sequence of injective resolutions `0 → I_A → I_B → I_C → 0` — into the
660	resolution-level dimension shift `rightDerivedShiftIsoOfSplitResolutionSES`. -/
661	noncomputable def Functor.rightDerivedShiftIsoOfAcyclic
662	    (G : 𝒜 ⥤ ℬ) [G.Additive] {ses : ShortComplex 𝒜} (hses : ses.ShortExact)
663	    [G.IsRightAcyclic ses.X₂] (k : ℕ) :
664	    (G.rightDerived (k + 1)).obj ses.X₃ ≅ (G.rightDerived (k + 2)).obj ses.X₁ :=
665	  let I_A : InjectiveResolution ses.X₁ := (inferInstance : HasInjectiveResolution ses.X₁).out.some
666	  let I_C : InjectiveResolution ses.X₃ := (inferInstance : HasInjectiveResolution ses.X₃).out.some
667	  G.rightDerivedShiftIsoOfSplitResolutionSES I_A
668	    (InjectiveResolution.ofShortExact_resolvesMiddle hses I_A I_C) I_C
669	    (InjectiveResolution.horseshoeSES hses I_A I_C).f
670	    (InjectiveResolution.horseshoeSES hses I_A I_C).g
671	    (InjectiveResolution.horseshoeSES hses I_A I_C).zero
672	    (InjectiveResolution.horseshoeSES_splitting hses I_A I_C) k
673	
674	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — lowest-degree cokernel description -/
675	
676	/-- **Lowest-degree cokernel description across an acyclic short exact sequence** (blueprint
677	`lem:acyclic_one_iso_coker`, leaf of TARGET 3). For an additive left-exact functor `G` and a short
678	exact sequence `0 → A → J → Z → 0` with middle term `J` right-`G`-acyclic, the first right-derived
679	functor at `A` is the cokernel of `G` applied to the surjection `J ↠ Z`:
680	`(R¹ G)(A) ≅ coker(G(J) → G(Z))`.
681	
682	The proof reads the bottom of the homology long exact sequence of the horseshoe-lifted,
683	degreewise-split short exact sequence of injective resolutions `0 → I_A → I_J → I_Z → 0` after
684	applying `G`. There `δ⁰ : H⁰(GI_Z) → H¹(GI_A)` is epi (because `H¹(GI_J) = 0` by acyclicity of `J`)
685	and exact after `homologyMap(Gψ) 0 : H⁰(GI_J) → H⁰(GI_Z)`, so `δ⁰` exhibits `H¹(GI_A) ≅ (R¹G)(A)`
686	as the cokernel of `homologyMap(Gψ) 0`. The naturality of `isoRightDerivedObj` (lifting `ses.g` to
687	`ψ`) and of `rightDerivedZeroIsoSelf` (`R⁰G ≅ G`) identify `homologyMap(Gψ) 0` with `G.map ses.g`.
688	-/
689	noncomputable def Functor.rightDerivedOneIsoCokerOfAcyclic
690	    (G : 𝒜 ⥤ ℬ) [G.Additive] [PreservesFiniteLimits G]
691	    {ses : ShortComplex 𝒜} (hses : ses.ShortExact) [G.IsRightAcyclic ses.X₂] :
692	    (G.rightDerived 1).obj ses.X₁ ≅ cokernel (G.map ses.g) := by
693	  classical
694	  let I_A : InjectiveResolution ses.X₁ := (inferInstance : HasInjectiveResolution ses.X₁).out.some
695	  let I_C : InjectiveResolution ses.X₃ := (inferInstance : HasInjectiveResolution ses.X₃).out.some
696	  let I_J : InjectiveResolution ses.X₂ :=
697	    InjectiveResolution.ofShortExact_resolvesMiddle hses I_A I_C
698	  let ψ : I_J.cocomplex ⟶ I_C.cocomplex := (InjectiveResolution.horseshoeSES hses I_A I_C).g
699	  have hSG : ((InjectiveResolution.horseshoeSES hses I_A I_C).map
700	      (G.mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ))).ShortExact :=
701	    shortExact_map_mapHomologicalComplex_of_degreewise_splitting
702	      (InjectiveResolution.horseshoeSES_splitting hses I_A I_C) G
703	  haveI hepi : Epi (hSG.δ 0 1 (by simp)) :=
704	    hSG.epi_δ 0 1 (by simp)
705	      (G.isZero_homology_mapHomologicalComplex_of_isRightAcyclic I_J 0)
706	  have hex := hSG.homology_exact₃ 0 1 (by simp)
707	  have hcok := hex.gIsCokernel
708	  have comm : I_J.ι.f 0 ≫ ψ.f 0 = ses.g ≫ I_C.ι.f 0 := by
709	    have h0 := congrArg (fun m => HomologicalComplex.Hom.f m 0)
710	      (InjectiveResolution.horseshoeφ_comm₂₃ hses I_A I_C)
711	    simp only [HomologicalComplex.comp_f, ShortComplex.map_g,
712	      CochainComplex.single₀_map_f_zero] at h0
713	    exact h0
714	  refine (I_A.isoRightDerivedObj G 1) ≪≫
715	    (IsColimit.coconePointUniqueUpToIso
716	      (cokernelIsCokernel (HomologicalComplex.homologyMap
717	        ((InjectiveResolution.horseshoeSES hses I_A I_C).map
718	          (G.mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ))).g 0)) hcok).symm ≪≫
719	    (cokernel.mapIso _ ((G.rightDerived 0).map ses.g)
720	      (I_J.isoRightDerivedObj G 0).symm (I_C.isoRightDerivedObj G 0).symm ?_) ≪≫
721	    cokernel.mapIso _ (G.map ses.g)
722	      ((G.rightDerivedZeroIsoSelf).app ses.X₂) ((G.rightDerivedZeroIsoSelf).app ses.X₃)
723	      ((G.rightDerivedZeroIsoSelf).hom.naturality ses.g)
724	  rw [Iso.symm_hom, Iso.symm_hom, Iso.comp_inv_eq, Category.assoc, Iso.eq_inv_comp]
725	  exact (InjectiveResolution.isoRightDerivedObj_hom_naturality ses.g I_J I_C ψ comm G 0).symm
726	
727	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — cosyzygy short exact sequences
728	
729	For an exact cochain complex `K` (the augmentation-dropped resolution `J⁰ → J¹ → ⋯`), the
730	cosyzygies `Zⁿ := K.cycles n = ker(dⁿ)` fit into short exact sequences
731	`(Sₙ) : 0 → Zⁿ → Kⁿ → Zⁿ⁺¹ → 0`, where the inclusion is `iCycles` and the surjection is the
732	corestriction `toCycles` of the differential onto the next cycles. These are the sequences cut
733	out of the resolution that drive the dimension-shift staircase in the comparison theorem
734	`rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution` (blueprint `lem:cosyzygy_ses`). The construction is purely
735	abelian-categorical; the right-`G`-acyclicity of the middle terms `Kⁿ` is carried as a typeclass
736	hypothesis only at the point of use. These declarations are not in Mathlib. -/
737	
738	section Cosyzygy
739	
740	omit [HasInjectiveResolutions 𝒜]
741	
742	/-- The cosyzygy composite `Zⁿ ↪ Kⁿ ↠ Zⁿ⁺¹` vanishes: the cycles inclusion lands in the kernel
743	of the differential, hence in the kernel of its corestriction `toCycles`. -/
744	theorem cosyzygy_iCycles_comp_toCycles (K : CochainComplex 𝒜 ℕ) (n : ℕ) :
745	    K.iCycles n ≫ K.toCycles n (n + 1) = 0 := by
746	  rw [← cancel_mono (K.iCycles (n + 1)), Category.assoc, HomologicalComplex.toCycles_i,
747	    HomologicalComplex.iCycles_d, zero_comp]
748	
749	/-- If the complex `K` is exact at `n + 1`, the corestriction `toCycles n (n+1) : Kⁿ → Zⁿ⁺¹` is an
750	epimorphism: its cokernel is the homology `Hⁿ⁺¹(K)`, which vanishes by exactness. -/
751	theorem epi_toCycles_of_exactAt (K : CochainComplex 𝒜 ℕ) (n : ℕ) (h : K.ExactAt (n + 1)) :
752	    Epi (K.toCycles n (n + 1)) := by
753	  rw [HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff_isZero_homology] at h
754	  have hcok := K.homologyIsCokernel n (n + 1) (by simp)
755	  rw [Preadditive.epi_iff_cancel_zero]
756	  intro T g hg
757	  set d := hcok.desc (CokernelCofork.ofπ g (by simpa using hg)) with hdd
758	  have hd : K.homologyπ (n + 1) ≫ d = g := hcok.fac _ Limits.WalkingParallelPair.one
759	  have hz : K.homologyπ (n + 1) = 0 := h.eq_of_tgt _ _
760	  rw [← hd, hz]; exact zero_comp
761	
762	/-- The cycles inclusion `iCycles n` is the kernel of the corestriction `toCycles n (n+1)`: both
763	`d n (n+1)` and `toCycles n (n+1)` have the same kernel, since `d = toCycles ≫ (mono iCycles)`. -/
764	noncomputable def cosyzygyKernelFork (K : CochainComplex 𝒜 ℕ) (n : ℕ) :
765	    Limits.IsLimit
766	      (Limits.KernelFork.ofι (K.iCycles n) (cosyzygy_iCycles_comp_toCycles K n)) := by
767	  have hk := K.cyclesIsKernel n (n + 1) (by simp)
768	  have key : ∀ {T : 𝒜} (x : T ⟶ K.X n),
769	      x ≫ K.toCycles n (n + 1) = 0 → x ≫ K.d n (n + 1) = 0 := by
770	    intro T x hx
771	    rw [← K.toCycles_i n (n + 1), ← Category.assoc, hx, zero_comp]
772	  refine Limits.KernelFork.IsLimit.ofι _ _
773	    (fun {T} x hx => hk.lift (Limits.KernelFork.ofι x (key x hx)))
774	    (fun {T} x hx => hk.fac _ Limits.WalkingParallelPair.zero)
775	    (fun {T} x hx m hmeq => by
776	      have e2 : hk.lift (Limits.KernelFork.ofι x (key x hx)) ≫ K.iCycles n = x :=
777	        hk.fac _ Limits.WalkingParallelPair.zero
778	      rw [← cancel_mono (K.iCycles n)]
779	      exact hmeq.trans e2.symm)
780	
781	/-- The `n`-th cosyzygy short complex `Zⁿ → Kⁿ → Zⁿ⁺¹` (`iCycles` then `toCycles`). -/
782	noncomputable def Functor.cosyzygyShortComplex (K : CochainComplex 𝒜 ℕ) (n : ℕ) :
783	    ShortComplex 𝒜 :=
784	  ShortComplex.mk (K.iCycles n) (K.toCycles n (n + 1)) (cosyzygy_iCycles_comp_toCycles K n)
785	
786	/-- **Cosyzygy short exact sequence** (blueprint `lem:cosyzygy_ses`). For a complex `K` exact at
787	`n + 1`, the cosyzygy short complex `0 → Zⁿ → Kⁿ → Zⁿ⁺¹ → 0` is short exact: the inclusion
788	`iCycles n` is a monomorphism and a kernel of the corestriction `toCycles n (n+1)`, which is an
789	epimorphism by exactness. -/
790	theorem Functor.cosyzygyShortExact (K : CochainComplex 𝒜 ℕ) (n : ℕ)
791	    (h : K.ExactAt (n + 1)) : (Functor.cosyzygyShortComplex K n).ShortExact where
792	  exact := ShortComplex.exact_of_f_is_kernel _ (cosyzygyKernelFork K n)
793	  mono_f := by dsimp [Functor.cosyzygyShortComplex]; infer_instance
794	  epi_g := epi_toCycles_of_exactAt K n h
795	
796	/-- **Applied cosyzygy is the cocycle object** (core of blueprint `lem:applied_cosyzygy_cycles`).
797	A finite-limit-preserving (e.g. left-exact) functor `G` carries the cycles object
798	`Zⁿ = K.cycles n` of a complex to the cycles object of the applied complex `G(K)` — that is, to
799	the `n`-th cocycle object `ker(G(dⁿ) : G(Kⁿ) → G(Kⁿ⁺¹))`. This is the left-exactness
800	identification `G(Zⁿ) ≅ ker(G(Jⁿ) → G(J^{n+1}))`: `G` preserves the kernel
801	`iCycles n` defining the cycles, transported to the canonical cycles kernel of `G(K)`. -/
802	noncomputable def Functor.gCosyzygyIsoCocycles (G : 𝒜 ⥤ ℬ) [G.Additive]
803	    [Limits.PreservesFiniteLimits G] (K : CochainComplex 𝒜 ℕ) (n : ℕ) :
804	    G.obj (K.cycles n) ≅ ((G.mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj K).cycles n :=
805	  Limits.IsLimit.conePointUniqueUpToIso
806	    (Limits.isLimitForkMapOfIsLimit' G (K.iCycles_d n (n + 1))
807	      (K.cyclesIsKernel n (n + 1) (by simp)))
808	    (((G.mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj K).cyclesIsKernel n (n + 1) (by simp))
809	
810	/-- The cycles iso `Functor.gCosyzygyIsoCocycles` is compatible with the cycles inclusions:
811	composing with the cycles inclusion of `G(K)` recovers `G` applied to the cycles inclusion of `K`.
812	This expresses that the iso identifies `G(Zⁿ)`, as a subobject of `G(Kⁿ)`, with the cocycle
813	object. -/
814	@[reassoc]
815	lemma Functor.gCosyzygyIsoCocycles_hom_iCycles (G : 𝒜 ⥤ ℬ) [G.Additive]
816	    [Limits.PreservesFiniteLimits G] (K : CochainComplex 𝒜 ℕ) (n : ℕ) :
817	    (G.gCosyzygyIsoCocycles K n).hom ≫
818	        ((G.mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj K).iCycles n =
819	      G.map (K.iCycles n) :=
820	  Limits.IsLimit.conePointUniqueUpToIso_hom_comp _ _ Limits.WalkingParallelPair.zero
821	
822	/-- Compatibility of `Functor.gCosyzygyIsoCocycles` with the cosyzygy surjections `toCycles`:
823	`G` applied to the corestriction `Jᵐ ↠ Z^{m+1}` followed by the cycles iso is the corestriction of
824	the applied complex `G(K•)`. This is the naturality square that lets the cosyzygy iso transport the
825	cohomology cokernel in `Functor.cohomologyAppliedResolutionIso`. -/
826	lemma Functor.gCosyzygyIsoCocycles_toCycles (G : 𝒜 ⥤ ℬ) [G.Additive]
827	    [Limits.PreservesFiniteLimits G] (K : CochainComplex 𝒜 ℕ) (m : ℕ) :
828	    G.map (K.toCycles m (m + 1)) ≫ (G.gCosyzygyIsoCocycles K (m + 1)).hom =
829	      ((G.mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj K).toCycles m (m + 1) := by
830	  have hL : G.map (K.toCycles m (m + 1)) ≫ G.map (K.iCycles (m + 1)) = G.map (K.d m (m + 1)) := by
831	    rw [← G.map_comp, K.toCycles_i]
832	  have hR := ((G.mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj K).toCycles_i m (m + 1)
833	  rw [← cancel_mono (((G.mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj K).iCycles (m + 1)),
834	    Category.assoc, G.gCosyzygyIsoCocycles_hom_iCycles]
835	  exact hL.trans hR.symm
836	
837	/-- **Cohomology of the applied resolution, positive degrees** (positive-degree case of blueprint
838	`lem:cohomology_of_applied_resolution`). For a finite-limit-preserving `G` and `n = m+1 ≥ 1`, the
839	`n`-th cohomology of the applied complex `G(K•)` is the cokernel of `G` applied to the cosyzygy
840	surjection `J^{n-1} ↠ Zⁿ`:
841	`Hⁿ(G(K•)) ≅ coker(G(J^{n-1}) → G(Zⁿ))`.
842	The homology in degree `n` is the cokernel of the corestriction `(G(K•)).toCycles m (m+1)`
843	(`homologyIsCokernel`); the cosyzygy iso `gCosyzygyIsoCocycles` rewrites that corestriction as
844	`G(Jᵐ ↠ Z^{m+1})` post-composed with an isomorphism (`gCosyzygyIsoCocycles_toCycles`), and a
845	post-composed isomorphism does not change the cokernel (`cokernel.mapIso`). -/
846	noncomputable def Functor.cohomologyAppliedResolutionIso (G : 𝒜 ⥤ ℬ) [G.Additive]
847	    [Limits.PreservesFiniteLimits G] (K : CochainComplex 𝒜 ℕ) (m : ℕ) :
848	    ((G.mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj K).homology (m + 1) ≅
849	      cokernel (G.map (K.toCycles m (m + 1))) :=
850	  have iso2 : cokernel (((G.mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj K).toCycles m (m + 1)) ≅
851	      cokernel (G.map (K.toCycles m (m + 1))) :=
852	    cokernel.mapIso _ (G.map (K.toCycles m (m + 1))) (Iso.refl _)
853	      (G.gCosyzygyIsoCocycles K (m + 1)).symm (by
854	        simp only [Iso.symm_hom, Iso.refl_hom, Iso.comp_inv_eq]
855	        simp [G.gCosyzygyIsoCocycles_toCycles K m])
856	  Limits.IsColimit.coconePointUniqueUpToIso
857	      (((G.mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj K).homologyIsCokernel m (m + 1) (by simp))
858	      (cokernelIsCokernel _) ≪≫ iso2
859	
860	/-- **Degree-zero cohomology of the applied resolution** (degree-`0` case of blueprint
861	`lem:cohomology_of_applied_resolution`). For a finite-limit-preserving `G`, the zeroth cohomology
862	of the applied complex `G(K•)` is `G` of the zeroth cosyzygy `Z⁰ = K.cycles 0`:
863	`H⁰(G(K•)) ≅ G(Z⁰)`. Since the complex starts in degree `0` there is no incoming differential, so
864	`H⁰` coincides with the cocycle object `ker(G(K⁰) → G(K¹))`, which `Functor.gCosyzygyIsoCocycles`
865	identifies with `G(Z⁰)`. Composed with an augmentation iso `A ≅ Z⁰` this gives the blueprint's
866	`H⁰(G(J•)) ≅ G(A)`. -/
867	noncomputable def Functor.gHomologyZeroIso (G : 𝒜 ⥤ ℬ) [G.Additive]
868	    [Limits.PreservesFiniteLimits G] (K : CochainComplex 𝒜 ℕ) :
869	    G.obj (K.cycles 0) ≅ ((G.mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj K).homology 0 :=
870	  G.gCosyzygyIsoCocycles K 0 ≪≫
871	    CochainComplex.isoHomologyπ₀ ((G.mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj K)
872	
873	end Cosyzygy
874	
875	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the acyclic-resolution comparison theorem -/
876	
877	/-- **An acyclic resolution computes the right-derived functor** (blueprint
878	`lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived`, TARGET 3; Stacks Tag 015E, Leray's acyclicity lemma).
879	Let `G` be an additive, finite-limit-preserving (hence left-exact) functor, and let `K` be a cochain
880	complex with every term `K.X n` right-`G`-acyclic, exact in every positive degree
881	(`hexact : ∀ n, K.ExactAt (n+1)`), and resolving `A` (presented as `e : A ≅ K.cycles 0`). Then for
882	every `n` the `n`-th right-derived functor of `G` at `A` is the `n`-th cohomology of the applied
883	complex `G(K•)`:
884	`(Rⁿ G)(A) ≅ Hⁿ(G(K•))`.
885	
886	The proof is the classical dimension shift. *Degree 0:* `R⁰G ≅ G` (`rightDerivedZeroIsoSelf`) and
887	the left-exact identification `H⁰(G K) ≅ G(Z⁰)` (`gHomologyZeroIso`) together with `e`. *Positive
888	degrees `m+1`:* the staircase `stairGen` composes the dimension-shift isomorphisms
889	`rightDerivedShiftIsoOfAcyclic` across the cosyzygy short exact sequences down to
890	`(R¹ G)(Zᵐ)`, which the lowest-degree cokernel description `rightDerivedOneIsoCokerOfAcyclic`
891	identifies with `coker(G(Kᵐ) → G(Zᵐ⁺¹))`, finally matched to `Hᵐ⁺¹(G K)` by
892	`cohomologyAppliedResolutionIso`. -/
893	noncomputable def Functor.rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution
894	    (G : 𝒜 ⥤ ℬ) [G.Additive] [PreservesFiniteLimits G]
895	    (K : CochainComplex 𝒜 ℕ) (A : 𝒜) (e : A ≅ K.cycles 0)
896	    (hexact : ∀ n, K.ExactAt (n + 1)) [∀ n, G.IsRightAcyclic (K.X n)] (n : ℕ) :
897	    (G.rightDerived n).obj A ≅
898	      ((G.mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj K).homology n := by
899	  -- staircase: `(R^{m+1} G)(Zˢ) ≅ (R¹ G)(Z^{s+m})` by iterating the dimension shift `m` times
900	  have stairGen : ∀ (m s : ℕ), (G.rightDerived (m + 1)).obj (K.cycles s) ≅
901	      (G.rightDerived 1).obj (K.cycles (s + m)) := by
902	    intro m
903	    induction m with
904	    | zero => exact fun s => Iso.refl _
905	    | succ m ih =>
906	        intro s
907	        haveI : G.IsRightAcyclic (Functor.cosyzygyShortComplex K s).X₂ :=
908	          inferInstanceAs (G.IsRightAcyclic (K.X s))
909	        exact (G.rightDerivedShiftIsoOfAcyclic (Functor.cosyzygyShortExact K s (hexact s)) m).symm
910	          ≪≫ ih (s + 1) ≪≫
911	          eqToIso (congrArg (fun i => (G.rightDerived 1).obj (K.cycles i))
912	            (by omega : s + 1 + m = s + (m + 1)))
913	  cases n with
914	  | zero =>
915	      exact (G.rightDerivedZeroIsoSelf.app A) ≪≫ G.mapIso e ≪≫ G.gHomologyZeroIso K
916	  | succ m =>
917	      haveI : G.IsRightAcyclic (Functor.cosyzygyShortComplex K m).X₂ :=
918	        inferInstanceAs (G.IsRightAcyclic (K.X m))
919	      exact (G.rightDerived (m + 1)).mapIso e ≪≫ stairGen m 0 ≪≫
920	        eqToIso (congrArg (fun i => (G.rightDerived 1).obj (K.cycles i)) (by omega : 0 + m = m)) ≪≫
921	        G.rightDerivedOneIsoCokerOfAcyclic (Functor.cosyzygyShortExact K m (hexact m)) ≪≫
922	        (G.cohomologyAppliedResolutionIso K m).symm
923	
924	/-! ### P4 complete — `rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution` is proved axiom-clean above. -/
925	
926	end CategoryTheory

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechToCohomology
7	
8	/-!
9	# Serre vanishing on affines (Stacks 02KG) — affine cover-system infrastructure
10	
11	Project-local: builds the *affine cover system* feeding the basis-comparison criterion
12	`cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` (01EO). The basis `B` is the distinguished opens `D(f)`
13	of an affine scheme, and the admissible coverings `Cov` are the standard open covers.
14	
15	This file builds the cover-system infrastructure (Lane 1 of the 02KG decomposition) as far
16	as possible; the quasi-coherent seed `affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh` and the top
17	`affine_serre_vanishing` are handed off to the assembly iteration (they consume Lane 2's
18	`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`).
19	-/
20	
21	universe u
22	
23	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace
24	
25	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
26	
27	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
28	
29	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — distinguished opens closed under finite intersection -/
30	
31	/-- **Distinguished opens are closed under finite intersection** (Stacks 02KG, condition (1)).
32	For a family `s : ι → R` of ring elements and a Čech multi-index `σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι`, the
33	`(p + 1)`-fold intersection of distinguished opens `⨅ₖ D(s_{σ k})` is again a distinguished
34	open, namely `D(∏ₖ s_{σ k})`; hence it lies in the basis of distinguished opens. This discharges
35	the `faces_mem` field of the affine cover system. Project-local: re-export of `basicOpen_sprod`
36	in the membership shape the `BasisCovSystem.faces_mem` field consumes. -/
37	theorem affine_faces_mem {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {ι : Type u} (s : ι → R)
38	    {p : ℕ} (σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι) :
39	    (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)) : (Spec R).Opens)
40	      ∈ Set.range (fun f : R => (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens)) :=
41	  ⟨∏ k, s (σ k), (basicOpen_sprod (p + 1) s σ).symm⟩
42	
43	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — covering-datum bridge to the open-cover Čech form -/
44	
45	/-- **The standard affine open cover realizes the distinguished-open family.** For a spanning
46	family `s : ι → R`, the `i`-th member of the standard affine open cover
47	`affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop s hs` of `Spec R` has open range exactly the distinguished open
48	`D(s_i)`, i.e. `coverOpen 𝒰 i = PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s i)`. This is the open-level half of the
49	covering-datum bridge (`lem:cover_datum_bridge`): funext-rewriting the raw family `c.2 = fun i ↦
50	D(s_i)` to `coverOpen 𝒰` identifies the two section Čech complexes, so the Čech cohomology computed
51	over the raw `CovDatum` and over the `X.OpenCover` agree. Project-local: needed to feed the
52	`X.OpenCover`-shaped `injective_cech_acyclic` into the raw-family `BasisCovSystem` fields. -/
53	theorem coverOpen_affineOpenCoverOfSpan {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {ι : Type u} [Finite ι]
54	    (s : ι → R) (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤) (i : ι) :
55	    coverOpen (Scheme.AffineOpenCover.openCover (Scheme.affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop s hs)) i
56	      = PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s i) := by
57	  unfold coverOpen
58	  change (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (s i))))).opensRange = _
59	  apply TopologicalSpace.Opens.ext
60	  change Set.range (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (s i))))).base = _
61	  rw [Spec.map_base]
62	  exact PrimeSpectrum.localization_away_comap_range (Localization.Away (s i)) (s i)

64	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — injective acyclicity for the standard affine cover -/
65	
66	/-- **Injective Čech-acyclicity for the standard affine cover** (Stacks 02KG, `injective_acyclic`
67	field; Stacks `lemma-injective-trivial-cech`). For a spanning family `s : ι → R` (so the
68	distinguished opens `D(s_i)` cover `Spec R`) and an injective `O_X`-module `I`, the positive-degree
69	section Čech cohomology over the standard cover vanishes:
70	`Ȟ^q(𝒰, I) = 0` for all `q > 0`. Reduces the raw-family `cechCohomology` to the `X.OpenCover` form
71	via `coverOpen_affineOpenCoverOfSpan` and applies `injective_cech_acyclic`. Project-local: this is
72	the affine instantiation of `injective_cech_acyclic` discharging the
73	`BasisCovSystem.injective_acyclic` field for standard covers of the whole affine. -/
74	theorem affine_injective_acyclic {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {ι : Type u} [Finite ι]
75	    (s : ι → R) (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤)
76	    (I : (Spec R).Modules) [Injective I] (q : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q) :
77	    IsZero (cechCohomology (fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s i))
78	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj I) q) := by
79	  have hbridge : (fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s i))
80	      = coverOpen (Scheme.affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop s hs).openCover := by
81	    funext i; exact (coverOpen_affineOpenCoverOfSpan s hs i).symm
82	  haveI : Finite (Scheme.affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop s hs).openCover.I₀ :=
83	    inferInstanceAs (Finite ι)
84	  rw [cechCohomology, hbridge]
85	  exact injective_cech_acyclic (Scheme.affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop s hs).openCover I q hq

87	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the underlying-abelian-sheaf functor preserves colimits
88	
89	The gap-fill `toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits` / `toSheaf_preservesEpimorphisms` (blueprint
90	`lem:toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits`, `lem:to_sheaf_preserves_epi`) — the statement that
91	`SheafOfModules.toSheaf R` (the forgetful functor to abelian sheaves) preserves finite colimits,
92	hence epimorphisms — is the foundational ingredient for `affine_surj_of_vanishing` (and hence for
93	the `surj_of_vanishing` field of `affineCoverSystem`).  Mathlib provides only the limit-side dual
94	`PreservesFiniteLimits (toSheaf R)` (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.Limits`); the
95	colimit side is genuinely absent because epis of sheaves of modules are only *locally* surjective
96	and no factorisation through the right adjoint `forget` can capture it.
97	
98	The build below mirrors Mathlib's `…/ModuleCat/Sheaf/Colimits.lean` construction but routes through
99	the **sheafification square** `PresheafOfModules.sheafificationCompToSheaf (𝟙 R.obj)` (not through
100	`forget`).  Write `L := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 R.obj)`, a left adjoint (counit iso).
101	Step 1: `L ⋙ toSheaf R ≅ toPresheaf R.obj ⋙ presheafToSheaf J Ab` (the square), and the right-hand
102	composite preserves finite colimits (`toPresheaf` does objectwise; `presheafToSheaf` is a left
103	adjoint).  Step 2 (descent): for a finite diagram `F`, `F ≅ (F ⋙ forget R) ⋙ L`; applying the
104	colimit-preserving `L` and then `L ⋙ toSheaf R` to a colimit cocone of `F ⋙ forget R` exhibits
105	`toSheaf R` as preserving the colimit of `F`.  Epi-preservation follows from `WalkingSpan` being
106	finite. -/
107	
108	/-- **The underlying-abelian-sheaf functor preserves finite colimits** (blueprint
109	`lem:toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits`).  The forgetful functor `SheafOfModules.toSheaf R` from
110	sheaves of `R`-modules to the underlying sheaves of abelian groups preserves finite colimits — the
111	right-exact dual of the Mathlib-supplied `PreservesFiniteLimits (toSheaf R)`.  Project-local: the
112	missing colimit side, built through the sheafification square (never through the right adjoint
113	`forget`). -/
114	theorem toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits.{v', u', w'} {C : Type u'} [Category.{w'} C]
115	    {J : GrothendieckTopology C} (R : Sheaf J RingCat.{v'})
116	    [HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{v'}] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{v'}] :
117	    PreservesFiniteColimits (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v'} R) := by
118	  have step1 : PreservesFiniteColimits
119	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 R.obj) ⋙ SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v'} R) :=
120	    preservesFiniteColimits_of_natIso
121	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationCompToSheaf (𝟙 R.obj)).symm
122	  haveI : PreservesColimitsOfSize (PresheafOfModules.sheafification.{v'} (𝟙 R.obj)) :=
123	    (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 R.obj)).leftAdjoint_preservesColimits
124	  constructor
125	  intro K _ _
126	  constructor
127	  intro F
128	  set D := F ⋙ SheafOfModules.forget R with hD
129	  have e : F ≅ D ⋙ PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 R.obj) :=
130	    Functor.isoWhiskerLeft F
131	      (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 R.obj)).counit).symm
132	  have hc : IsColimit (colimit.cocone D) := colimit.isColimit D
133	  have hLc := isColimitOfPreserves (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 R.obj)) hc
134	  have hF := isColimitOfPreserves
135	    (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 R.obj) ⋙ SheafOfModules.toSheaf R) hc
136	  haveI hpres : PreservesColimit (D ⋙ PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 R.obj))
137	      (SheafOfModules.toSheaf R) :=
138	    preservesColimit_of_preserves_colimit_cocone hLc hF
139	  exact preservesColimit_of_iso_diagram (SheafOfModules.toSheaf R) e.symm
140	
141	/-- **The underlying-abelian-sheaf functor preserves epimorphisms** (blueprint
142	`lem:to_sheaf_preserves_epi`).  A one-line corollary of `toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits`:
143	finite-colimit preservation implies preservation of pushouts (`WalkingSpan`), hence of
144	epimorphisms.  This is the instance unlocking the passage from a module epimorphism to local
145	surjectivity in `affine_surj_of_vanishing`. -/
146	theorem toSheaf_preservesEpimorphisms.{v', u', w'} {C : Type u'} [Category.{w'} C]
147	    {J : GrothendieckTopology C} (R : Sheaf J RingCat.{v'})
148	    [HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{v'}] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{v'}] :
149	    (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v'} R).PreservesEpimorphisms := by
150	  haveI : PreservesColimitsOfShape WalkingSpan (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v'} R) :=
151	    (toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits R).preservesFiniteColimits WalkingSpan
152	  exact preservesEpimorphisms_of_preservesColimitsOfShape _
153	
154	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — standard covers are cofinal -/
155	
156	/-- **Standard covers are cofinal among open covers of a distinguished open** (Stacks 02KG, Tag
157	009L; blueprint `lem:standard_cover_cofinal`).  Given a distinguished open `D(f)` of `Spec R` and an
158	arbitrary open cover `W : α → (Spec R).Opens` of it, there is a finite standard subcover refining
159	it: finitely many `g : Fin n → R` with `D(f) = ⨆ᵢ D(gᵢ)` and each `D(gᵢ) ≤ W (φ i)`.  This is the
160	refinement step invoked in `affine_surj_of_vanishing`.  Project-local: combines quasi-compactness of
161	`D(f)` with the basic-open basis. -/
162	theorem standard_cover_cofinal {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (f : R) {α : Type u}
163	    (W : α → (Spec R).Opens)
164	    (hcov : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ ⨆ a, W a) :
165	    ∃ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R) (φ : Fin n → α),
166	      (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens) = ⨆ i, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i) ∧
167	      ∀ i, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i) : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ W (φ i) := by
168	  classical
169	  set Uf : (Spec R).Opens := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f with hUf
170	  have hK : IsCompact (Uf : Set ↥(Spec R)) := PrimeSpectrum.isCompact_basicOpen f
171	  have hbasis := PrimeSpectrum.isTopologicalBasis_basic_opens (R := R)
172	  -- `B r` is the distinguished open `D(r)` typed as an open of `Spec R`.
173	  let B : R → (Spec R).Opens := fun r => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r
174	  -- Index type: basic opens contained in `Uf ⊓ W a`, carrying the witness `a`.
175	  let I := {p : R × α // B p.1 ≤ Uf ⊓ W p.2}
176	  let cover : I → Set ↥(Spec R) := fun p => (B p.1.1 : Set ↥(Spec R))
177	  have hopen : ∀ i, IsOpen (cover i) := fun i => (B i.1.1).isOpen
178	  -- The chosen basic opens cover `Uf`.
179	  have hsub : (Uf : Set ↥(Spec R)) ⊆ ⋃ i, cover i := by
180	    intro x hx
181	    have hxUf : x ∈ Uf := hx
182	    obtain ⟨a, hxa⟩ := TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mp (hcov hxUf)
183	    have hxinf : x ∈ ((Uf ⊓ W a : (Spec R).Opens) : Set ↥(Spec R)) := by
184	      rw [TopologicalSpace.Opens.coe_inf]
185	      exact ⟨hxUf, hxa⟩
186	    obtain ⟨v, hvrange, hxv, hvsub⟩ :=
187	      hbasis.exists_subset_of_mem_open hxinf (Uf ⊓ W a).isOpen
188	    obtain ⟨r, hr⟩ := hvrange
189	    have hr' : (B r : Set ↥(Spec R)) = v := hr
190	    have hle : B r ≤ Uf ⊓ W a := by
191	      rw [← SetLike.coe_subset_coe, hr']; exact hvsub
192	    refine Set.mem_iUnion.mpr ⟨⟨(r, a), hle⟩, ?_⟩
193	    change x ∈ (B r : Set ↥(Spec R))
194	    rw [hr']; exact hxv
195	  -- Quasi-compactness: extract a finite subcover.
196	  obtain ⟨t, ht⟩ := hK.elim_finite_subcover cover hopen hsub
197	  -- Repackage the finite index set `t : Finset I` as `Fin n`.
198	  let e := t.equivFin
199	  refine ⟨t.card, fun i => (e.symm i).1.1.1, fun i => (e.symm i).1.1.2, ?_, ?_⟩
200	  · apply le_antisymm
201	    · intro x hx
202	      obtain ⟨i, hit, hxi⟩ := Set.mem_iUnion₂.mp (ht hx)
203	      rw [TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup]
204	      refine ⟨e ⟨i, hit⟩, ?_⟩
205	      change x ∈ B (e.symm (e ⟨i, hit⟩)).1.1.1
206	      rw [Equiv.symm_apply_apply]; exact hxi
207	    · rw [iSup_le_iff]
208	      intro i
209	      exact le_trans (e.symm i).1.2 inf_le_left
210	  · intro i
211	    exact le_trans (e.symm i).1.2 inf_le_right
212	
213	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — section surjectivity for the affine cover system -/
214	
215	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
216	/-- **Section surjectivity for the affine cover system** (Stacks 02KG, `surj_of_vanishing` field;
217	Stacks `lemma-ses-cech-h1`). Let `S : 0 → S₁ → S₂ → S₃ → 0` be a short exact sequence of
218	`O_X`-modules on `Spec R` whose left term `S₁` has vanishing positive Čech cohomology over every
219	standard cover `i ↦ D(gᵢ)` (`hvanish`). Then the section map `S₂(D f) → S₃(D f)` is surjective over
220	every distinguished open `D f`. This discharges the `surj_of_vanishing` field of `affineCoverSystem`.
221	
222	Proof: `S.g` is an epi of `O_X`-modules, so (via `toSheaf_preservesEpimorphisms` + the sheaf
223	local-surjectivity criterion) the underlying map of abelian presheaves is locally surjective. A
224	section `t ∈ S₃(D f)` therefore lifts locally on an open cover of `D f`; refine it to a standard
225	cover `D(gᵢ)` (`standard_cover_cofinal`) carrying local lifts, feed the cover, the lifts and the
226	vanishing `Ȟ¹(𝒰, S₁) = 0` to the Čech `Ȟ¹`-surjectivity criterion `ses_cech_h1`, and glue to a
227	global lift. Project-local: the affine instantiation of the `ses_cech_h1` criterion. -/
228	theorem affine_surj_of_vanishing {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
229	    (S : ShortComplex (Spec R).Modules) (hS : S.ShortExact)
230	    (hvanish : ∀ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R) (f' : R),
231	      (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f' : (Spec R).Opens)
232	        = ⨆ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down) →
233	      ∀ (q : ℕ), 0 < q →
234	        IsZero (cechCohomology (fun i : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down))
235	          ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj S.X₁) q))
236	    (f : R) :
237	    Function.Surjective (ConcreteCategory.hom
238	      (((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.obj).map
239	        ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).map S.g)).app
240	          (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)))) := by
241	  classical
242	  set V₀ : (Spec R).Opens := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f with hV₀
243	  set FX := (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj S.X₁ with hFX
244	  set GX := (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj S.X₂ with hGX
245	  set HX := (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj S.X₃ with hHX
246	  set fι := (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.obj).map
247	    ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).map S.f) with hfι
248	  set gπ := (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.obj).map
249	    ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).map S.g) with hgπ
250	  -- Step 1: a module epi becomes a locally surjective map of abelian presheaves.
251	  haveI hgepi : Epi S.g := hS.epi_g
252	  have hepiTS : Epi ((SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{u} (Spec R).ringCatSheaf).map S.g) :=
253	    @Functor.map_epi _ _ _ _ (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{u} (Spec R).ringCatSheaf)
254	      (toSheaf_preservesEpimorphisms.{u} (Spec R).ringCatSheaf) _ _ S.g hgepi
255	  have hls : TopCat.Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective gπ :=
256	    (Sheaf.isLocallySurjective_iff_epi' AddCommGrpCat.{u}
257	      ((SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{u} (Spec R).ringCatSheaf).map S.g)).mpr hepiTS
258	  rw [TopCat.Presheaf.isLocallySurjective_iff] at hls
259	  intro t
260	  -- Step 2: per-point local lifts of `t`.
261	  have hch : ∀ p : ↥V₀, ∃ (W : (Spec R).Opens) (hWle : W ≤ V₀)
262	      (sl : ToType (GX.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op W))),
263	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (Opposite.op W)) sl
264	        = ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (homOfLE hWle).op) t ∧ (p : Spec R) ∈ W := by
265	    intro p
266	    obtain ⟨W, hWle, ⟨sl, hsl⟩, hmem⟩ := hls V₀ t p.1 p.2
267	    exact ⟨W, hWle, sl, hsl, hmem⟩
268	  choose W hWle sLift hsLift hmem using hch
269	  have hVcov : V₀ ≤ ⨆ p : ↥V₀, W p := by
270	    intro x hx
271	    exact TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mpr ⟨⟨x, hx⟩, hmem ⟨x, hx⟩⟩
272	  -- Step 3: refine to a standard cover `D(gᵢ)`.
273	  obtain ⟨n, g, φ, hVeq, hgle⟩ := standard_cover_cofinal f W hVcov
274	  set U : ULift.{u} (Fin n) → (Spec R).Opens := fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down) with hU
275	  have hUsup : ⨆ i, U i = V₀ := by
276	    rw [hU, hV₀, hVeq]
277	    exact Equiv.ulift.{u}.iSup_comp (g := fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i))
278	  -- the restricted local lifts on the standard cover
279	  set sLoc : ∀ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), ToType (GX.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (U i))) :=
280	    fun i => ConcreteCategory.hom (GX.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hgle i.down)).op)
281	      (sLift (φ i.down)) with hsLoc
282	  -- the section `t` transported to `⨆ U` (`= V₀`)
283	  have hopV : (Opposite.op (⨆ i, U i) : (TopologicalSpace.Opens (Spec R))ᵒᵖ) = Opposite.op V₀ :=
284	    congrArg Opposite.op hUsup
285	  set s : ToType (HX.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨆ i, U i))) :=
286	    ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (eqToHom hopV.symm)) t with hs
287	  -- assemble the hypotheses of `ses_cech_h1`
288	  have hπι : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥(Spec R))ᵒᵖ) (x : ToType (FX.presheaf.obj V)),
289	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app V) (ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V) x) = 0 := by
290	    intro V x
291	    have hz : fι ≫ gπ = 0 := by
292	      rw [hfι, hgπ, ← Functor.map_comp, ← Functor.map_comp, S.zero,
293	        Functor.map_zero, Functor.map_zero]
294	    have := congrArg (fun (ψ : FX.presheaf ⟶ HX.presheaf) => ConcreteCategory.hom (ψ.app V) x) hz
295	    simpa using this
296	  have hmono : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥(Spec R))ᵒᵖ),
297	      Function.Injective (ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V)) := by
298	    intro V
299	    haveI hpzm : (sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V)).PreservesZeroMorphisms := by
300	      unfold sectionsFunctor; infer_instance
301	    haveI hpfl : PreservesFiniteLimits (sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V)) := by
302	      unfold sectionsFunctor; infer_instance
303	    haveI : Mono S.f := hS.mono_f
304	    have hmonoAb : Mono (fι.app V) :=
305	      inferInstanceAs (Mono ((sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V)).map S.f))
306	    rwa [AddCommGrpCat.mono_iff_injective] at hmonoAb
307	  have hker : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥(Spec R))ᵒᵖ) (x : ToType (GX.presheaf.obj V)),
308	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app V) x = 0 → ∃ y, ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V) y = x := by
309	    intro V x hx
310	    haveI hpzm : (sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V)).PreservesZeroMorphisms := by
311	      unfold sectionsFunctor; infer_instance
312	    haveI hpfl : PreservesFiniteLimits (sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V)) := by
313	      unfold sectionsFunctor; infer_instance
314	    have hex : (S.map (sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V))).Exact :=
315	      ShortComplex.Exact.map_of_mono_of_preservesKernel hS.exact
316	        (sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V)) hS.mono_f inferInstance
317	    rw [ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact] at hex
318	    exact (hex x).mp hx
319	  -- the vanishing input, packaged as homology of the section Čech complex
320	  have hcovf : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens)
321	      = ⨆ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down) := by
322	    rw [hVeq]
323	    exact (Equiv.ulift.{u}.iSup_comp (g := fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i))).symm
324	  have hH1 : IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U FX).homology 1) := hvanish n g f hcovf 1 one_pos
325	  -- the local-lift compatibility `gπ(sLocᵢ) = s |_ Uᵢ`
326	  have hlift : ∀ i, ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (Opposite.op (U i))) (sLoc i)
327	      = ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (TopologicalSpace.Opens.leSupr U i).op) s := by
328	    intro i
329	    simp only [hsLoc, hs]
330	    erw [gπ.naturality_apply (homOfLE (hgle i.down)).op (sLift (φ i.down)), hsLift (φ i.down)]
331	    show ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hgle i.down)).op)
332	        (ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hWle (φ i.down))).op) t)
333	      = ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (TopologicalSpace.Opens.leSupr U i).op)
334	        (ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (eqToHom hopV.symm)) t)
335	    have hmaps : HX.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hWle (φ i.down))).op
336	          ≫ HX.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hgle i.down)).op
337	        = HX.presheaf.map (eqToHom hopV.symm)
338	          ≫ HX.presheaf.map (TopologicalSpace.Opens.leSupr U i).op := by
339	      rw [← HX.presheaf.map_comp, ← HX.presheaf.map_comp]
340	      congr 1
341	    simp only [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply]
342	    rw [← hmaps]
343	    exact (ConcreteCategory.comp_apply _ _ t).symm
344	  -- apply the Čech surjectivity criterion
345	  obtain ⟨glob, hglob⟩ := ses_cech_h1 U FX GX HX fι gπ S.X₂.isSheaf S.X₃.isSheaf
346	    hπι hmono hker s hH1 sLoc hlift
347	  -- transport the global lift back to `V₀`
348	  refine ⟨ConcreteCategory.hom (GX.presheaf.map (eqToHom hopV)) glob, ?_⟩
349	  erw [gπ.naturality_apply (eqToHom hopV) glob, hglob]
350	  show ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (eqToHom hopV))
351	      (ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (eqToHom hopV.symm)) t) = t
352	  rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← HX.presheaf.map_comp]
353	  simp only [eqToHom_trans, eqToHom_refl, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
354	  rfl

356	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the affine cover system (Stacks 02KG) -/
357	
358	set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
359	/-- **The affine cover system** (Stacks 02KG, `def:affine_cover_system`). For `X = Spec R`, the
360	basis `B` is the distinguished opens `D f` (`f : R`) and the admissible coverings `Cov` are the
361	standard finite covers `i ↦ D(gᵢ)` (indexed by `ULift (Fin n)`, so as to land in `Type u`). The
362	three nontrivial fields are discharged by:
363	- `faces_mem` ← `affine_faces_mem` (finite intersections of distinguished opens are distinguished);
364	- `surj_of_vanishing` ← `affine_surj_of_vanishing` (the `ses_cech_h1` section-surjectivity criterion);
365	- `injective_acyclic` ← `injective_cech_acyclicFam` (cover-agnostic injective Čech-acyclicity, applied
366	  directly to the distinguished opens of each standard cover — no `Spec R_f` restriction detour).
367	Project-local: the affine instantiation of `BasisCovSystem` consumed by `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis`. -/
368	noncomputable def affineCoverSystem (R : CommRingCat.{u}) : BasisCovSystem (Spec R) where
369	  B := Set.range (fun f : R => (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens))
370	  Cov := { c : CovDatum (Spec R) | ∃ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R) (f : R),
371	    c = ⟨ULift.{u} (Fin n), fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)⟩ ∧
372	    (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens)
373	      = ⨆ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down) }
374	  faces_mem := by
375	    rintro c ⟨n, g, f, rfl, hcov⟩ p σ
376	    exact affine_faces_mem (fun j : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => g j.down) σ
377	  surj_of_vanishing := by
378	    rintro S hSE hvanish V ⟨f, rfl⟩
379	    refine affine_surj_of_vanishing S hSE (fun n g f' hcov q hq => ?_) f
380	    exact hvanish ⟨ULift.{u} (Fin n), fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)⟩
381	      ⟨n, g, f', rfl, hcov⟩ q hq
382	  injective_acyclic := by
383	    rintro I hI c ⟨n, g, f, rfl, hcov⟩ q hq
384	    haveI : Injective I := hI
385	    have hfam := injective_cech_acyclicFam
386	      (U := fun i : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)) I q hq
387	    exact hfam
388	
389	end AlgebraicGeometry

62	/-- **Standard-cover Čech-complex vanishing on affines** (P3; Stacks 02KG,
63	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`).

65	Let `R` be a commutative ring, `s : ι → R` a spanning family
66	(`Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤`), and `F` a quasi-coherent `O_{Spec R}`-module.
67	The relative Čech complex of the standard affine cover
68	`Scheme.affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop s hs` has vanishing cohomology in all
69	positive degrees: `Hᵖ = 0` for `p ≥ 1`.

71	Route: (L1) identify the complex with `∏_σ M_{s_σ}` via away-localisation
72	sections; (L2) reduce positive-degree exactness to the localised complexes via

74	`h(σ)_{i₀…i_p} = σ_{r i₀…i_p}` on `M_{s_r}` (where `s_r` is invertible). -/
75	theorem CechAcyclic.affine {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {S : Scheme.{u}}
76	    (f : Spec R ⟶ S) [IsAffineHom f]
77	    {ι : Type u} [Finite ι] (s : ι → R) (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤)
78	    (F : (Spec R).Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) (p : ℕ) (hp : 1 ≤ p) :
79	    IsZero ((CechComplex f ((Scheme.affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop s hs).openCover) F).homology p) := by

87	  --     `s_r` the fixed index `r` is global, so the alternating Čech coboundary
88	  --     is contracted by the prepend homotopy.  TWO forms are now available:
89	  --       - constant coefficients: `combDifferential_exact` (`Function.Exact`);
90	  --       - *varying* coefficients `M_{s_σ}` (the dependent-coefficient port the
91	  --         localised complex actually has): `CombinatorialCech.Dependent.*`,
92	  --         culminating in `depDiff_exact` — the `Function.Exact` of the
93	  --         dependent alternating differential, given the away-localisation
94	  --         maps `δ` (cofaces), `c` (prepend isos), and their unit/shift/comm
95	  --         compatibilities `hu`/`hsh`/`hcomm`.  This is the exact shape L2
96	  --         consumes per node after localising at `s_r`.
97	  -- L3 (both forms) is the from-scratch combinatorial core that the iter-011
98	  -- prover was blocked on; it is now available as `CombinatorialCech.*` and
