9	/-!
10	# Presheaf-level Čech section complex (P3b)
11	
12	This file contains the section Čech complex and the free-Yoneda equivalences:
13	- `sectionCechComplex`        — cosimplicial `Ab`-valued section Čech complex
14	                                (`\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechComplex}`)
15	- `freeYonedaHomEquiv`        — Hom-set equivalence out of the free presheaf
16	                                (`\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.freeYonedaHomEquiv}`)
17	- `freeYonedaHomAddEquiv`     — additive version of the above
18	                                (`\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.freeYonedaHomAddEquiv}`)
19	- `injective_toPresheafOfModules` — injectivity transport to presheaves of modules
20	                                (`\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.injective_toPresheafOfModules}`)
21	
22	Related machinery that has moved to sibling files:
23	- `cechFreePresheafComplex` / `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso` → `FreePresheafComplex.lean`
24	- `cechComplex_hom_identification` / `injective_cech_acyclic` → `CechBridge.lean`
25	-/
26	
27	universe u
28	
29	open CategoryTheory Limits
30	
31	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
32	
33	/-
34	Planner strategy (P3b presheaf-Čech bridge; see analogies/p3b-presheafcech.md +
35	blueprint §Presheaf-level Čech machinery):
36	
37	The goal is to prove that injective `O_X`-modules have trivial positive-degree Čech
38	cohomology with respect to any open cover.  The strategy avoids `presheafModules_
39	enoughInjectives` and `cech_delta_functor_presheaves` (decisions 4–5 in the analogy
40	file are expensive Mathlib gaps and are NOT on the critical path).  The build order is:
41	
42	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
43	Step 1 — `sectionCechComplex`
44	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
45	Type: given a scheme `X`, an open cover `𝒰 : X.OpenCover`, an open `U : Opens ↥X`,
46	and a presheaf of modules `F : X.PresheafOfModules`, produce the section Čech
47	cochain complex
48	
49	    Č•(𝒰, F, U) : CochainComplex (ModuleCat (X.ringCatSheaf.val.obj (op U))) ℕ
50	
51	whose degree-`p` term is the product `∏_{(i₀,…,iₚ) : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.J}
52	  F.obj (op (𝒰.U i₀ ⊓ … ⊓ 𝒰.U iₚ ⊓ U))`, equipped with the alternating restriction
53	differential.
54	
55	THIS OBJECT IS DISTINCT from `CechComplex` in `CechHigherDirectImage.lean`:
56	`CechComplex` lives in `S.Modules` (pushforward along `f`) and is a relative complex.
57	`sectionCechComplex` is a plain cochain complex of `R`-modules where `R = O_X(U)`.
58	
59	Key Mathlib hooks:
60	- `PresheafOfModules` — the category `X.PresheafOfModules =
61	  PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf.val`.
62	- `PresheafOfModules.evaluation R (op V) : PresheafOfModules R ⥤ ModuleCat (R.obj (op V))`
63	  — the exact "sections over `V`" functor; use it to extract `F.obj (op V)`.
64	- `CochainComplex.of` or `HomologicalComplex.mk` — assemble the complex from
65	  degree-`p` objects and differentials.
66	
67	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
68	Step 2 — `cechFreePresheafComplex`
69	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
70	Type: given `X` and `𝒰`, produce the chain complex of presheaves of modules
71	
72	    K(𝒰)_• : ChainComplex X.PresheafOfModules ℕ
73	
74	whose degree-`p` term is the direct sum `⨁_{(i₀,…,iₚ) : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.J}
75	  (PresheafOfModules.free _).obj (yoneda.obj (𝒰.U i₀ ⊓ … ⊓ 𝒰.U iₚ))`.
76	
77	DO NOT introduce a bespoke extension-by-zero functor `j_!`.  The blueprint's
78	`(j_{i₀…iₚ})_! (O_X|_{U_{i₀…iₚ}})` is canonically identified with
79	`(PresheafOfModules.free _).obj (yoneda.obj U_…)`: on sections over `V`, this is
80	the free `R(V)`-module on `Hom(V, U_…)` — which is `R(V)` when `V ⊆ U_…` and `0`
81	otherwise, matching extension-by-zero of `O_X|_{U_…}` exactly.
82	
83	The differentials are `(PresheafOfModules.free _).map` of the representable maps
84	`yoneda.obj (U_{i₀⊓…⊓U_{iₚ₊₁}}) → yoneda.obj (U_{i₀⊓…⊓̂ᵢⱼ⊓…⊓U_{iₚ₊₁}})` induced
85	by the inclusion of opens `𝒰.U i₀ ⊓ … ⊓ 𝒰.U iₚ₊₁ ≤ 𝒰.U i₀ ⊓ … ⊓̂ᵢⱼ ⊓ …`, with
86	the standard alternating sign.
87	
88	Key Mathlib hooks:
89	- `PresheafOfModules.free R : (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u) ⥤ PresheafOfModules R` — the free
90	  presheaf-of-modules functor.
91	- `PresheafOfModules.freeAdjunction R : free R ⊣ toPresheaf R ⋙ whiskeringRight … (forget Ab)`
92	  — the adjunction; `freeObjDesc` / `freeHomEquiv` give the universal property
93	  `Hom(free(P), F) ≅ NatTrans P (F ∘ forget)` used in step 3.
94	- `yoneda : Opens ↥X ⥤ (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u` — the Yoneda embedding.
95	- Use `Finset.univ`-indexed direct sums; `ChainComplex.of` or `HomologicalComplex.mk`
96	  to assemble.
97	
98	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
99	Step 3 — `cechComplex_hom_identification`
100	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
101	Type: a natural isomorphism of cochain complexes of abelian groups (or
102	`O_X(U)`-modules):
103	
104	    Hom_{X.PresheafOfModules}(K(𝒰)_•, F)  ≅  Č•(𝒰, F, U)
105	
106	i.e., the complex of hom-sets out of `K(𝒰)_•` is the section Čech cochain complex.
107	
108	Strategy:
109	- At degree `p`, term-by-term: use the adjunction iso
110	  `(PresheafOfModules.freeAdjunction _).homEquiv` to identify
111	  `Hom_{PMod}(free(yoneda(U_I)), F)  ≅  NatTrans (yoneda(U_I)) (toPresheaf F)`
112	  and then Yoneda (`yonedaEquiv`) to get `≅  (toPresheaf F).obj (op U_I) = F.obj (op U_I)`.
113	  The product over multi-indices matches the degree-`p` term of `Č•(𝒰, F, U)`.
114	- Check that the differential intertwines: the differential on `Hom(K_•, F)` induced
115	  by pre-composition with the alternating-sign `free.map` maps matches the alternating
116	  restriction differential of `Č•(𝒰, F, U)`.
117	- Build as a cochain-complex isomorphism using `HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents`.
118	
119	Key Mathlib hooks:
120	- `PresheafOfModules.freeAdjunction` — the core adjunction.
121	- `yonedaEquiv` — the Yoneda natural bijection.
122	- `PresheafOfModules.evaluation` — sections extraction.
123	
124	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
125	Step 4 — `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`
126	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
127	Type: the augmented complex `K(𝒰)_• → O_𝒰[0]` (augmentation = "sheaf restricted to
128	the cover") is a quasi-isomorphism, i.e., `K(𝒰)_•` is a free resolution of `O_𝒰`.
129	
130	Strategy (Mathlib has no packaged Čech contractibility lemma; build it directly):
131	- Homology of presheaves of modules is computed objectwise
132	  (limits/colimits in `PresheafOfModules R` are objectwise; see `HasColimits`
133	  instance). So it suffices to show that for each open `V : Opens ↥X`,
134	  the complex `K(𝒰)_•(V)` of `R(V)`-modules is contractible.
135	- The sectionwise complex `K(𝒰)_•(V)` is:
136	  degree `p` = `⨁_{I : Fin(p+1)→J} R(V)` when `V ⊆ U_{I(0)} ⊓ … ⊓ U_{I(p)}`, else `0`.
137	- Fix any index `i_fix` such that `V ⊆ 𝒰.U i_fix` (if no such index, the complex is
138	  `0` and trivially exact). The contracting homotopy `h : K_p(V) → K_{p+1}(V)` is:
139	      `h(s)_{i₀,i₁,…,iₚ} = (i₀ = i_fix) · s_{i₁,…,iₚ}`
140	  (extend the multi-index by prepending `i_fix`). Check: `dh + hd = id` at each degree.
141	- Assemble using `Homotopy` / `HomotopyEquiv` from Mathlib (`HomologicalComplex.Homotopy`
142	  in `Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.Homotopy`). From the chain homotopy conclude
143	  `HomotopyEquiv` and hence quasi-isomorphism.
144	- Do NOT route through `ExtraDegeneracy` (it has a different index convention and is
145	  not directly applicable here).
146	
147	Key Mathlib hooks:
148	- `HomologicalComplex.Homotopy` — chain homotopy type.
149	- `HomotopyEquiv.toQuasiIso` (or `Homotopy.toQuasiIso`) — chain homotopy ⟹ quasi-iso.
150	
151	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
152	Step 5 — `injective_cech_acyclic`
153	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
154	Type: for an injective `I : X.Modules` and an open cover `𝒰 : X.OpenCover`, the
155	positive-degree Čech cohomology `Ȟ^p(𝒰, I) = 0` for all `p > 0`.
156	
157	Strategy (two independent parts; does NOT need decisions 4–5):
158	
159	Part (a) — Injective sheaf ⟹ injective presheaf:
160	- Apply `CategoryTheory.Injective.injective_of_adjoint` to the adjunction
161	  `PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction α` where `α = 𝟙` for the scheme `X`.
162	  This adjunction is `sheafification α ⊣ (forget R) ⋙ restrictScalars α`, i.e.
163	  sheafification is left adjoint to the inclusion `toPresheafOfModules`.
164	- The left adjoint `sheafification` is exact (it is exact as a colimit of
165	  sheafification-endofunctors over a Grothendieck topology), hence in particular
166	  `PreservesMonomorphisms`. So `Injective.injective_of_adjoint` gives:
167	  `I` injective in `X.Modules` ⟹ `Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules.obj I`
168	  is injective in `X.PresheafOfModules`.
169	- Relevant Mathlib names:
170	  `PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction`,
171	  `CategoryTheory.Injective.injective_of_adjoint`,
172	  `Functor.preservesInjectiveObjects_of_adjunction_of_preservesMonomorphisms`,
173	  `Adjunction.map_injective`.
174	
175	Part (b) — Vanishing of positive Čech cohomology:
176	- By step 4, `K(𝒰)_• → O_𝒰[0]` is an exact augmented complex (quasi-iso from step 4).
177	- Since `I_pshf := toPresheafOfModules.obj I` is injective in `X.PresheafOfModules`
178	  (by part (a)), the functor `Hom_{PMod}(-, I_pshf)` is exact.
179	- Applying `Hom_{PMod}(-, I_pshf)` to the exact augmented complex `K(𝒰)_• → O_𝒰` and
180	  using step 3 (`cechComplex_hom_identification`) to identify the resulting complex
181	  with `Č•(𝒰, I, U)`, conclude that `Č•(𝒰, I, U)` is exact in positive degrees, i.e.
182	  `Ȟ^p(𝒰, I) = 0` for `p > 0`.
183	- This is the Stacks-project proof of `lemma-injective-trivial-cech` (Stacks tag
184	  0BKP and surrounding; see `references/stacks-cohomology.md`).
185	
186	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
187	Verified Mathlib API summary (LSP-checked in this project):
188	  PresheafOfModules
189	  PresheafOfModules.free
190	  PresheafOfModules.freeAdjunction
191	  PresheafOfModules.evaluation
192	  PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
193	  CategoryTheory.Injective.injective_of_adjoint
194	  yoneda
195	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
196	-/
197	
198	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — PresheafCech (injective transfer) -/
199	
200	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
201	
202	/-- **An injective sheaf of `O_X`-modules is injective as a presheaf of modules.**
203	
204	This is Part 1 of the {\v C}ech-acyclicity of injectives (blueprint
205	`lem:injective_cech_acyclic`, Stacks `lemma-injective-trivial-cech`): the inclusion
206	`Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X : X.Modules ⥤ X.PresheafOfModules` is the right
207	adjoint of the (exact) sheafification functor, so it carries injective objects to
208	injective objects.
209	
210	Project-local because Mathlib has no packaged "injective sheaf of modules ⟹ injective
211	presheaf of modules" statement; it is obtained here by feeding
212	`PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 _)` (whose left adjoint
213	`sheafification` preserves monomorphisms) to `Injective.injective_of_adjoint`. The
214	right adjoint of that adjunction is *definitionally* `toPresheafOfModules X`
215	(`SheafOfModules.forget _ ⋙ restrictScalars (𝟙 _)`). -/
216	theorem injective_toPresheafOfModules (I : X.Modules) [Injective I] :
217	    Injective ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj I) := by
218	  -- the left adjoint of `toPresheafOfModules X` is `sheafification (𝟙 _)`, which is
219	  -- exact and in particular preserves monomorphisms
220	  haveI : (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).PreservesMonomorphisms :=
221	    inferInstance
222	  -- realign the ambient category instance so the `Injective` instance is found cheaply
223	  haveI : Injective (C := SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf) I := ‹Injective I›
224	  exact Injective.injective_of_adjoint
225	    (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)) I
226	
227	/-- **Free–Yoneda representability (per-term bijection).**
228	
229	For an open `V : Opens X` and a presheaf of `O_X`-modules `F`, the morphisms out of the
230	free presheaf of modules on the representable `yoneda V` are exactly the sections of
231	`F` over `V`:
232	```
233	  Hom_{PMod(O_X)}(free(yoneda V), F) ≃ F(V).
234	```
235	This is the free–forgetful adjunction (`PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv`) composed with
236	the Yoneda bijection (`yonedaEquiv`). It is the single-multi-index core of the
237	{\v C}ech complex hom-identification (blueprint `lem:cech_complex_hom_identification`,
238	Stacks `lemma-cech-map-into`): taking the product over multi-indices turns this into
239	`Hom(K(𝒰)_p, F) = Č^p(𝒰, F)`.
240	
241	Project-local because Mathlib packages the two halves separately but not their
242	composite as the corepresentability of evaluation by `free(yoneda V)`. The target
243	`(F.presheaf ⋙ forget Ab).obj (op V)` is the underlying set of the section group
244	`F(V)`. -/
245	noncomputable def freeYonedaHomEquiv (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
246	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
247	    ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ⟶ F) ≃
248	      (F.presheaf ⋙ forget Ab).obj (Opposite.op V) :=
249	  PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv.trans yonedaEquiv
250	
251	/-- **Generator formula for `freeYonedaHomEquiv`.** The bijection
252	`Hom(free(yoneda V), F) ≃ F(V)` is evaluation of the morphism's component at `V` on the
253	canonical free generator `freeMk (𝟙 V)`. Recorded project-locally because this concrete
254	description is what makes the bijection visibly additive (`freeYonedaHomAddEquiv`) and is
255	unavailable as a packaged Mathlib lemma. -/
256	lemma freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.PresheafOfModules)
257	    (ψ : (PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ⟶ F) :
258	    freeYonedaHomEquiv V F ψ
259	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (ψ.app (Opposite.op V))) (ModuleCat.freeMk (𝟙 V)) := by
260	  simp only [freeYonedaHomEquiv]; rfl
261	
262	/-- **Additive upgrade of `freeYonedaHomEquiv`.** The free–Yoneda hom bijection
263	`Hom_{PMod}(free(yoneda V), F) ≃ F(V)` is an isomorphism of abelian groups: the source is
264	the (preadditive) hom-group of presheaves of modules and the target is the section group
265	`F(V)`. This is the per-multi-index additive core of the {\v C}ech hom-identification
266	(blueprint `lem:cech_complex_hom_identification`); taking products over multi-indices and
267	intertwining with the differential upgrades it to the full complex isomorphism. Additivity
268	holds because, by `freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply`, the bijection is evaluation of the
269	component `ψ.app (op V)` (a module map, hence additive) on a fixed generator, and the
270	component of a sum of morphisms is the sum of components.
271	
272	Project-local because Mathlib packages the underlying bijection (`freeHomEquiv` ∘
273	`yonedaEquiv`) only at the level of types, not as an `AddEquiv`. -/
274	noncomputable def freeYonedaHomAddEquiv (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
275	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
276	    ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ⟶ F) ≃+
277	      (F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)) where
278	  toFun := freeYonedaHomEquiv V F
279	  invFun := (freeYonedaHomEquiv V F).symm
280	  left_inv := (freeYonedaHomEquiv V F).left_inv
281	  right_inv := (freeYonedaHomEquiv V F).right_inv
282	  map_add' f g := by
283	    rw [freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply,
284	      PresheafOfModules.add_app]
285	    change (ModuleCat.Hom.hom (f.app (Opposite.op V) + g.app (Opposite.op V))) _ = _
286	    rw [ModuleCat.hom_add]; rfl
287	
288	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — section {\v C}ech complex -/
289	
290	/-- **Cosimplicial abelian group underlying the section {\v C}ech complex.**
291	
292	For a family of opens `U : ι → Opens X` (an open covering) and a presheaf of modules `F`,
293	the cosimplicial object in `Ab` whose value in cosimplicial degree `p` is the product
294	`∏_{σ : Fin (p+1) → ι} F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` of sections over the `(p+1)`-fold intersections. A
295	cosimplicial map `f : [m] ⟶ [n]` acts at multi-index `σ : Fin (n+1) → ι` by projecting to
296	the coordinate `σ ∘ f` and then restricting along `⨅ₖ U (σ k) ≤ ⨅ₗ U (σ (f l))`. This is the
297	covariant ({\v C}ech) variance that the alternating-coface-map complex turns into a cochain
298	complex.
299	
300	Project-local: Mathlib has no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules. Built
301	as an honest `SimplexCategory ⥤ Ab` functor so that `alternatingCofaceMapComplex` supplies
302	`d² = 0` for free (no hand-rolled alternating-sum identity). -/
303	noncomputable def sectionCechCosimplicial {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
304	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CosimplicialObject Ab.{u} where
305	  obj n := ∏ᶜ (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
306	  map {m n} f := Pi.lift (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι =>
307	    Pi.π _ (σ ∘ f.toOrderHom) ≫
308	      F.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _ (f.toOrderHom l)))).op)
309	  map_id n := by
310	    apply Pi.hom_ext
311	    intro σ
312	    simp only [Pi.lift_π, Category.id_comp, SimplexCategory.id_toOrderHom,
313	      OrderHom.id_coe, Function.comp_id]
314	    rw [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE _).op (𝟙 _)]
315	    simp
316	  map_comp {l m n} f g := by
317	    apply Pi.hom_ext
318	    intro σ
319	    simp only [Pi.lift_π, Category.assoc, SimplexCategory.comp_toOrderHom, OrderHom.comp_coe]
320	    rw [← Category.assoc, Pi.lift_π, Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp]
321	    congr 1
322	
323	/-- **Section {\v C}ech cochain complex of a presheaf of modules**
324	(blueprint `def:section_cech_complex`).
325	
326	The cochain complex `Č•(𝒰, F)` of abelian groups with degree-`p` term
327	`∏_{σ : Fin (p+1) → ι} F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` and the alternating-sum-of-restrictions differential,
328	obtained as the alternating-coface-map complex of `sectionCechCosimplicial`. This is the
329	section complex of the blueprint, kept distinct from the relative pushforward `CechComplex`;
330	it is the right-hand side of the {\v C}ech hom-identification
331	`Hom_{PMod}(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Č•(𝒰, F)`.
332	
333	Project-local: no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules exists in Mathlib. -/
334	noncomputable def sectionCechComplex {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
335	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CochainComplex Ab.{u} ℕ :=
336	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).obj (sectionCechCosimplicial U F)
337	
338	end AlgebraicGeometry
339

7	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Tilde
8	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Restrict
9	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.Quasicoherent
10	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PushforwardContinuous
11	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackFree
12	import Mathlib.Topology.Sheaves.Over
13	import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.CoverPreserving
14	import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.DenseSubsite.Basic
15	
16	/-!
17	# Restriction of an `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module to a basic open (Stacks 01I8) — Route-P step P1a
18	
19	Project-local supplement for the affine quasi-coherence equivalence.
20	-/
21	
22	open AlgebraicGeometry CategoryTheory
23	
24	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
25	
26	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — continuity of `Opens.overEquivalence`
27	
28	Mathlib provides `TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U : Over U ≌ Opens ↥U` but, as recorded in
29	its source `## TODO`, does *not* yet prove that the two functors are continuous for the relevant
30	Grothendieck topologies.  These instances supply exactly that (the gateway brick for the Route B
31	restrict–over bridge B3): both functors are cover-preserving, and `CompatiblePreserving` is automatic
32	for the cover-dense functors of an equivalence, so `Functor.IsContinuous` follows. -/
33	
34	open TopologicalSpace in
35	/-- The forward functor of `Opens.overEquivalence U` preserves covers. -/
36	theorem Opens.overEquivalence_functor_coverPreserving
37	    {X : Type u} [TopologicalSpace X] (U : Opens X) :
38	    CoverPreserving ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U)
39	      (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor where
40	  cover_preserve {Y S} hS := by
41	    rw [GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff] at hS
42	    intro y hy
43	    obtain ⟨V, f, hVf, hyV⟩ := hS y.1 hy
44	    obtain ⟨W, h, h', hSh, hfeq⟩ := hVf
45	    refine ⟨(Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.obj W,
46	      (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.map h, ⟨W, h, 𝟙 _, hSh, by simp⟩, ?_⟩
47	    show y.1 ∈ W.left
48	    exact leOfHom h' hyV
49	
50	open TopologicalSpace in
51	/-- The inverse functor of `Opens.overEquivalence U` preserves covers. -/
52	theorem Opens.overEquivalence_inverse_coverPreserving
53	    {X : Type u} [TopologicalSpace X] (U : Opens X) :
54	    CoverPreserving (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U)
55	      (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse where
56	  cover_preserve {Y S} hS := by
57	    rw [GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff]
58	    intro x hx
59	    obtain ⟨⟨x', hx'U⟩, hx'Y, rfl⟩ := hx
60	    obtain ⟨P, f, hSf, hxP⟩ := hS ⟨x', hx'U⟩ hx'Y
61	    exact ⟨((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj P).left,
62	      ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.map f).left,
63	      ⟨(Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj P, (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.map f, 𝟙 _,
64	        ⟨P, f, 𝟙 _, hSf, by simp⟩, by simp⟩,
65	      ⟨⟨x', hx'U⟩, hxP, rfl⟩⟩
66	
67	open TopologicalSpace in
68	/-- The forward functor of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is continuous. -/
69	instance Opens.overEquivalence_functor_isContinuous
70	    {X : Type u} [TopologicalSpace X] (U : Opens X) :
71	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.IsContinuous
72	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) :=
73	  Functor.IsCoverDense.isContinuous _ _ _ (Opens.overEquivalence_functor_coverPreserving U)
74	
75	open TopologicalSpace in
76	/-- The inverse functor of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is continuous. -/
77	instance Opens.overEquivalence_inverse_isContinuous
78	    {X : Type u} [TopologicalSpace X] (U : Opens X) :
79	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsContinuous
80	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) :=
81	  Functor.IsCoverDense.isContinuous _ _ _ (Opens.overEquivalence_inverse_coverPreserving U)
82	
83	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — restriction of modules to a basic open -/
84	
85	variable {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (f : R)
86	
87	/-- The basic open `D(f) ⊆ Spec R`, as an open subscheme of `Spec R`. -/
88	abbrev specBasicOpen : (Spec R).Opens := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f
89	
90	/-- The localisation morphism `Spec R_f ⟶ Spec R` factoring through `D(f)`: the inverse of the
91	affine identification `D(f) ≅ Spec R_f` followed by the open immersion of `D(f)`. -/
92	noncomputable abbrev specAwayToSpec :
93	    Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away f)) ⟶ Spec R :=
94	  (basicOpenIsoSpecAway f).inv ≫ (specBasicOpen f).ι
95	
96	/-- **Stacks 01I8, `lemma-widetilde-pullback`.** Restriction of an `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module to the basic
97	open `D(f)`, transported to a sheaf of `𝒪_{Spec R_f}`-modules along the affine identification
98	`D(f) ≅ Spec R_f`.  Built by restricting first along the open immersion `D(f) ↪ Spec R`, then along
99	the (iso, hence open) inverse of `basicOpenIsoSpecAway f`.  Project-local: assembles two Mathlib
100	restrictions into the single transport used by the affine quasi-coherence equivalence. -/
101	noncomputable def modulesRestrictBasicOpen (F : (Spec R).Modules) :
102	    (Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away f))).Modules :=
103	  (F.restrict (specBasicOpen f).ι).restrict (basicOpenIsoSpecAway f).inv
104	
105	/-- **Stacks 01I8, `lemma-widetilde-pullback`.** The transported restriction
106	`modulesRestrictBasicOpen f F` is canonically isomorphic to the inverse image of `F` along the
107	localisation morphism `Spec R_f ⟶ Spec R`.  This identifies the double restriction with a single
108	pullback, and is the comparison isomorphism `F|_{D(f)} ≅ F_{(f)}` of the blueprint.  Project-local:
109	reconciles the (good-defeq) iterated `restrict` with the conceptual inverse-image description. -/
110	noncomputable def modulesRestrictBasicOpenIso (F : (Spec R).Modules) :
111	    modulesRestrictBasicOpen f F ≅
112	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback (specAwayToSpec f)).obj F :=
113	  ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorComp (basicOpenIsoSpecAway f).inv (specBasicOpen f).ι).app F).symm
114	    ≪≫ (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback (specAwayToSpec f)).app F
115	
116	/-- The localisation morphism `Spec R_f ⟶ Spec R` of `specAwayToSpec` is exactly the spectrum of the
117	away-localisation ring map `R → R_f`.  Project-local: identifies the geometric transport map with
118	the algebraic `Spec.map (algebraMap …)`, so that pullback along it computes base change. -/
119	theorem specAwayToSpec_eq :
120	    specAwayToSpec f = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap R (Localization.Away f))) := by
121	  rw [specAwayToSpec, Iso.inv_comp_eq]
122	  exact (IsOpenImmersion.isoOfRangeEq_hom_fac _ _ _).symm
123	
124	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Route B presentation transport (B2–B4) -/
125	
126	open SheafOfModules in
127	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
128	/-- **Route B, step B2.** If `M.over U` carries a presentation and `D(g) ⊆ U`, then the further
129	over-restriction `M.over D(g)` admits a presentation. -/
130	noncomputable def presentationOverBasicOpen
131	    (M : (Spec R).Modules) (U : (Spec R).Opens)
132	    (P : (M.over U).Presentation) (g : R) (hg : specBasicOpen g ≤ U) :
133	    (M.over (specBasicOpen g)).Presentation :=
134	  letI W : Over U := Over.mk (homOfLE hg)
135	  letI e : SheafOfModules.{u} ((Spec R).ringCatSheaf.over W.left) ≌
136	      SheafOfModules.{u} (((Spec R).ringCatSheaf.over U).over W) :=
137	    pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence
138	    (Over.iteratedSliceEquiv W)
139	    (S := ((Spec R).ringCatSheaf.over U).over W)
140	    (R := (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.over W.left) (𝟙 _) (𝟙 _)
141	    (by ext : 2; exact (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.1.map_id _)
142	    (by ext : 2; exact (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.1.map_id _)
143	  letI P1 : ((M.over U).over W).Presentation :=
144	    P.map (pushforward (𝟙 (((Spec R).ringCatSheaf.over U).over W))) (by rfl)
145	  letI P2 : (e.inverse.obj ((M.over U).over W)).Presentation :=
146	    P1.map e.inverse (.refl _)
147	  letI iso : e.inverse.obj ((M.over U).over W) ≅ M.over W.left :=
148	    e.fullyFaithfulFunctor.preimageIso
149	      (by exact e.counitIso.app ((M.over U).over W))
150	  show (M.over W.left).Presentation from Presentation.ofIsIso.{u, u, u} iso.hom P2
151	
152	section RestrictOverBridge
153	
154	open SheafOfModules TopologicalSpace
155	
156	private lemma specBasicOpen_ι_image_overEquivalence_functor (g : R) (V : Over (specBasicOpen g)) :
157	    (specBasicOpen g).ι ''ᵁ (Opens.overEquivalence (specBasicOpen g)).functor.obj V = V.left := by
158	  apply Opens.ext
159	  exact Set.image_preimage_eq_of_subset (fun x hx => ⟨⟨x, leOfHom V.hom hx⟩, rfl⟩)
160	
161	/-- Continuity of `overEquivalence.functor` phrased for the open-subscheme carrier (defeq to the
162	plain subtype, but instance search needs the `toScheme` form to fire). -/
163	instance overEquivalence_functor_isContinuous_toScheme (g : R) :
164	    (Opens.overEquivalence (specBasicOpen g)).functor.IsContinuous
165	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(Spec R)).over (specBasicOpen g))
166	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(specBasicOpen g).toScheme) :=
167	  Opens.overEquivalence_functor_isContinuous (specBasicOpen g)
168	
169	/-- Continuity of `overEquivalence.inverse` phrased for the open-subscheme carrier. -/
170	instance overEquivalence_inverse_isContinuous_toScheme (g : R) :
171	    (Opens.overEquivalence (specBasicOpen g)).inverse.IsContinuous
172	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(specBasicOpen g).toScheme)
173	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(Spec R)).over (specBasicOpen g)) :=
174	  Opens.overEquivalence_inverse_isContinuous (specBasicOpen g)
175	
176	/-- The forgetful functor `Over D(g) ⥤ Opens (Spec R)` agrees (via the open-immersion image–preimage
177	identity `ι ''ᵁ (ι ⁻¹ᵁ V) = V` for `V ≤ D(g)`) with the over-site equivalence followed by the
178	open-immersion `opensFunctor`. In the thin `Opens` category naturality is automatic. Project-local:
179	the geometric datum underlying the Route B restrict–over bridge (B3a). -/
180	noncomputable def overForgetIso (g : R) :
181	    Over.forget (specBasicOpen g) ≅
182	      (Opens.overEquivalence (specBasicOpen g)).functor ⋙ (specBasicOpen g).ι.opensFunctor :=
183	  NatIso.ofComponents
184	    (fun V => eqToIso (specBasicOpen_ι_image_overEquivalence_functor g V).symm)
185	    (fun {_ _} _ => Subsingleton.elim _ _)
186	
187	/-- The structure-sheaf comparison `φ` feeding `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence`: the over-picture
188	ring sheaf `(Spec R).ringCatSheaf.over D(g)` maps to the `overEquivalence`-pushforward of the
189	subscheme ring sheaf. Built by whiskering `overForgetIso.inv` into `(Spec R).ringCatSheaf`. -/
190	noncomputable def overBasicOpenRingHom (g : R) :
191	    Sheaf.over (Spec R).ringCatSheaf (specBasicOpen g) ⟶
192	      ((Opens.overEquivalence (specBasicOpen g)).functor.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat
193	        ((Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(Spec R)).over (specBasicOpen g))
194	        (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(specBasicOpen g).toScheme)).obj
195	      (specBasicOpen g).toScheme.ringCatSheaf :=
196	  ⟨Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op (overForgetIso g).inv) (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.val⟩
197	
198	/-- The inverse over-site equivalence followed by `Over.forget` is *definitionally* the
199	open-immersion `opensFunctor` (`overEquivalence.inverse` sends `W` to `⟨Subtype.val '' W, _⟩`, whose
200	`.left` is `ι ''ᵁ W`). Project-local: the (trivial) reverse datum of the B3 bridge. -/
201	noncomputable def overForgetInvIso (g : R) :
202	    (Opens.overEquivalence (specBasicOpen g)).inverse ⋙ Over.forget (specBasicOpen g) ≅
203	      (specBasicOpen g).ι.opensFunctor :=
204	  Iso.refl _
205	
206	/-- The reverse structure-sheaf comparison `ψ` feeding `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` (whiskering
207	of `overForgetInvIso.inv`, which is the identity). -/
208	noncomputable def overBasicOpenRingInvHom (g : R) :
209	    (specBasicOpen g).toScheme.ringCatSheaf ⟶
210	      ((Opens.overEquivalence (specBasicOpen g)).inverse.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat
211	        (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(specBasicOpen g).toScheme)
212	        ((Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(Spec R)).over (specBasicOpen g))).obj
213	      (Sheaf.over (Spec R).ringCatSheaf (specBasicOpen g)) :=
214	  ⟨Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op (overForgetInvIso g).inv) (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.val⟩
215	
216	/-- **Route B, step B3 (the load-bearing bridge — engine).** The equivalence of categories of sheaves
217	of modules between the open subscheme `D(g)` and the over-site `(Spec R).ringCatSheaf.over D(g)`,
218	obtained from `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` along the (continuous) over-site equivalence
219	`Opens.overEquivalence (specBasicOpen g)` fed the structure-sheaf comparison data
220	`overBasicOpenRingHom`/`overBasicOpenRingInvHom`. Its functor sends a subscheme module
221	`F.restrict ι` to the over-picture restriction `F.over D(g)` (agreeing on sections by `restrict_obj`);
222	this object correspondence is the bridge `overBasicOpenIsoRestrict` consumed by B4. -/
223	noncomputable def modulesOverBasicOpenEquivalence (g : R) :
224	    (specBasicOpen g).toScheme.Modules ≌
225	      SheafOfModules.{u} ((Spec R).ringCatSheaf.over (specBasicOpen g)) :=
226	  pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence (Opens.overEquivalence (specBasicOpen g))
227	    (overBasicOpenRingHom g) (overBasicOpenRingInvHom g)
228	    (by
229	      refine NatTrans.ext (funext fun (V : (Opens ↥(specBasicOpen g))ᵒᵖ) => ?_)
230	      simp only [overBasicOpenRingHom, overBasicOpenRingInvHom, NatTrans.comp_app, Functor.whiskerRight_app, NatTrans.op_app,
231	        Functor.whiskerLeft_app, Functor.comp_obj, Functor.op_obj]
232	      erw [← Functor.map_comp]
233	      exact congrArg (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.val.map (Subsingleton.elim _ _))
234	    (by
235	      refine NatTrans.ext (funext fun (V : (Over (specBasicOpen g))ᵒᵖ) => ?_)
236	      simp only [overBasicOpenRingHom, overBasicOpenRingInvHom, NatTrans.comp_app, Functor.whiskerRight_app, NatTrans.op_app,
237	        Functor.whiskerLeft_app, Functor.comp_obj, Functor.op_obj, NatTrans.id_app,
238	        overForgetInvIso, Iso.refl_inv]
239	      erw [← Functor.map_comp]

301	    (modulesRestrictBasicOpen g M).Presentation := by
302	  letI P2 : (M.over (specBasicOpen g)).Presentation := presentationOverBasicOpen M U P g hg

304	      (M.over (specBasicOpen g))).Presentation :=
305	    P2.map (modulesOverBasicOpenEquivalence g).inverse (.refl _)

312	  exact @SheafOfModules.Presentation.map _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ P4
313	    (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor.{u} (basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv) hpc
314	    (restrictBasicOpenUnitIso g)

10	/-!
11	# Exactness of the tilde functor (Stacks 01HV) — Route-P step P3
12	
13	Project-local supplement feeding `AlgebraicGeometry.qcoh_kernel_qcoh`.
14	
15	For `X = Spec R`, the tilde functor `~ : ModuleCat R ⥤ (Spec R).Modules`,
16	`M ↦ M^~`, is exact.  The named target of this file is
17	`AlgebraicGeometry.tildePreservesFiniteLimits : PreservesFiniteLimits (tilde.functor R)`
18	(the *left*-exactness / kernel-preservation half).
19	
20	## What is delivered (axiom-clean)
21	
22	* `tilde_preservesFiniteColimits` — the *right*-exactness half: `~` is a left adjoint
23	  (`AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.adjunction`), hence preserves all colimits, in particular finite
24	  colimits.  This is one of the two halves of exactness.
25	* `tilde_toStalk_map_injective` — the *flatness core* in the only publicly-accessible form:
26	  for an injective `R`-module map `f : M ⟶ N`, the localised stalk map
27	  `M_𝔭 →ₗ[R] N_𝔭` (built from Mathlib's `IsLocalizedModule (tilde.toStalk · x)` instances) is
28	  injective.  This is the algebraic content that "localisation `R → R_𝔭` is flat" contributes
29	  to mono-preservation.
30	
31	## What is delivered (axiom-clean), continued
32	
33	* `tilde_stalkFunctor_map_toStalk` — the **germ-naturality** transport identity (the crux flagged
34	  by the planner).  For `f : M ⟶ N` and a point `x ↔ 𝔭`, the `Ab`-valued stalk map of `~f`
35	  (computed through the faithful, limit-preserving `Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf`) intertwines the two
36	  localisation maps `tilde.toStalk`: `toStalk M x ≫ (Ab-stalk map) = f ≫ toStalk N x`.  This
37	  identifies the otherwise opaque `Ab`-germ-induced stalk map with the localised module map of `f`
38	  on the image of `toStalk`.  Proven on the public `Ab`-stalk path (`stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply` +
39	  the `⊤`-section naturality `StructureSheaf.comapₗ_const`), avoiding the module-private handles.
40	* `tildePreservesFiniteLimits_of_toPresheaf` — the **categorical reduction**: since
41	  `Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf` reflects finite limits (faithful + preserves limits + reflects isos,
42	  and `(Spec R).Modules` has finite limits), `~` preserves finite limits as soon as the composite
43	  `~ ⋙ toPresheaf` does.  This is `Limits.preservesFiniteLimits_of_reflects_of_preserves`.
44	
45	## Why `tildePreservesFiniteLimits` itself is NOT closed here
46	
47	The mathematical route (blueprint `lem:tilde_preserves_kernels`) is stalkwise flatness.  The
48	**categorical-glue obstruction is FALSE** — `tildePreservesFiniteLimits_of_toPresheaf` (above)
49	discharges it via `preservesFiniteLimits_of_reflects_of_preserves`; no "right-exact + mono ⟹
50	left-exact" lemma is needed.  The single remaining gap is the **stalkwise localisation step**:
51	showing the composite `~ ⋙ Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf` preserves finite limits.  Stalkwise this is
52	exactly localisation-is-flat:
53	* The `Ab`-germ-induced stalk map of `~f` is identified on the image of `toStalk` with the localised
54	  map of `f` by `tilde_stalkFunctor_map_toStalk`; and the localised map is injective
55	  (`tilde_toStalk_map_injective`).
56	* What still has to be built (~100–150 LOC): upgrade this to a full stalkwise *isomorphism* of the
57	  kernel-comparison map.  Concretely, (a) prove the `Ab` stalk map is `R`-linear (via `germₗ` +
58	  `R`-linearity of `Scheme.Modules.Hom.app`) so it equals `IsLocalizedModule.map …`, then (b)
59	  feed `∀ x, PreservesFiniteLimits (~ ⋙ toPresheaf ⋙ stalkFunctor x)` through a jointly-reflecting
60	  stalk family (`CategoryTheory.JointlyReflectIsomorphisms.jointlyReflectsLimit`) to obtain
61	  `PreservesFiniteLimits (~ ⋙ toPresheaf)`, then `tildePreservesFiniteLimits_of_toPresheaf`.
62	  The `ModuleCat R`-valued stalk path is dead (Mathlib privacy of `toStalkₗ'`, `stalkIsoₗ`,
63	  `stalkToLocalizationₗ`, `structurePresheafInModuleCat`); use the public `Ab` path throughout.
64	
65	See `task_results/AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.TildeExactness.md` for the precise next steps.

74	variable {R : CommRingCat.{u}}

76	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — exactness of the tilde functor -/
77	
78	/-- **Right-exactness of `~`.**  The tilde functor `~ : ModuleCat R ⥤ (Spec R).Modules`
79	preserves finite colimits: it is a left adjoint (`AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.adjunction`), so it
80	preserves all colimits.  Project-local because the packaged statement is what the
81	kernel/cokernel quasi-coherence argument (Stacks `lemma-kernel-cokernel-quasi-coherent`)
82	consumes alongside the (still open) finite-limit half. -/
83	theorem tilde_preservesFiniteColimits :
84	    Limits.PreservesFiniteColimits (tilde.functor R) := inferInstance
85	
86	/-- **Flatness core of mono-preservation for `~`.**  For an injective `R`-module map
87	`f : M ⟶ N` and a point `x ↔ 𝔭` of `Spec R`, the induced localised map on stalks
88	`M_𝔭 →ₗ[R] N_𝔭` — assembled from Mathlib's `IsLocalizedModule (tilde.toStalk · x).hom`
89	instances via `IsLocalizedModule.map` — is injective.  This is exactly the contribution of
90	"localisation `R → R_𝔭` is flat" to the statement that `~` preserves monomorphisms (Stacks
91	01HV, exactness of `~`).  Stated with the publicly-accessible stalk-localisation map
92	`AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toStalk`, the only such handle exported by Mathlib. -/
93	theorem tilde_toStalk_map_injective {M N : ModuleCat R} (f : M ⟶ N)
94	    (hf : Function.Injective f.hom) (x : PrimeSpectrum.Top R) :
95	    Function.Injective (IsLocalizedModule.map x.asIdeal.primeCompl
96	      (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toStalk M x).hom (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toStalk N x).hom f.hom) :=
97	  IsLocalizedModule.map_injective _ _ _ _ hf
98	
99	/-- **Reduction of the named target.**  `tildePreservesFiniteLimits` follows once `~` is shown to
100	preserve every kernel `parallelPair f 0`; all the ambient typeclass hypotheses of
101	`Functor.preservesFiniteLimits_of_preservesKernels` are already discharged for
102	`tilde.functor R` (it is additive, `ModuleCat R` and `(Spec R).Modules` have the requisite
103	finite (co)products / zero objects).  Recorded project-locally so the remaining obligation is a
104	single, sharply-stated hypothesis for the continuation lane. -/
105	theorem tilde_preservesFiniteLimits_of_preservesKernels
106	    (H : ∀ {M N : ModuleCat R} (f : M ⟶ N),
107	      PreservesLimit (parallelPair f 0) (tilde.functor R)) :
108	    PreservesFiniteLimits (tilde.functor R) :=
109	  Functor.preservesFiniteLimits_of_preservesKernels _
110	
111	/-- **Germ-naturality of the localisation map `toStalk`.**  For an `R`-module map
112	`f : M ⟶ N` and a point `x ↔ 𝔭` of `Spec R`, the `Ab`-valued stalk map of `~f` (computed via the
113	faithful, limit-preserving forgetful functor `Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf`) intertwines the two
114	localisation maps `tilde.toStalk`: precomposing with `toStalk M x` and postcomposing `f` agree.
115	
116	This is the load-bearing transport identity for mono/kernel preservation of `~`: it identifies the
117	otherwise opaque `Ab`-germ-induced stalk map with the localised module map of `f`.  Project-local
118	because Mathlib only exports the localisation handles `tilde.toStalk` and `toOpenₗ` and the section
119	naturality `comapₗ_const`; the germ-level statement is assembled here. -/
120	theorem tilde_stalkFunctor_map_toStalk {M N : ModuleCat R} (f : M ⟶ N)
121	    (x : PrimeSpectrum.Top R) (m : M) :
122	    (TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor _ x).map
123	        ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf (Spec (.of R))).map (tilde.map f))
124	        ((tilde.toStalk M x).hom m)
125	      = (tilde.toStalk N x).hom (f.hom m) := by
126	  change (TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor _ x).map
127	        ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf (Spec (.of R))).map (tilde.map f))
128	        (TopCat.Presheaf.germ (AlgebraicGeometry.moduleStructurePresheaf R M).presheaf ⊤ x
129	          (by trivial) (StructureSheaf.toOpenₗ R M ⊤ m))
130	      = TopCat.Presheaf.germ (AlgebraicGeometry.moduleStructurePresheaf R N).presheaf ⊤ x
131	          (by trivial) (StructureSheaf.toOpenₗ R N ⊤ (f.hom m))
132	  erw [TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply ⊤ x True.intro
133	    ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf (Spec (.of R))).map (tilde.map f)) (StructureSheaf.toOpenₗ R M ⊤ m)]
134	  congr 1
135	  simp only [Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf_map, Scheme.Modules.mapPresheaf_app,
136	    Scheme.Modules.Hom.app]
137	  rw [StructureSheaf.toOpenₗ_eq_const, StructureSheaf.toOpenₗ_eq_const]
138	  simp only [AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.map, AlgebraicGeometry.SpecModulesToSheafFullyFaithful,
139	    CategoryTheory.NatTrans.comp_app, AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.modulesSpecToSheafIso,
140	    ModuleCat.hom_comp]
141	  erw [StructureSheaf.comapₗ_const (hb := le_of_eq PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_one.symm)]
142	  rfl
143	
144	/-- **Reduction of `tildePreservesFiniteLimits` to the presheaf level.**  The forgetful functor
145	`Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf` from `𝒪_{Spec R}`-modules to presheaves of abelian groups is faithful,
146	preserves limits, and reflects isomorphisms; hence (since `(Spec R).Modules` has finite limits) it
147	reflects finite limits.  Therefore, to show `~` preserves finite limits it suffices to show the
148	composite `~ ⋙ toPresheaf` does.  This isolates the remaining obligation of
149	`lem:tilde_preserves_kernels` to a statement about the abelian-presheaf-valued composite, whose
150	stalks are computed by `tilde_stalkFunctor_map_toStalk`.  Project-local categorical glue (it refutes
151	the earlier-feared "no right-exact + mono ⟹ left-exact" obstruction: the reduction is purely
152	`preservesFiniteLimits_of_reflects_of_preserves`). -/
153	theorem tildePreservesFiniteLimits_of_toPresheaf
154	    (H : PreservesFiniteLimits
155	      (tilde.functor R ⋙ Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf (Spec (.of R)))) :
156	    PreservesFiniteLimits (tilde.functor R) :=
157	  haveI := H
158	  Limits.preservesFiniteLimits_of_reflects_of_preserves (tilde.functor R)
159	    (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf (Spec (.of R)))
160	
161	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — R-linear packaging of the Ab-stalk map -/
162	
163	/-- **Germ–scalar compatibility (`R`-linearity of the germ map).**  For `s` a section of `M^~`
164	over `U` and `r : R`, the germ at `x` of `(algebraMap R Γ(O_X,U) r) • s` equals `r •` the germ of
165	`s` (the `R`-action on the stalk being the localisation one through `tilde.toStalk R x`).  This is
166	the section-level half of "the `Ab`-stalk map of `~f` is `R`-linear".  Project-local because the
167	linear germ `StructureSheaf.germₗ` is not exported (no `public`); it is rebuilt here from the public
168	`PresheafOfModules.germ_smul` and `StructureSheaf.algebraMap_germ_apply`. -/
169	theorem tilde_germ_algebraMap_smul {M : ModuleCat R} (U : (Spec (.of R)).Opens)
170	    (x : PrimeSpectrum.Top R) (hxU : x ∈ U) (r : R) (s : Γ(AlgebraicGeometry.tilde M, U)) :
171	    (ConcreteCategory.hom
172	        ((AlgebraicGeometry.tilde M).presheaf.germ U x hxU))
173	        ((algebraMap R Γ(Spec (.of R), U) r) • s)
174	      = r • (ConcreteCategory.hom
175	        ((AlgebraicGeometry.tilde M).presheaf.germ U x hxU)) s := by
176	  erw [PresheafOfModules.germ_smul, StructureSheaf.algebraMap_germ_apply]
177	  rfl
178	
179	/-- **Sub-step (A): the `Ab`-stalk map `σ_x` is `R`-linear.**  The stalk-functor image
180	`σ_x := (stalkFunctor Ab x).map (toPresheaf.map (~f))` is a priori only an `Ab`-morphism between
181	the stalks; this packages it as the genuine `R`-linear map `M_𝔭 →ₗ[R] N_𝔭`.  Project-local: the
182	`ModuleCat R`-valued stalk functor for `SheafOfModules` is not exported (its building blocks
183	`stalkIsoₗ`/`toStalkₗ'` are private), so the linear structure is reconstructed here on the public
184	`Ab` stalk via germ-linearity. -/
185	noncomputable def stalkMapₗ {M N : ModuleCat R} (f : M ⟶ N) (x : PrimeSpectrum.Top R) :
186	    (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde M).presheaf.stalk x →ₗ[R]
187	      (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde N).presheaf.stalk x where
188	  toFun := (TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor _ x).map
189	    ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf (Spec (.of R))).map (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.map f))
190	  map_add' a b := map_add _ a b
191	  map_smul' r ζ := by
192	    dsimp only [RingHom.id_apply]
193	    obtain ⟨U, hxU, s, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde M).presheaf x ζ
194	    rw [← tilde_germ_algebraMap_smul U x hxU r s]
195	    erw [TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply U x hxU
196	        ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf (Spec (.of R))).map (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.map f)),
197	      TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply U x hxU
198	        ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf (Spec (.of R))).map (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.map f))]
199	    rw [Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf_map, Scheme.Modules.mapPresheaf_app]
200	    simp only [Opposite.unop_op]
201	    erw [Scheme.Modules.Hom.app_smul, tilde_germ_algebraMap_smul U x hxU r]
202	    rfl
203	
204	/-- **Identification of `σ_x` with the localised module map.**  The `R`-linear `Ab`-stalk map
205	`stalkMapₗ f x` is exactly the localisation `M_𝔭 →ₗ[R] N_𝔭` of `f`, i.e.
206	`IsLocalizedModule.map _ (toStalk M x) (toStalk N x) f`.  Both are `R`-linear and agree on the image
207	of the localisation map `tilde.toStalk M x` (by `tilde_stalkFunctor_map_toStalk`), so they coincide
208	by `IsLocalizedModule.ext`.  Project-local: it packages the otherwise opaque germ-induced stalk map
209	as the concrete localised map, whose flatness-injectivity is `tilde_toStalk_map_injective`. -/
210	theorem stalkMapₗ_eq {M N : ModuleCat R} (f : M ⟶ N) (x : PrimeSpectrum.Top R) :
211	    stalkMapₗ f x = IsLocalizedModule.map x.asIdeal.primeCompl
212	      (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toStalk M x).hom (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toStalk N x).hom
213	      f.hom := by
214	  apply IsLocalizedModule.ext x.asIdeal.primeCompl (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toStalk M x).hom
215	    (fun s => IsLocalizedModule.map_units (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toStalk N x).hom s)
216	  ext m
217	  change stalkMapₗ f x ((AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toStalk M x).hom m) = _
218	  rw [LinearMap.comp_apply, IsLocalizedModule.map_apply]
219	  exact tilde_stalkFunctor_map_toStalk f x m
220	
221	/-- **Stalkwise injectivity of `~f` for a monomorphism `f`.**  For an injective `R`-module map
222	`f`, the `R`-linear `Ab`-stalk map `σ_x = stalkMapₗ f x` of `~f` is injective at every point `x`.
223	This is the stalkwise-flatness contribution to mono-preservation of `~`, now stated on the genuine
224	linear stalk map: it combines the identification `stalkMapₗ_eq` with the localisation injectivity
225	`tilde_toStalk_map_injective`.  Project-local stepping stone toward `tildePreservesFiniteLimits`. -/
226	theorem stalkMapₗ_injective {M N : ModuleCat R} (f : M ⟶ N) (hf : Function.Injective f.hom)
227	    (x : PrimeSpectrum.Top R) : Function.Injective (stalkMapₗ f x) := by
228	  rw [stalkMapₗ_eq]
229	  exact tilde_toStalk_map_injective f hf x
230	
231	end AlgebraicGeometry

1756	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) :

1759	          (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpenFam U σ))) ≅
1760	      (∐ fun s : {σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι // V ≤ coverInterOpenFam U σ} =>
1761	        (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
1762	          (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpenFam U s.1))) where
1763	  hom := Limits.Sigma.desc fun σ => by
1764	    classical
1765	    exact if h : V ≤ coverInterOpenFam U σ then
1766	      Limits.Sigma.ι (fun s : {σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι // V ≤ coverInterOpenFam U σ} =>

1768	          (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpenFam U s.1))) ⟨σ, h⟩

1811	/-- The engine differential `C_{p+1} ⟶ C_p`: the alternating sum over `i : Fin (p+2)` of the

1832	`(i, j) ↦ (i.succAbove j, j.predAbove i)` as `FreeCechEngine.combDifferential_comp`, transported to
1833	the coproduct injections. -/
1834	lemma cechEngineD_compFam (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) :
1835	    cechEngineDFam U V (p + 1) ≫ cechEngineDFam U V p = 0 := by
1836	  apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro σ
1837	  rw [Limits.comp_zero, ← Category.assoc, cechEngineD_ιFam]
1838	  rw [Preadditive.sum_comp]
1839	  simp only [Preadditive.zsmul_comp, cechEngineD_ιFam, Finset.smul_sum, smul_smul]
1840	  rw [← Fintype.sum_prod_type (f := fun q : Fin (p + 3) × Fin (p + 2) =>
1841	    ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (q.1 : ℕ) * (-1) ^ (q.2 : ℕ)) •
1842	      Limits.Sigma.ι (fun _ : Fin (p + 1) → {i : ι // V ≤ U i} =>
1843	        coverSectionModule V) ((σ ∘ q.1.succAbove) ∘ q.2.succAbove))]
1844	  apply Finset.sum_involution (fun q _ => (q.1.succAbove q.2, q.2.predAbove q.1))
1845	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
1846	    have harg : (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) ∘ (i.predAbove j).succAbove
1847	        = (σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove := by
1848	      funext k
1849	      simp only [Function.comp_apply]
1850	      rw [Fin.succAbove_succAbove_succAbove_predAbove]
1851	    simp only [harg]
1852	    rw [← add_smul, FreeCechEngine.combSign_flip j i]
1853	    simp
1854	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _ _
1855	    simp only [ne_eq, Prod.mk.injEq, not_and]
1856	    intro hj
1857	    exact absurd hj (Fin.succAbove_ne j i)
1858	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
1859	    simp only [Prod.mk.injEq]
1860	    exact ⟨Fin.succAbove_succAbove_predAbove j i, Fin.predAbove_predAbove_succAbove j i⟩
1861	  · intro a _; exact Finset.mem_univ _

1995	      ≫ AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD (cechFreeSimplicialFam U) p
